title: Urgent: Latency regression after rw-open-3b-v2 rollout — rollback escalation
body:
Sam Carter <sam.carter@fintechco.com>: Priya —

We started seeing a sharp increase in inference tail latency immediately after the model version switchover last night (rw-open-3b -> rw-open-3b-v2). Over the last 45 minutes our p99 has jumped from ~220ms to ~980ms and we’re seeing sporadic 5xx responses from your hosted endpoint. This is impacting our quote generation flow.

Quick context:
- Endpoint: prod-fintech-infer-01 (us-west)
- Approx. request vo...

Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>: Sam —
Acknowledged, and sorry for the disruption. Looping in Karthik and Lauren to triage immediately.

Immediate asks:
- Karthik: can you confirm whether the rollout to rw-open-3b-v2 matches our kernel selection matrix for prod-fintech-infer-01 and whether we’re seeing KV cache miss amplification?
- Lauren / Markus: we may need an expedited rollback and an SLA exception; please advise o...

normalized.subject: Urgent: Latency regression after rw-open-3b-v2 rollout — rollback escalation

messages.subject: Sudden p99 latency spike after model update (rw-open-3b-v2)
messages.body:
Priya —

We started seeing a sharp increase in inference tail latency immediately after the model version switchover last night (rw-open-3b -> rw-open-3b-v2). Over the last 45 minutes our p99 has jumped from ~220ms to ~980ms and we’re seeing sporadic 5xx responses from your hosted endpoint. This is impacting our quote generation flow.

Quick context:
- Endpoint: prod-fintech-infer-01 (us-west)
- Approx. request volume: 250 qps (steady)
- Degradation started ~2026-03-09T09:56:00Z (we cross-checked client timestamps)

I attached a small sample of the slow traces (latency_spike_sample.csv). Can you triage? If this is a regression tied to the model version, we need rollback or mitigation ASAP...

to: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Sudden p99 latency spike after model update (rw-open-3b-v2)
messages.body:
Sam —
Acknowledged, and sorry for the disruption. Looping in Karthik and Lauren to triage immediately.

Immediate asks:
- Karthik: can you confirm whether the rollout to rw-open-3b-v2 matches our kernel selection matrix for prod-fintech-infer-01 and whether we’re seeing KV cache miss amplification?
- Lauren / Markus: we may need an expedited rollback and an SLA exception; please advise on approval and customer communications cadence.

We received the sample — thanks. I’ll keep you updated every 15 minutes until we have a mitigation plan.

— Priya
Priya Natarajan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.ai
Tel: +1 415-555-0124

---

to: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Sudden p99 latency spike after model update (rw-open-3b-v2)
messages.body:
Thanks Priya / Sam — I pulled the traces and started tailing the serving logs for prod-fintech-infer-01. Preliminary notes:

- We are seeing a pattern consistent with KV cache churn — rw-open-3b-v2 uses a larger key distribution (different tokenizer alignment) that increases KV writes for these request shapes.
- p50 remains ~180-250ms; p95 ~320-450ms; p99 legs spike and sometimes show 1.2–1.5s at peak.
- Error rate correlates with Gemma worker OOM retries on node gpu-3 (we’re seeing retries and backpressure).

Immediate mitigation options (ranked):
1) Roll back the routing for prod-fintech-infer-01 to rw-open-3b (v1) — fast and low-risk.
2) Temporarily restrict sequence length/auto-batchi...

to: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Sudden p99 latency spike after model update (rw-open-3b-v2)
messages.body:
Approved: manual rollback to rw-open-3b (v1) for prod-fintech-infer-01.

Notes / decisions:
- Approve rollback now. Treat this as a regression incident and open a postmortem.
- Agree with the rollback trigger thresholds Karthik suggested; we’ll formalize them in the SLA playbook.
- Priya: please notify FinTechCo with planned ETA and confirm whether they want a credit per SLA (we should flag for finance).
- Lauren: please coordinate CS/comms and estimate potential SLA credit.

I’m copying Sam so they’re aware of the immediate action.

— Markus Klein
VP, Customer Reliability
markus.klein@redwood.ai

---

cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Sudden p99 latency spike after model update (rw-open-3b-v2)
messages.body:
Sam —
We’re initiating a manual rollback to the previous model (rw-open-3b v1) for your prod-fintech-infer-01 endpoint now. Karthik will perform the rollback; ETA is ~25-35 minutes.

Actions for you:
- We’ll monitor p99 and error rate and confirm when metrics return to baseline.
- If you prefer, we can schedule a short call to walk through the change window; otherwise we’ll update over email.

Regarding SLA: we will treat this as a regression and open an SLA exception request; I’ll follow up separately with the proposed credit per our SLA terms.

Thanks for the quick signal — appreciate the sample you sent.
— Priya

---

to: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Sudden p99 latency spike after model update (rw-open-3b-v2)
messages.body:
On Mon, Mar 9, 2026 at 10:45 AM Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Sam —
> We’re initiating a manual rollback to the previous model (rw-open-3b v1) for your prod-fintech-infer-01 endpoint now. Karthik will perform the rollback; ETA is ~25-35 minutes.

Rollback completed at 11:08 PDT. Post-rollback telemetry:
- p50: 190ms (baseline)
- p95: 340ms
- p99: 260–310ms (returned to expected range)
- 5xx errors: dropped to baseline <0.1%

I’ve reopened the SUP ticket (SUP-1839) and attached the trace bundle for the postmortem. Recommend a rolling redeploy plan and additional guard rails in the release pipeline:
- Add model-version canary with synthetic traffic for 15–30 minutes ...

messages.body:
Mateo,

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the workbook. A few clarifications to help you tune the recommendations:
- Primary workload: customer-facing chat and summarization pipelines (Llama-3 family, 70B-ish), occasional reranking jobs (small models).
- Expected steady-state: ~2,500 inference requests per minute during business hours (08:00-18:00 PT).
- P50 latency target: <=120ms. P95: aim for <=400ms. Tail bursts up to 4x for marketing campaigns.
- Deployment requirement: private VPC with customer-managed keys (HSM).
- Budget guidance: looking for committed discount tiers — our procurement team prefers to see 12-month reservation pricing vs conversion to on-demand.

- Lower-cost option (if budget constrained): 10x RTX6000-equivalent ...

They need a small, dedicated transient slice of GPUs (estimated 4 x A100 equivalent vGPUs) for a 36-hour window to run throughput/latency comparisons against our hosting configurations.

Why this matters: these sweeps are sensitive to noisy neighbors and we expect tight 95th percentil...

2) Network/Egress: will Acme need elevated egress bandwidth or large artifact pulls from S3? If so, we should pre-warm artifacts to avoid bur...

messages.subject: Request: short-lived GPU slice for latency-sensitive model evals
messages.body:
Hi team,

Quick ask from Product/Research: Acme AI (external partner) is running a focused latency-sensitive model sweep next week. They need a small, dedicated transient slice of GPUs (estimated 4 x A100 equivalent vGPUs) for a 36-hour window to run throughput/latency comparisons against our hosting configurations.

Why this matters: these sweeps are sensitive to noisy neighbors and we expect tight 95th percentile latency variation. If we can provide an isolated slice for the evaluation window, we can (a) validate our autoscale/latency SLOs for managed Dedicated customers, and (b) capture per-token cost delta for that workload.

Ask: can we provision a temporary reserved slice (non-persi...

cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Request: short-lived GPU slice for latency-sensitive model evals
messages.body:
Jonas —

Thanks for the heads-up. A few quick clarifying Qs so we can size the exception correctly:

1) Exact GPU shape/runtime: when you say \"4 x A100 equivalent,\" do you mean 4 full cards or 4 fractional slices (e.g., 4 vGPU slices that sum to 1x full card)?
2) Network/Egress: will Acme need elevated egress bandwidth or large artifact pulls from S3? If so, we should pre-warm artifacts to avoid bursty network noise.
3) Fallback behavior: you mentioned preemption threshold at 5% — confirm whether you'd accept a last-resort fallback to a lower-precision quantized variant (same model family) if capacity drops below 2%. That keeps the test running but changes the latency profile.

I'll run...

cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Request: short-lived GPU slice for latency-sensitive model evals
messages.body:
Kim/Jonas —

We can do this with an \"ephemeral carve\" flow in the allocator, but there are two operational notes:

- Allocation semantics: we can allocate a 4-slice logical pool (equivalent to 4 full A100s) that is tenant-isolated for the 36-hour window. It will consume capacity from the Dedicated pool, and we will mark it as nondurable so it auto-deletes after teardown.
- Autoscale preemption exception: allocator can mark the slice as soft-protected; it will only preempt if global free capacity < 5% (we can change threshold). We will NOT support >2% fallback to quantized variant automatically — that requires an app-level switch. Ops recommends we coordinate with Product to include a fa...

messages.body:

Confirming acceptance criteria for Acme: capture 95th/99th token latencies, kernel-selection variance, and per-token cost delta between our default hosted config and the dedicated slice. Start window confirmed: 2026-04-28 14:00 UTC; teardown: 2026-04-30 02:00 UTC (36 hours).

Action items assigned:
- Jonas: create JIRA INFRA-3421 and attach the runbook + spreadsheet; request ops carve.
- Marcus: set up the ephemeral carve with soft-protect threshold at 5% and enable token-level latency sampling. Pre-warm artifacts 1 hour prior.
- Kimberly: approve the temporary budget for the incremental ~USD 1,200 (or flag if this needs leadership sign-of...

messages.subject: Quick recap + proposed POC charter (multilingual support & model fallback)
messages.body:
Hi Priya / Aaron —

Thanks again for taking the time today. Quick recap from our discovery and a proposed starter charter for the POC so we can move to scheduling and scoping. I summarized the high-level asks below and put owners against each deliverable.

What you told us (priority):
- Customer-facing summarization across product pages (EN, ES, PT-BR, FR) is the highest priority.
- Strong quality on domain-specific terminology (retail SKUs, promotions) is required.
- Latency expectations: <300ms p95 for single-item queries, faster for small summaries.
- Migrations: the solution must fall back to a smaller, cheaper model in the event of capacity/latency pressure.
- Security: no persistent...

messages.subject: Re: Quick recap + proposed POC charter (multilingual support & model fallback)
messages.body:
Thanks Lauren — this is helpful. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Dataset: we can deliver the 250 examples per language by Jan 22 EOD. We'll include canonical product descriptions and a set of customer query logs (sanitized).
2) Success metrics: the accuracy/relevance threshold of 85% is fine; would like to add a business SLA around hallucination tolerance — max 2% critical hallucinations (claims about pricing/availability that are verifiably false).
3) Latency: 300ms p95 is aggressive for multi-region traffic; can we define that as a US-only target for the POC and then measure EU separately?
4) Fallback behavior: prefer that fallback responses indicate degraded mode to the custome...

messages.subject: Re: Quick recap + proposed POC charter (multilingual support & model fallback)
messages.body:
Great points, Priya — thanks for the quick turnaround. Responses inline: 

1) Dataset: Jan 22 works. We'll confirm receipt and a quick sampling sanity check the same day.
2) Hallucination SLA: adding a 2% critical-hallucination cap is reasonable. We'll include a manual adjudication step in the eval checklist and add that to the success criteria.
3) Latency: we can scope the p95 <300ms to US traffic for the POC and record EU metrics separately. We'll include regional traces in the console.
4) Fallback behavior: agreed — fallback responses will include a short flag line ("Note: limited mode — results may be abbreviated") and we will preserve guardrail layers. To save cost, the fallback mode...

messages.subject: Re: Quick recap + proposed POC charter (multilingual support & model fallback)
messages.body:
Hi all — quick technical note following Lauren's ask. Short answers first: yes, we can preserve guardrails and most retrieval flows on the Mistral-7B fallback, but there are trade-offs.

Details:
- Safety/filters: yes, same filter chain can run pre- and post-model. No functional regression expected.
- Retrieval: we can keep retrieval, but to hit target latency on the fallback we recommend: 1) fewer retrieved passages (top-3 instead of top-10), 2) smaller embedding model for nearest-neighbor lookups in fallback mode. We'll quantify the delta in the Feb 2 review.
- Prompting: fallback will use a simplified system prompt to reduce token count; expect a 3-7 point drop in human-rated relevance...

messages.subject: Observability mosaic: pricing structure & tradeoffs for retention, tracing, dashboards
messages.body:
Elliot —

Great to meet you on the call earlier. Per our conversation, CloudMosaic is evaluating a single vendor for model inference + ops visibility and asked us to scope the add-on pricing for:
- long-term log/trace retention (30/90/365 day buckets)
- full-trace vs sampled tracing costs
- advanced dashboards (custom visual widgets, replays, saved queries)

Attached are two artifacts to start: a short SOW draft (high level scope) and a simple retention cost model (workbook) showing slab vs metered scenarios. A few quick framing notes so we can align on the right packaging: 

1) Slab (flat retention buckets): predictable, easier to budget at scale; better for customers who keep long-tail ...

messages.subject: Re: Observability mosaic: pricing structure & tradeoffs for retention, tracing, dashboards
messages.body:
Ben —

Thanks for the materials and the clear options. A few follow-ups from our side (inline):

> Can you confirm CloudMosaic's current 99th percentile trace volume (avg spans/s)?

We currently average ~1,200 spans/s at peak traffic, with bursts to ~4k for short windows (CI traffic, deployment spikes).

> Do you need full raw-trace replay for compliance?

We have two teams: Platform (needs raw replays for debugging) and Security/Compliance (needs sampled full-fidelity for incident investigations). For now, we’d like the option to retain full-trace for 30 days and sampled tails for 365 days.

We also have procurement questions:
- Can you provide a simple one-pager that maps the slab vs me...

messages.subject: Re: Observability mosaic: pricing structure & tradeoffs for retention, tracing, dashboards
messages.body:
Elliot — thanks for the details, that helps a lot. Quick answers and a pragmatic recommendation inline.

1) At 1.2k spans/s sustained (4k peaks): the retention_cost_model.xlsx shows approximate monthly delta:
- 30-day slab: $6.5k/mo
- 90-day slab: $14k/mo
- 365-day slab: $45k/mo
Metered (ingest+store): about 10–18% higher at current volumes assuming no aggressive sampling.

2) Sampling recommendation: start with 80/20 head-sample (capture top 80% of traces fully, tail-sample 20% for anomalies) — that often captures 90% of debugging value while cutting trace cost ~40%. We can tune per-service.

3) Discount question: the 15% on a 12-month commit applies to add-ons only in our standard offer...

messages.subject: Internal: CloudMosaic concession ask — onboarding credit approval
messages.body:
Quick internal note — CloudMosaic is leaning to the 12-month add-on commit but procurement asked for a $10k onboarding credit and dashboard concurrency waiver for the pilot. I proposed the $10k onboarding credit (one-time) and to keep dashboard concurrency in the standard seat model.

Can you approve the one-time $10k credit and an exception to waive the setup fee? If we want to push for the full-bundle discount (extend 15% to inference), we should ask for a minimum ARR commit of $550k to justify.

Why it’s OK: CloudMosaic is a solid reference candidate, high visibility in our target vertical, and they show an appetite for scaling to enterprise terms in year two.

If no objections I’ll ac...

messages.subject: Re: Internal: CloudMosaic concession ask — onboarding credit approval
messages.body:
Ben —

Approve the $10k onboarding credit and waive the setup fee for the pilot. We should reserve the right to revisit dashboard concurrency pricing on renewal. If they ask to extend the 15% to inference, push for the $550k ARR or else we stick to add-ons-only discount.

Cc’ing Marissa for SOW finalization.

—Jordan

Hi Marcus,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the latency improvement in the EU region looks promising. We are preparing a short pilot for our EU customers and have a few contractual/transfer asks before legal can greenlight the PoC. High-level:

- CardioHealth will act as controller for ICD-coded telemetry from EU patients; we need assurances about where processing occurs and a short-term carveout while our own legal team finalizes the UK Addendum and local data transfer assessment.
- Specifically we need:
 * Confirmation you can support SCCs for transfers from the EU to the US for the pilot, plus a temporary Technical & Organizational Measures addendum (TIA/DTIA) that documents safe...

messages.subject: Re: Data routing carveout for CardioHealth pilot

Andrew — thanks for the materials and clear ask. Quick acknowledgement and a couple of clarifying Qs before we loop Legal & Privacy Ops: 

1) When you say 'EU to the US transfers' — is the primary flow EU customer telemetry -> Redwood-hosted US inference -> EU obs store (or do you require processing to remain in-region)?
2) For the pilot, is CardioHealth willing to accept a documented temporary mitigation package (SCCs + TIA) with a defined sunset (we typically request a 60-90 day window while addenda are signed)?

Prelim thinking from engineering: we can pin the model instances to EU regions for inference and enable cross-region fallback to US only as a documented exception; that approac...

messages.subject: Re: Data routing carveout for CardioHealth pilot
messages.body:
Marcus / Marissa — reviewed Andrew's packet. Summary of options and recommended approach below. I'm writing as Redwood legal for intake; operational/engineering dependencies noted.

Options (fastest to slowest):
A) SCCs + Temporary DTIA/TIA (recommended short-term path):
 - Execute EU SCCs (standard clauses) with CardioHealth for controller->processor flows.
 - Supplement with a DTIA/TIA that documents technical safeguards (EU region pinning, encryption at rest/in transit, KV cache TTLs, subprocessors list & attestations, audit logging retention).
 - Sunset: time-boxed exception (e.g., 60 days) with automatic re-evaluation and plan to sign UK Addendum within that window.

B) SCCs + UK IDT...

Hi team,

Following up from last week's intake call with Acme: they'd like a technical deep-dive focused on dedicated GPU capacity for their inference workloads. Key objectives they asked for: pool sizing guidance (steady state + burst), autoscaling policies with cost/latency tradeoffs, and isolation/tenancy options for multi-tenant pipelines.

Proposed attendees: Soojin (platform), Marissa (solutions), Karthik (runtime), Marcus (SRE), Mina (security review), and Sarah + two engineers from Acme.

Draft agenda attached (agenda + a lightweight pool-sizing template). Suggested windows (PST):
- Wed Jan 7, 10:00–11:30
- Thu Jan 8, 13:00–14:30
- Mon Jan 12, 09:00–10:30

Please confirm availabil...

messages.subject: Re: Dedicated GPU capacity deep dive — schedule, agenda, and timezone routing
messages.body:
Thanks, Sooj — the agenda looks great. Our engineers prefer Eastern time; Thu Jan 8 at 13:00 PST (which is 16:00 EST) would work for two of us, but our lead (Priya) is out that afternoon. If you can do Wed Jan 7 at 10:00 PST, that hits 13:00 EST and would be ideal.

We also want a short pre-read on the burst isolation model you mentioned (how we guarantee noisy-neighbor isolation for short high-priority jobs). The team will review the pool-sizing template and add our workload profiles.

Best,
Sarah
Sarah Lewis
Director, Platform Engineering | Acme AI

On Mon, Jan 5, 2026 at 09:22 Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.com> wrote:
> (see above)

to: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>, Sarah Lewis <sarah.lewis@acmeai.com>
messages.subject: Re: Dedicated GPU capacity deep dive — schedule, agenda, and timezone routing
messages.body:
Thanks, Sooj and Sarah. Wed Jan 7 10:00 PST works for Redwood. I suggest we keep it to 75 minutes: 45 minutes architecture + 30 minutes ops/playbook + 5 minutes buffer.

Specific requests from my side:
- Karthik, can you prepare two short slides on quantization/throughput tradeoffs and expected token/sec for the two model classes Sarah mentioned?
- Marcus, please surface our recommended autoscale knobs (min pool size, scale step, cooldown) and any recent failure modes we should call out.
- Mina, would you be able to join for the 15-minute isolation deep-dive portion? If you can only spare 15 minutes, we'll schedule that at the start.

I've tentatively booked the slot and attached a calend...

to: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>, Sarah Lewis <sarah.lewis@acmeai.com>
messages.subject: Re: Dedicated GPU capacity deep dive — schedule, agenda, and timezone routing
messages.body:
Quick update before the call — I uploaded a one-page throughput estimate into the shared Drive link (see related links). Key assumptions: avg seq length 128, 8 concurrent workers per container, 16-bit quantized variant. If Acme's profiles deviate (longer seqs or synthetic batching), numbers will change by ~25–40%. I'll keep the slides tight.

On isolation: we should prepare 2 short patterns — strict tenancy (dedicated pools per customer) vs. soft isolation (priority queues + burst reservations). Each has different cost implications; I'll call out rough monthly delta.

-Karthik

to: Sarah Lewis <sarah.lewis@acmeai.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Dedicated GPU capacity deep dive — schedule, agenda, and timezone routing
messages.body:
Confirmed: Wed Jan 7 10:00–11:15 PST (75 minutes) — we will cover architecture, autoscale knobs, and a 15-minute isolation Q&A at the top. Marcus and Karthik will present the sizing slides; Mina will join for isolation.

Action items before the call:
- Acme: upload workload profiles into the shared drive (Sarah) — thanks for doing that.
- Redwood: Karthik to finalize throughput slide; Marcus to prepare autoscale runbook excerpt; Marissa will consolidate and send final 1-page pre-read.

I've attached the final agenda and re-shared the calendar invite. Please let me know if anything else should be added. Looking forward to it.

Sooj

title: MPA: acceptance lane decision playbook — SOC 2 / SIG / DPA questions
body:
Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@ledgerpath.com>: Hi Mina / Ben,

Thanks for the demo last week — LedgerPath is lining up an internal review for the POC and procurement team. Before we lock dates I need a few clarifications so we can feed them into our MAP and legal review: 

- SOC 2: can you confirm whether Redwood's current attestation covers the hosted API and Dedicated nodes (we will be testing hosted but evaluating dedicated for production)? Which trust serv...

Thanks Jordan — this looks good. We can provide the 100-sample data set by EOD Friday. Two small asks from our side before the meeting:

1) Can you call out what data we should sanitize vs what you need intact for representative cache/hit measurements?
2) Our SRE (James Park) will join; can you include an APAC-friendly time alternative?

Head of Product, Optimo AI

messages.subject: Re: Optimo onboarding — tuning plan & kickoff
messages.body:
James — quick notes on sanitization and what we need for accurate measurements:

- For caching: keep full prompt prefix/token boundaries but remove any PII fields (names, emails, account IDs). If you replace PII with consistent tokens (e.g., <USER_1>) that preserves hit behavior.
- For latency/cost sweep: we need per-request token counts (prompt + expected completion) and a binary flag for whether responses are streaming or full.
- For quantization analysis: sample model logits aren't necessary; just provide sample inputs and expected output lengths. We'll run model variants under the hood on our infra.

I've attached a small example of a kv cache distribution we like to use (kv_cache_exa...

messages.subject: Re: Optimo onboarding — tuning plan & kickoff
messages.body:
Adding some logistics and the proposed kickoff agenda:

Proposed kickoff agenda (60 min):
- 0-5 min: Introductions & goals (Jordan)
- 5-20 min: Optimo workload profile and constraints (James / Elena)
- 20-35 min: Redwood proposed sweep: batching, cache TTLs, quant profiles (Ava)
- 35-50 min: Routing policy discussion — latency vs cost tiers & fallback behaviors (Connor)
- 50-60 min: Action items & owners (all)

Deliverables within 7 business days post-kickoff:
- A: Tuning sweep report with 3 profiles and cost/latency matrix
- B: Recommended production config (batch sizes, cache TTLs, quant profile) and rollout plan (canary->full)
- C: Cost delta estimate (monthly) for chosen config vs bas...

messages.subject: Re: Optimo onboarding — tuning plan & kickoff
messages.body:
Thanks — we can take Tue 10am PT. A couple of clarifying constraints to surface ahead of time:

- Cost target: we want at least a 25% reduction in per-token spend vs our current hosted endpoint within the first 30 days post-rollout, without moving to a lower-quality model.
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile under 350ms for interactive paths; batch jobs can be up to 2s.
- Cache policy: no persistent storage of substrings of user PII; opt-in only. TTLs must be configurable per-customer segment.

One more question: can you share an example of tradeoffs between int8 vs q4_1 for our model family? We have a hard requirement to not regress on coherence for longer completions.

—Elena
Product Ops, Op...

cc: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, accounts@optimo.ai
messages.subject: Re: Optimo onboarding — tuning plan & kickoff
messages.body:
Elena / James — perfect, appreciate the clarity. Quick replies to your constraints/questions and the agreed plan: 

- Cost target: we'll prioritize the batch/quant sweep to surface the best config that meets your 25% cost reduction target. Historically for similar families, q4_1 with a 32-token batching sweet spot hit ~20-30% savings with <3% quality delta on long completions; int8 often gives more savings but sometimes requires a small temperature/decoding tweak to maintain coherence. We'll include the exact examples in the sweep report. See redwood-quant-matrix.csv for baseline reference.
- Latency SLO: we'll run 95th percentile checks across the sweep and include a recommended routing ...

messages.subject: Quick Q: confirm you "don't train on customer text" + retention nuance
messages.body:
Hi Jordan,

Thanks for walking us through the integration yesterday. Legal asked for a couple of clarifications before we move to procurement:

1) We need a plain-English confirmation that Redwood will not use Acme customer text to train or fine-tune any models (including internal research), unless Acme explicitly opts in.
2) Please explain what you mean by "ephemeral" caches vs "backups/snapshots" and the practical delete window for each. Our security team is concerned about debug snapshots and cold backups that might be stored outside the usual retention window.

If you have a one-page or diagram we can hand to Legal, that would be perfect.

Appreciate it,
Priya
Priya Sood
Senior Privac...

messages.subject: Re: Quick Q: confirm you "don't train on customer text" + retention nuance
messages.body:
Hi Priya — thanks for the clear asks. Short answers up front:

- We will not use Acme customer content to train, fine-tune, or update any production models or downstream model checkpoints unless Acme signs an explicit data-sharing opt-in. That includes both automated training pipelines and manual researcher experiments.

- For retention: runtime artifacts (request payloads, transient KV cache entries, and token-level inference traces used for routing/batching) are treated as ephemeral: they are held in memory or short-lived storage and are eligible for deletion within 0–30 days depending on config. Backups, snapshots, and debug archives are retained only per contract and are not used for ...

to: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@ben.carter>
messages.subject: Fwd: Quick Q: confirm you "don't train on customer text" + retention nuance
messages.body:
Team — forwarding Acme's asks below. They want: (A) a clear commitment not to train on customer text, and (B) explicit retention windows for ephemeral vs archival artifacts. Can you confirm the precise legal-safe phrasing we should provide?

Notes:
- They asked about debug snapshots and cold backups (where we sometimes persist compressed model-state snapshots for disaster recovery).
- They also want to understand whether any internal research uses redacted customer data without opt-in.

I attached the one-pager I just sent. If we need to add a sentence about irreversible tombstoning vs archival, let's propose wording.

Thanks — quick turnaround appreciated.
Jordan

---

to: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@ben.carter>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Quick Q: confirm you "don't train on customer text" + retention nuance
messages.body:
Jordan/Marissa/Ben — happy to take this. Draft language below is intentionally conservative for Legal:

1) Non-Training Assurance (proposed): "Redwood Inference will not use Customer Content to train, fine-tune, or improve any of Redwood's production models, research models, or internal model checkpoints, unless Customer provides an explicit, written opt-in that describes the scope and duration of the use. This commitment covers automated training pipelines, manual experiments, and any derivative model artifacts."

2) Retention and Deletion (proposed): "Runtime artifacts (including request payloads, ephemeral KV cache entries, and short-lived inference traces) are stored only for operatio...

messages.subject: Re: Quick Q: confirm you "don't train on customer text" + retention nuance
messages.body:
Hi Priya / Ian — Following up with the conservative language Rishi suggested. Summary for Legal: we will provide the full draft wording (below) in the contract addendum; we can shorten or tighten retention windows if Acme requires it as part of the deal.

Quoted draft for contract: 
"Redwood Inference will not use Customer Content to train, fine-tune, or improve any of Redwood's production or research models, or derive any new models from Customer Content, unless Customer provides an explicit, written opt-in that defines scope and duration. Runtime artifacts (request payloads, KV cache entries and inference traces) are eligible for deletion within 0–7 days by default; operational logs are...

messages.body:
Forwarding the plan to provide the technical checklist / quick notes. Attachment: redwood-network-reqs.pdf (network + KMS checklist).

High-level tech notes:
- We support VPC peering or a secure VPN tunnel. For the POC, VPC peering is preferred (lower latency).
- Required firewall: allow outbound to 52.23.11.0/24 on ports 443, 8443; ephemeral ports for batching.
- KMS: we support Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) via AWS KMS; audit logs are available and can be retained 90 days (extendable).
- Data: all persisted artifacts for the POC are encrypted at rest. We recommend a separate test dataset with PHI redacted; if you need a quick redaction script we can provide one.

For the other items (IR overview, encryption/KMS, audit logging/export + retention), can we point to customer-facing one-pagers + docs links instead of sending internal policy docs?

If there are any constraints (e.g., only via secure portal, wat...

messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Aurora Financial — URGENT SOC 2 Type II report request (under NDA) + artifact list
messages.body:
Confirmed:

1) SOC 2 Type II report: shareable under executed NDA only. We should route through the evidence request workflow (even if expedited) so we have an audit trail.

2) SLA language: please don’t “commit” to EOD Monday in writing unless we’re sure NDA will be executed today. We can say:
 - “Upon receipt of a fully executed NDA, we typically provide the SOC 2 report within 2 business days.”
 - “Given your timeline, we will prioritize this request and aim to turn it around as quickly as possible.”

3) “Management response / CAP”: if there are any noted exceptions in the report, the management response is included in the report package. We should not separately send internal remediat...

cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Aurora Financial — URGENT SOC 2 Type II report request (under NDA) + artifact list
messages.body:
+1 to Olga’s guidance.

Operationally:
- Latest SOC 2 Type II report package is ready in the restricted evidence repository.
- Delivery: we can provide via a time-bound secure link (preferred) once NDA is executed and the requester is verified.
- Watermarking: report is already watermarked; we should not remove.

If Aurora executes the mutual NDA today, we can likely deliver the SOC 2 report tomorrow (Sunday) since this is a blocker, but I’d rather keep the written promise at “2 business days” and then overdeliver.

For the rest of the artifact list, we can attach:
- IR overview (approved 1-pager)
- Encryption/KMS one-pager
- Audit logging overview (customer-facing) + export options docs
...

- We can provide our most recent SOC 2 Type II report under a fully executed NDA.

- For the additional items, we will provide customer-facing summaries/one-pagers for incident response overview, encryption/key management, and audit logging/export/retention.

Do NOT:
- promise EOD Monday explicitly

Daniel/Olga will coordinate fulfillment once NDA is signed.

-Aisha

messages.subject: DPA negotiation: data retention + backup/DR carve-outs for NovusHealth PoC
messages.body:
Hi Lucy / team,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — excited about the PoC next month. Legal on both sides asked me to kick off a short thread to align on a couple of DPA items before we sign, specifically retention, deletion, and a limited backup/DR carve-out we discussed.

Two quick calls-outs from our side:
- Retention: Novus requested 12 months for certain logs. We typically default to 90 days for production data but can support longer for logs/metrics with a narrow scope.
- Backup/DR carve-out: we need a small exception so that backups made for Disaster Recovery are retained per our DR schedule (90 days) and can be restored only for operational continuity; those backups will be ina...

messages.subject: Re: DPA negotiation: data retention + backup/DR carve-outs for NovusHealth PoC
messages.body:
Jordan — thanks for the note and the redline. Couple of quick clarifications from our side before we loop Legal formally:

1) Retention: For the PoC we can accept 90 days for customer data, but need 12 months for audit logs and system telemetry that will be used for compliance reporting.
2) Backup/DR carve-out: We are uncomfortable with a carve-out that allows restored backups to exist beyond customer access controls. Any restoration event should be logged and be subject to the same deletion/retention policies.
3) Subprocessors: please provide the latest subprocessor list and confirm whether any three-letter subcontractors are used for backups (e.g., storage providers, cross-region replic...

messages.subject: Fwd: DPA negotiation: data retention + backup/DR carve-outs for NovusHealth PoC
messages.body:
Karthik / Marissa — forwarding Novus’ quick reply. They want 12 months for audit logs and tighter controls around restores. They also asked for the full subprocessor list and whether any storage replication partners are used.

Can Legal propose final language that:
- Allows a narrowly scoped DR backup retention (90 days) specifically for operational recovery,
- Ensures any restore events are logged and subject to the same access controls and deletion on request,
- Confirms subprocessor transparency and a standard notification period for changes.

If we can land language that is acceptable to both, I’ll send the DocuSign to Victor/Lucy.

Thanks —
Jordan

---

messages.body:
Hi Lucy / Victor — appreciate the quick turnaround. I’ve reviewed Novus’ asks and propose the attached counter-redline (Redwood_DPA_response_v2.pdf). High-level summary of our position: 

1) Retention
- Customer content: Redwood will retain customer-provided content for 90 days post-termination/contract end for the PoC. We can make an exception for audit logs/telemetry up to 12 months, but only if Novus confirms a documented, business-justified need and accepts a scoped access/usage clause limited to compliance reporting.

2) Backup / DR carve-out
- Proposed carve-out language allows Redwood to retain disaster recovery backups for up to 90 days solely for operational recovery.
- Any resto...

messages.body:

1) Audit logs: Novus requires 12 months for audit logs as a non-negotiable compliance requirement for the PoC. We acknowledge your ask for a documented business justification — we will provide a short statement from our compliance team.
2) Restore access controls: Novus will accept restores limited to operational recovery if Redwood supplies a restore audit within 48 hours of any event and confirms that restored data will not be used for feature development or analytics.
3) Subprocessor notice: 30 days’ notice is acceptable. Please confirm whether any subcontractor has access to decrypted customer c...

messages.subject: RE: DPA negotiation: data retention + backup/DR carve-outs for NovusHealth PoC
messages.body:
Lucy / Victor — great — thanks for the alignment. Next steps and mutual action plan: 

1) Marissa will add the 12-month audit-log exception into section 4.2 with strict usage limits (compliance reporting only) and an obligation for Novus to provide the compliance justification document.
2) Marissa will also add the restore-audit commitment (Redwood provides restore logs within 48 hours and confirms no feature/analytics use).
3) Marissa will prepare the final DPA and upload to AdobeSign per Novus preference — Victor, please confirm the recipient email to use for the envelope staging.
4) Jordan will coordinate a short kickoff call for the PoC on 2026-07-15 and will not commence data transfe...

Director, ML Platforms — ACME.ai
priya@acme.ai

messages.subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning request - ACME Q3 commitments
messages.body:
Jordan / Karthik —

I'm attaching a short note (Q3-Capacity-Impact-Notes.txt) and a worked spreadsheet that shows three allocation policies:

1) Strict reservation-first: signed Reserved capacity > anything else. Preempt trials and bursts. (Pros: predictable billing, meets SLAs. Cons: temporary utilization dips if reserved pool underused.)

2) Revenue-weighted share: allocate by committed ARR + short-term expected usage. Useful when multiple signed deals but limited hardware. (Pros: balances business impact. Cons: complex arbitration, requires up-to-date ARR mapping.)

3) Soft-reservation with burst windows: reserve baseline for committed customers, allow short scheduled burst windows for...

messages.subject: Fwd: Dedicated capacity planning request - request for arbitration
messages.body:
Sean — forwarding the thread. TL;DR: ACME intends to convert to Reserved (75k token-hours/day) and expects Dedicated GPUs in early July. OrbitAI requests temporary burst access during demo week (not reserved).

Impact analysis (quick):
- If we honor ACME reservation (policy #1), we commit ~6 GPUs for the month of July. That consumes ~18% of our US-west Dedicated A100 pool during peak.
- If we instead split and give OrbitAI a portion, we risk missing ACME SLA or having to offer credits if we preempt them later.

Options to arbitrate:
A) Prioritize ACME (recommendation) — requires sign + payment by 6/20. Notify OrbitAI and schedule demo on preemptible cluster.
B) Split 50/50 for July — requ...

messages.subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning request - decision and next steps
messages.body:
Team —

Thanks for the clear options. After weighing revenue predictability, SLA obligations, and operational risk, we will:

1) Prioritize ACME under Strict reservation-first policy. Once Jordan confirms signed SOW and initial invoice cleared by 2026-06-20, Capacity Ops will pin a 6-GPU block and mark it as Reserved in the scheduler by 2026-07-02.

2) Offer OrbitAI a 4-hour scheduled demo window on preemptible nodes the week of 7/06 with a 20% service credit if preempted. We will also offer a 30-day trial extension (no reserved capacity) and a 10% discount on first-month committed capacity if they choose to convert.

3) Jordan: please send ACME a brief confirmation email with deadlines (...

You indicated an initial commit of ~75k token-hours/day which maps roughly to a 6-GPU dedicated pool under our baseline latency targets (p50 120ms for inference). We need a signed SOW + payment milestone to lock that capacity in our Dedicated pool.
...

Priya Desai <priya@acme.ai>: Jordan —

Thanks for the clear summary. We can confirm the following:
- Target start date: 2026-07-06
- Initial commit: 75k token-hours/day (subject to SOW sign)
- SLA: we need 99.9% uptime during business hours (9a–6p ET), but open to negotiating p99 latency targets if that helps sizing.

Procurement contact is copied (purchasing@acme.ai) — they will be the ones to finalize the SOW and payment milesto...

Hi Jordan / team,

Thanks for the intro call earlier today. Beacon wants to lock down a mutual action plan so we can evaluate Redwood and firm up the hosting decision. A few constraints from our side:
- PHI workloads must remain in US regions and be under a contract that supports customer-managed KMS (HSM preferred).
- We need SOC 2 evidence and a short summary of audit controls up front.
- Initial acceptance testing should include latency < 250ms p99 for the 512-token path and throughput of 25 qps for batched inference.

We expect a 6-week evaluation window and would like a proposed MAP that calls out: PoC scope, acceptance criteria, timeline to production (hosted/dedicated/private optio...

Marissa / Priyom — looping you in to turn Sara's ask into a concrete MAP. High level thinking: start with a non-PHI PoC on Hosted (US-East) to validate latency and batch behavior, then run a short Dedicated trial for PHI workloads (VPC + KMS) and plan Private/on-prem only if there are gating regulatory items.

Ask: can you draft a 6-week MAP with milestones for the two-phase approach (Hosted -> Dedicated) and include acceptance tests (latency, throughput, quality checks)? Also note any infra or engineering tasks that would lengthen the timeline (e.g., KMS onboarding, SOC evidence packaging). Priyom — commercial ask: rough cost delta for a 3-month Dedicated trial so we can give Beacon an i...

messages.subject: RE: MAP kickoff: hosting & region constraints
messages.body:
Jordan — quick technical readout and recommended evaluation plan.

Recommendation summary:
- Phase 1 (2 weeks): Hosted PoC in us-east1 for non-PHI telemetry and functional acceptance. Validate model variants (fp16 vs int8), steady-state latency at target load, and KV/prefix cache behavior. Use a small representative prompt set (we should ask Beacon for 200 canonical prompts).
- Phase 2 (4 weeks): Dedicated trial in a customer-dedicated pool inside a US region with VPC peering and customer-managed KMS. This covers PHI while keeping our control plane hosted (optional Private if Beacon insists on private control plane).

Acceptance criteria (examples):
- Latency: p99 < 250ms for 512 tokens u...

messages.subject: RE: MAP kickoff: hosting & region constraints
messages.body:
Adding the commercial lens. Two items to include in the MAP before we share externally:
1) Pricing snapshot — Estimated cost for a 4-week Dedicated trial (reserved baseline) + delta vs hosted per-token run rates. We can show a short-term trial credit to accelerate decisioning (we should be prepared to offer 20% off list for the pilot).
2) Contract timeline — procurement often needs a draft MSA and data processing agreement; ask Beacon legal for a single point of contact and target completion date.

Suggested close plan:
- Week 0: MAP signed and prompt set delivered
- Weeks 1–2: Hosted PoC (non-PHI) — collection of telemetry and QA
- Weeks 3–6: Dedicated trial (PHI) — KMS + network validat...

messages.subject: MAP proposal & proposed sequencing (Hosted -> Dedicated) — Beacon Health
messages.body:
Sara — thanks for the clear constraints. Quick summary and proposed MAP steps below. Please reply with confirmation of the legal contact and whether Beacon can provide the 200-prompt seed set by next Monday.

Proposed sequence (recommended):
1) Hosted PoC (us-east1) — 2 weeks, non-PHI workloads only. Objectives: validate latency and batching behavior, collect telemetry for model selection.
2) Dedicated trial (US region) — 4 weeks for PHI. Setup: VPC peering, customer-managed KMS (HSM optional), SOC 2 evidence review. This will be our default production recommendation if your procurement/legal require managed KMS and VPC isolation.
3) Private deployment — only if Beacon requires an air-gap...

"Making inference predictable and economical for production apps."

- You care about deterministic/validated outputs: constrained decoding where possible, and a validation + retry pattern for downstream systems.

Redwood proposal (high level):
1) SDK + auth: provide o...

messages.subject: Re: Post-call recap & next steps — SDK auth + JSON-output guarantees
messages.body:
Jordan —

Thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifying questions from our side so Dev can scope the POC: 

1) When you say constrained decoding + validator + retry, can you outline the expected failure modes and SLA for retries? For instance, if the model fails schema validation how many retries do you attempt and do retries incur additional cost or latency guarantees?

2) Auth: we prefer short-lived tokens — OAuth sounds good. Is there a recommended rotation period you support for SDK-managed tokens? Also curious if mTLS is necessary only for Private deployments or whether it can be layered on top of hosted as an optional control.

3) Routing: we need assurance that traffic from our US g...

messages.subject: RE: Post-call recap & next steps — SDK auth + JSON-output guarantees
messages.body:
Sara / Tom —

Thanks — good questions. I outlined a pragmatic pattern below that we use at Redwood for structured-output reliability; happy to walk through these in the dev sync. TL;DR: mix constrained decoding when feasible, prefer deterministic sampling (temp=0) for rigid fields, validate post-gen, and use a bounded re-prompt/retry strategy that escalates to a higher-capacity model when necessary.

Failure modes and retry SLAs:
- Validation failure (schema mismatch, missing required field, type error): perform up to 3 deterministic retries with increasingly strict decoding constraints (see code-like policy below). Each retry increments latency; we can keep overall P99 latency SLO at X m...

- We’ll lock the POC SOW this week and send a DocuSign link (preview attached).
- Dev sync: Tue 4/14 at 11:00 AM PT ✅ (Jordan will send calendar invite).
- Deliverables for POC kickoff: sample JSON Schema, 50 example prompts, acceptance criteria (schema pass rate target, latency target).

Jordan — please send the calendar invite and the DocuSign link once the SOW is uploaded.

Sara — thanks for confirming you’ll share the schema today. Looking forward to the sync.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Head of Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference

messages.body:
Hi Sophie — thanks for the clear list. I want to be explicit and practical about what Redwood can commit to for Acme's pilot and what would require an enterprise Private engagement. High-level summary up front:

- EU Primary Residency: We can serve production inference from EU-only capacity and pin your model/data to EU-only tenants for the pilot.
- Controlled ephemeral cross-border processing: for latency or capacity burst scenarios, we can allow a controlled cross-border processing "ephemeral window" with the following constraints:
 * Duration: any request that is routed out of EU will be processed transiently and all request-derived state (KV caches, temporary logs) will be deleted wit...

messages.subject: Re: Data residency, subprocessors, and cross-region handling questions
messages.body:
Alex — quick internal note before we finalize:

1) The 24-hour deletion window for ephemeral caches is reasonable as a pilot commitment, but legal will want explicit language that: deletion includes KV cache shards, ephemeral logs, and any temporary models spawned for batching.
2) We should offer an optional configuration: EU-only KMS (customer-managed) vs Redwood-managed EU KMS; customers should be able to enforce customer-managed for regulated tenants.
3) For the DPA clause, recommend we add a monitoring and reporting commitment for the pilot: weekly volume and percent-of-requests that used cross-border burst routing.
4) Confirm that data retained in access logs (90 days) is pseudonymiz...

messages.subject: Re: Data residency, subprocessors, and cross-region handling questions
messages.body:
Hi Sophie — following internal alignment (copied Vanessa and Neha), here's a consolidated response we can commit to for the Oct pilot and our recommended path forward:

Committed pilot items (EU-pinned):
- Production inference will be served from EU-pinned capacity.
- Ephemeral cross-border processing: enabled but strictly controlled to "burst inference" only and limited to a 24-hour deletion window for ephemeral compute artifacts (KV cache shards, transient model worker state, and temporary logs).
- Access logs: retained for 90 days; logs will surface only request ids and timestamps by default. Mapping to customer identifiers (email, project id) requires a formal Redwood ops request and ...

messages.body:
Hi Alex / Priya —

Thanks again for the call earlier — really helpful to see how Meridian is prioritizing inference reliability for the new clinical summarization feature. Quick recap and next steps from our side so we stay aligned with your ops sprint: 

Key pain points we heard
- End-to-end 95th percentile latency target of <= 250ms for short queries (<= 512 tokens) under normal traffic.
- High concurrency during morning batch windows: expect spikes to ~1200 qps for embedding + generation mix.
- Strict data residency: model inference must run in us-east-2 private VPC with customer-managed egress.

Goals we recommended (aligned with platform constraints)
- Production SLOs: 99th p90/p95 b...

messages.subject: Re: Post-call: Ops sprint recap & topology recommendations for Meridian Health
messages.body:
Thanks Grace — solid summary. A couple clarifications from our side: 

- The morning spike is bursty: we see concentrated traffic for ~45–90s windows across ~20 minutes, not sustained for >5 minutes. That makes buffer autoscaling decisions important.
- For data residency, we need a customer-owned VPC peering option (no public egress from the model nodes).
- For model variants: accuracy-tuned variant should support sequence lengths up to 2k tokens; low-latency variant primarily up to 512 tokens.

Yes to the walkthrough Wednesday — can you do 10:00am ET? Priya and our infra lead will join.

—Alex
Alex Mercer
Director, Platform Engineering — Meridian Health

to: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Post-call: Ops sprint recap & topology recommendations for Meridian Health
messages.body:
Adding a couple of engineering notes before the Wednesday walkthrough: 

- Autoscaling: given the bursty 45–90s windows, we recommend a two-tier policy: a small warm pool (always-on) sized for ~15% of peak and a reactive burst pool with GPU pre-warm hooks. This keeps cold-start tail safe while limiting reserved capacity.
- Batching: enforce a 20–30ms continuous batching window on the low-latency pool; larger batching (80–120ms) can be used in warm pool for long-form tasks to reduce cost.
- KV cache: pin cache shards to the low-latency pool; ensure TTLs align with clinical session patterns (default 4 hours, adjustable).
- Fallbacks: route to the quantized accuracy variant if the latency po...

messages.subject: Re: Pilot scope & incremental top-up option
messages.body:
Hi Ava — thanks for the clear notes and the SOW. Short answers up front: yes on a one-week top-up, and we can offer a discounted incremental rate if we get a commitment signal to convert to an annual contract within 30 days after the pilot.

How we typically structure it: the 4-week timebox is a fixed pilot fee that covers baseline provisioning and support. The top-up is treated as a separate one-week add-on with metered charges; for this situation I can propose a 20% discount on the top-up week if Rivermark signs a 12-month commitment (we'd put that in a simple amendment). If you don't convert, the top-up would be billed at standard metered rate.

I'll loop in Marissa (sales lead) to con...

title: Proposal: AB rollout guardrails + automated revert for Peridot (SLA mitigation)
body:
Asha Rao <asha@peridot.ai>: Marcus —

Quick note from our SRE team: during the AB rollout you ran last night we saw 3x P99 tail latency for a subset of production requests that include long-context chat flows. That spike started at ~01:10 UTC and lasted ~12 minutes (see attached telemetry snapshot). It triggered downstream timeouts and two customer incidents.

We appreciate the gradual rollout, but we need stronger automatic mitigation to me...

Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>: Hi Asha — thank you for the data and flagging the customer impact. Sorry you hit that during the AB run. We dug into the telemetry and saw the same spike; appears correlated with long-token sequences and a particular quantized variant we pushed into the B cohort.

High-level take: we can enable a short-circuit automatic revert policy (we call it "guardrail auto-revert") tied to the P99 threshold and a rollin...

normalized.subject: Proposal: AB rollout guardrails + automated revert for Peridot (SLA mitigation)

messages.subject: High tail latency during recent AB test - need rollback safety
messages.body:
Marcus —

Quick note from our SRE team: during the AB rollout you ran last night we saw 3x P99 tail latency for a subset of production requests that include long-context chat flows. That spike started at ~01:10 UTC and lasted ~12 minutes (see attached telemetry snapshot). It triggered downstream timeouts and two customer incidents.

We appreciate the gradual rollout, but we need stronger automatic mitigation to meet our contractual 99.9% uptime/latency SLA while you run experimental models. Specifically, can Redwood enable an automatic revert/pin to the previous model variant the instant P99 exceeds 1.5x baseline for more than a rolling 2-minute window? We also want an explicit durability...

cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: High tail latency during recent AB test - need rollback safety
messages.body:
Hi Asha — thank you for the data and flagging the customer impact. Sorry you hit that during the AB run. We dug into the telemetry and saw the same spike; appears correlated with long-token sequences and a particular quantized variant we pushed into the B cohort.

High-level take: we can enable a short-circuit automatic revert policy (we call it "guardrail auto-revert") tied to the P99 threshold and a rolling window. A spec and proposed parameters are attached (redwood-proposed-ab-guardrails-v1.pdf). Proposed defaults for Peridot to satisfy your SLA:

- Trigger: P99 > 1.5x baseline for 120s continuous window (rolling 30s checks)
- Action: immediate traffic re-route to last pinned stable m...

to: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: High tail latency during recent AB test - need rollback safety
messages.body:
Folks — quick internal: I read Marcus's proposal. From a product/risk perspective I support enabling the guardrail auto-revert for Peridot as an SLA mitigation, but request the following modifications before we flip it on:

- Make the pin duration configurable per-account; for Peridot set initial pin to 2 hours (not 4) with escalation to 8 hours if SRE requests it.
- Add a cooldown that prevents immediate re-canary to the same variant for 24 hours unless manually authorized by the CE and SE leads.
- Ensure the auto-revert is accompanied by a read-only snapshot of the exact model binary hash + config sent to Peridot's audit log.

Jordan/Dylan: can you confirm runtime feasibility? Specifica...

to: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: High tail latency during recent AB test - need rollback safety
messages.body:
Lauren — fast follow on feasibility: yes, we can do an atomic traffic switch at the routing layer to the pinned variant with these caveats:

- KV cache mismatch: if the pinned variant uses a different tokenizer/kv-layout we may see a short cold-KV penalty for affected sessions. We can mitigate by routing existing session tokens to a degraded path for a short time while warming the cache asynchronously. That will keep per-request latency within the SLA but may temporarily increase token CPU for those sessions.
- We will implement a 30s precondition check that validates the pinned model is load-ready on all regional nodes before cutting traffic. If any node fails the precondition, the auto-...

messages.subject: Dedicated capacity intake — traffic & sizing data request
messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team liked the latency and rollout controls. We're evaluating Redwood Dedicated for our recommendation engine and need a sizing baseline to take into procurement. Can you send over your standard questionnaire for dedicated sizing? Specifically we want to capture: peak QPS, sustained concurrency, prompt/response token lengths (avg/p95), expected streaming vs non-streaming, and any guidance you use for cache hit rate assumptions.

I'll attach a sample day's traffic export from our staging cluster in a follow-up. Key constraints: our SLA requires 99th percentile response < 250ms for single-turn ranking calls; we expect heavy promotional spikes ...

messages.subject: Re: Dedicated capacity intake — traffic & sizing data request
messages.body:
Hi Javier —

Appreciate the context. I'm attaching our Dedicated Sizing Playbook questionnaire (XLSX) which asks for the fields you mentioned plus some operational details that materially affect fleet sizing (burst patterns, percent streaming, KV cache reuse, desired warm pool, and model pinning). A few quick clarifying asks that will speed a sizing pass:

- Are ranking calls single-turn or multi-turn (do you expect history / chat context in the request)?
- Is the 99th < 250ms requirement for cold-start tolerant traffic, or only once instances are warmed?
- Do you expect per-tenant isolation (dedicated pool per region) or a shared dedicated pool for all traffic?

We can turn around a prel...

messages.subject: Re: Dedicated capacity intake — traffic & sizing data request
messages.body:
Rachel —

Thanks, questionnaire attached (completed). Quick answers inline:

- Ranking calls are single-turn (no chat history) for the high-QPS path; we have a smaller set of exploration chat sessions (multi-turn) but they’re < 5% of the overall volume.
- The 99th < 250ms target applies to warmed instances during normal operation — we can tolerate slightly higher latency during cold starts if needed, but want warm routing to be the common path.
- We want a shared dedicated pool per region (US) with tenant-aware request quotas — not per-tenant pools.

Traffic summary (from the attached CSV):
- Baseline sustained QPS: 1,200 RPS (ranking calls)
- Peak bursts during promotions: up to 6,000 RP...

messages.subject: Internal: preliminary fleet sizing notes for Nimbus (do not forward)
messages.body:
Team — quick draft sizing thoughts based on Javier's numbers and our standard assumptions:

Summary of inputs used:
- Baseline sustained: 1,200 RPS
- Promo peak: 6,000 RPS (20–45 min windows)
- Avg tokens: 38 prompt + 140 completion; p95 total ~560 tokens
- 60% cacheable (KV/prefix) 
- 10% streaming calls

Preliminary recommendation (US region):
- Dedicated pool: 8 x A100-80GB (or 12 x A40-48GB equiv) serving Llama-2-13B-chat q4/q8 quantized variant with KV cache enabled.
- Autoscale policy: baseline warm pool of 6 workers, burst-to-12 workers with 10-minute cooldown for sustained spikes; immediate short-burst transient capacity via pre-warmed spare instances for sub-5m spikes.
- Batching...

cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>

- SLOs: target p99 < 350ms for warmed requests; p99 < 500ms during cold-start windows

Priya Sen <priya.sen@luminahc.com>: Hi Rachel,

We started the phased rollout of the fresher model (v3.2-opt) across production yesterday and are seeing sustained p95 tail latency regressions in Region us-west: ~480–520ms for a subset of requests, plus a 2.4% increase in 5xxs (most appear transient but correlated with longer contexts). Attached are samples and a snapshot from our telemetry.

Given our SLA commitment (99.9% uptime, p95 < 400ms for th...

- Traffic quarantine: carve the affected cohort (by request path + header token) and route 100% of that cohort to the stable model (v3.1-stable).
- Auto-pin policy: pin to v3.1-stable when ei...

messages.subject: Traffic quarantine & automatic pin request — Lumina Health rollout
messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

We started the phased rollout of the fresher model (v3.2-opt) across production yesterday and are seeing sustained p95 tail latency regressions in Region us-west: ~480–520ms for a subset of requests, plus a 2.4% increase in 5xxs (most appear transient but correlated with longer contexts). Attached are samples and a snapshot from our telemetry.

Given our SLA commitment (99.9% uptime, p95 < 400ms for the primary endpoints), we need a short-term operational allowance: can Redwood enable a traffic quarantine (isolate the affected cohort to the stable model) and configure an automatic pin to the stable model if p95 > 450ms for 10 continuous minutes or error rate delta > 1.5%? We w...

cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Traffic quarantine & automatic pin request — Lumina Health rollout
messages.body:
Hi Priya — thanks for the detailed data and the quick heads-up. We can absolutely put guardrails in place immediately. Proposed short-term mitigation (can enable within ~15 minutes after you give the go-ahead):

- Traffic quarantine: carve the affected cohort (by request path + header token) and route 100% of that cohort to the stable model (v3.1-stable).
- Auto-pin policy: pin to v3.1-stable when either: p95 > 450ms for 10 continuous minutes OR 5xx delta > 1.5% for 10 continuous minutes.
- Telemetry/audit: we will persist all routing changes, the triggering metric windows, and the pin action to your audit log (retained for 90 days) and generate a time-stamped remediation report.

I attac...

messages.subject: Re: Traffic quarantine & automatic pin request — Lumina Health rollout
messages.body:
Rachel / Priya — approving the operational allowance with the following caveats and requirements:

- Approval: Temporary SLA carve-out granted for up to 72 hours for the Lumina rollout contingent on the mitigation controls Rachel outlined.
- Audit & retention: We need full trace and decision logs stored (we'll use the 90-day audit retention you mentioned) plus a summary report shared with Lumina's compliance and our legal team within 24 hours of the pin action.
- Contract/Legal: Please confirm the deal id (Rachel noted LUM-2026-07-14) so I can inform Legal to prepare a short amendment documenting the exception.
- Instrumentation: Aditya, please create an incident JIRA so Eng/Infra can cap...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>, Priya Sen <priya.sen@luminahc.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Traffic quarantine & automatic pin request — Lumina Health rollout
messages.body:
Acknowledged — I'll spin up the incident JIRA and set up a dedicated telemetry slice for Lumina in the monitoring dashboard. Plan:

- Create JIRA: ENGSUP-1284 (Lumina traffic quarantine).
- Implement quarantine routing rule + auto-pin policy on canary group; run in dry-run for 5 minutes, then flip to active if no regressions in the routing infra.
- Add a continuous recorder to capture the full 15-min pre- and post-trigger traces and store them in the audit bucket.

Rachel — proceeding to enable dry-run now. Priya — expect a follow-up in ~20–30 minutes with dry-run results and a suggested 30-minute sync window.

—Aditya
Aditya Rao
Director, Site Reliability, Redwood Inference

On Mon, May ...

- 2026-04-09 target close works for the commercial timeline
- We can deliver a scoped dataset (HealthGrid-support-50k) by 2026-03-22; Samira will coordinate on any procurement access/permissions for sharing the export.

Samira: looping you in for procurement / infra access.

Liam

>

messages.subject: Fwd: MAP: Technical evaluation — benchmark criteria & shortlist
messages.body:
Jordan / Diego —

Forwarding Liam's confirmation above. Can you draft a concise benchmark runbook and a model shortlist (3–5 variants) with recommended quantization and expected cost estimates? Key constraints: P95 <= 180ms target, cost goal <= $0.40 / 1k tokens. Please also flag any tests we should run to validate caching and batching effects.

Deliverable expectations: runbook.md (steps + scripts), a CSV with model/flavor/expected-cost/notes, and an estimate of run time + infra cost. Aim for a draft by COB Tue 2026-03-24.

Thanks — Marissa

> -----Original Message-----
> From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@healthgrid.com>
> To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
> Cc: Samira Patel <sa...

to: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: MAP: Technical evaluation — benchmark criteria & shortlist — runbook + shortlist
messages.body:
Thanks Marissa — sketching the runbook and shortlist below. Diego and I will produce the runbook doc and the model-cost CSV by EOD today. High-level plan:

Benchmark runbook (high level)
1) Ingest HealthGrid-support-50k into our eval harness (anonymize PII according to their retention rules)
2) Warm KV cache with realistic prefixes from the dataset (to simulate conversational cache hits)
3) Run steady-state traffic generator at 50 RPS for 2 hours with concurrency patterns matched to their production profile
4) Measure: token-level throughput, P50/P95/P99 generation latency, model CPU/GPU memory footprint, error rates, and cost estimate computed from observed token/sec and instance-hour co...

- Deliverables: runbook.md, model-shortlist.csv, benchmark results + cost breakdown, and post-run recommendations
- Fixed dates: dataset due 2026-03-22; run execution 2026-03-25–03-27; results review and decision meeting week of 2026-03-30; target contract close 2026-04-09
- Owners: HealthGrid (dataset & infra access), Jordan/Diego (execution & costing), Marissa (commercial coordination)
- Open item: Samira to confirm procurement timeline and whether HealthGrid prefers Redwood sandbox or their cloud account for the runs

Thanks again for the quick turnaround on the agenda. We’re excited to see the roadmap demo, but procurement just flagged the contract redlines and said they won’t sign until we resolve two items (data retention and audit logging access).

messages.subject: Re: Roadmap QBR — procurement gating
messages.body:
Hi Rachel / Marissa,

Apologies for the short notice. Procurement reviewed and the two must-fix items are:
1) Data retention: need clearer deletion policy and customer-controlled retention windows in the SOW.
2) Audit access: require 90-days of access to request logs and ability to onboard an auditor on request (SLA language).

We can turn documents around quickly if Redwood provides redline language for the SOW and a brief note on how audit logs are exported. If you can share the suggested SOW redlines today, we’ll aim to return an approved DocuSign package by EOD Thursday (May 14). If that timeline slips, we’ll need to move the QBR off the 18th.

Preferred new times for us (blocked):
- ...

messages.subject: Re: Roadmap QBR — procurement gating and run-of-show
messages.body:
Hi all — grateful for the clarity, procurement team. I’ve attached a draft Mutual Action Plan (MAP) that maps the SOW redline bullets to owners and dates. Karthik, I put a short note in the security section about exportable logs and retention controls; happy to expand that if you want more technical detail.

Proposed run-of-show for a moved demo (45 + 15):
- 0–5 min: Quick introductions and goals (Rachel)
- 5–20 min: Roadmap highlights and expansion value (Rachel + Marissa)
- 20–35 min: Security & compliance deep dive (Karthik)
- 35–45 min: Live demo focused on integration points (Marissa)
- 45–60 min: Procurement/legal Q&A and next steps (all)

If procurement confirms the DocuSign by EOD...

messages.subject: RE: Observed Dedicated capacity spike — next steps for renewal (sizing note)
messages.body:
Marissa / Logan / Emma — attaching a short technical summary of observed utilization and my recommendation. Key points up front:

- Current Dedicated pool: 8x A100-40GB (shared across two clusters, AtlasHealth has dedicated slice of 6 GPUs historically).
- Typical steady-state consumption: 60% of committed capacity (avg token/sec 18k across models) during daytime ET.
- Peak windows (17:00–22:00 ET): sustained 92–98% utilization for 3–5 hours on weekdays; we recorded 7 incidents of request queuing in Q4 correlated with batch jobs.
- KV-cache hit rate for atlas-prod: 42% on average (could improve with longer-lived requests / prefix caching).

Sizing recommendation (to meet 99.5% non-queued ...

messages.subject: RE: Observed Dedicated capacity spike — pricing implications
messages.body:
Team / Emma — ran the numbers for the two most realistic options. High-level summary attached and pasted below. All numbers reflect committed monthly pricing plus expected on-demand burst in worst-case months.

- Option A (Add 4x A100 to committed slice -> 10 GPUs):
 * Incremental monthly commit: $48,000
 * Estimated blended per-token cost reduction: ~12% vs current because of reduced burst usage
 * 12-month committed delta (annualize): ~$576k

- Option B (Add 2x A100 + autoscale burst):
 * Incremental monthly commit: $25,800
 * Expected on-demand burst (worst months): additional $6k–$12k/month variable
 * 12-month committed delta: ~$309.6k (+ variable)

We also modeled a mid-year checkpo...

messages.subject: Re: Observed Dedicated capacity spike — next steps for renewal
messages.body:
Thanks — this is exactly what I needed. A couple of quick items:

1) We prefer Option B initially (2 GPUs + burst) to avoid an immediate large capital commit. Please include the 90-day utilization checkpoint with a one-time right-sizing credit if AtlasHealth average utilization falls below 50% for three months after renewal.
2) Could you send a short addendum that lists expected p95 latency ranges under Option B and the operational escalation path if we see repeated queuing? Procurement will want that in the MSA appendix.
3) Let's hold the 30-minute call for Friday 14:00 ET so our engineering lead can join — Marissa, is that okay?

If you can get the addendum and updated proposal by EOD t...

Hi Logan,

Quick note — procurement and execs are pushing to lock a June delivery window for the custom routing + audit-export controls we discussed. Senior management tied our Q3 rollout to this. We can move forward with an increased commitment (higher committed spend + 12-month MSA) but need a firm plan for delivering the two items below before July 20: 

- Tenant-level audit export (full trace + redaction options)
- Request-routing conditional routes (by region + model affinity)

I've attached the updated proposal with our legal/finance placeholders. If Redwood can commit to the above by July 20, we will sign the SOW and start onboarding planning immediately. Can you confirm whether th...

messages.subject: Fwd: Priority lift request & conditional timeline
messages.body:
Team — forwarding Artemis' ask. TL;DR: they will expand commit if we guarantee delivery of audit-export and conditional routing by July 20. This is higher ACV and strategic in the financial vertical. 

Asks: 
- Priyom/neha: What is the minimal acceptable scope we can deliver for "tenant-level audit export" in a July timeframe (compliance evidence + export pipeline)?
- Jordan: Engineering estimate for conditional routing (region + model affinity rules) as a targeted, limited-scope feature — can we carve a canary implementation?
- Marissa: I'll coordinate commercial terms but want to avoid overpromising. If product/eng can provide a firm commitment window, we'll move procurement to SOW.

Pl...

to: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Priority lift request & conditional timeline
messages.body:
On Mon, Jun 1, 2026 at 12:42 PM Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — forwarding Artemis' ask.

We did an impact pass on both asks. Short answer: possible with a scoped tradeoff. Details below.

1) Tenant-level audit export
- Minimal deliverable (July 20): export pipeline that ships per-tenant event logs (request metadata, trace ids, model id, user-provided obj ids) to a secure S3 bucket with masking redaction options. This would include a documented export schema and a one-off export job for their tenant.
- Gaps relative to full compliance product: no UI for ad-hoc exports, no retention-policy automation, manual runbook for secure transfer.
- Estimated effort: 2 sprints (teams...

to: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Priority lift request & conditional timeline
messages.body:
Quick eng note on effort and blocking items: 

- Conditional routing: the 3-sprint estimate assumes we can reuse existing routing hooks and that telemetry provides representative staging traffic. If we need full runtime affinity guarantees, add 2 more sprints for affinity-safe scheduling and cache residency changes.
- Audit export: telemetry infra will need service-account KMS integration and a one-time manual runbook for redaction logic. I'm ok delivering the minimal export pipeline by July 20 provided we get a prioritized sprint slot and a dedicated SRE for rollout support.

Blockers we need from Sales/CS:
- Signed SOW by June 10 to open sprint nomination for June/July.
- Commitment to ...

cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Artemis Financial Procurement <procurement@artemis-fin.com>
messages.subject: Re: Priority lift request & conditional timeline
messages.body:
Tom — appreciate the clarity and the willingness to expand commitment. We can support a scoped delivery that meets your immediate compliance and routing needs on the timeline you provided, with these conditions: 

1) Scope: tenant-level export pipeline (secure S3 export with redaction options, documented schema) + canary conditional routing via routing DSL and ops playbook. Not included: full UI export, retention automation, or global model affinity guarantees.
2) Timeline: delivery by July 20 contingent on the SOW signed and a funded SRE rotation.
3) Acceptance: we will enable a canary in Artemis staging for validation and require a 5-business-day validation period before wide enablement...

title: CleverPay livechat: elevated streaming latency — escalation and mitigation
body:
Samir Patel <samir@cleverpay.com>: Hi team,

We started seeing severe delays in our livechat streaming responses around 09:15 PDT this morning. Instead of incremental tokens arriving every 200-300ms, we often see 2–6s gaps, and a few sessions stalled entirely (no more tokens after initial prompt). This is affecting our checkout support flow — conversion impact is visible.

What we observed:
- A3 instances serving us showed higher p95 latency; sessi...

Short-term mitigation recommendations (client-side):
1) Switch to non-streaming responses for high-priority checkout flows (set stream=false) — this avoids token-by-token dependence when the network path is experienci...
