to: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5
messages.body:
Hi all —

We’re ready for cross-functional review on the refreshed Support escalation playbook (Hosted/Dedicated/Private). Goal is to publish the Confluence pages early next week and start using the new severity + SLA language immediately for new escalations.

Docs to review:
- Escalation Playbook Refresh v0.9 (attached)
- Sign-off checklist (attached)
- Working notes (Drive): shared_drives/customer-success/escalation-playbook-refresh-working-notes

Requested sign-off by: Thu 2/5 EOD PT

What I need from each group:
1) SRE (Sean/Rafael)
 - Confirm paging thresholds + escalation triggers are consistent with on-call + SLO alerting
 - Confirm Slack bridge / #incidents handoff rules
 - Confir...

cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5
messages.body:
Reviewed. Mostly good — one blocker + a couple nits.

BLOCKER:
- SLA table currently implies we page SRE for “P1 enterprise” by default even if impact is isolated to a single tenant with an available workaround.
 - Proposed wording change: make paging trigger “customer-impacting outage/degradation meeting Sev1+ thresholds” rather than “customer tier.” Tier should influence update cadence + comms, not paging.
 - Specifically: in Escalation Triggers, replace “Enterprise P1 => page SRE” with “Page SRE when: (a) elevated error rate/latency affecting multiple customers or a region, OR (b) one customer but no workaround and business-critical workflow blocked.”

Nits:
- Please add “request IDs +...

cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5
messages.body:
Thanks Dev.

Platform ownership looks correct with two changes:
- Quota/Rate Limits: primary = Control Plane on-call (Platform), backup = SRE if it’s correlated with broader degradation. Please avoid directing Support to ping individual engineers.
- Audit Logs: primary = Platform (data pipeline), but escalation path should include Security/Compliance only if customer is requesting formal attestation / evidence (otherwise it’s a Platform+SRE operational issue).

Other notes:
- Private installer issues: if a customer is mid-upgrade and stuck, we should trigger a Platform page once “rollback not possible” is confirmed (your checklist mentions this; just make it a bold callout).

Not a blocke...

cc: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5
messages.body:
Reviewed from CS + enterprise SLA perspective.

Edits requested (not blockers):
- “Every 30 minutes” updates for enterprise P0: please clarify that cadence is during active mitigation window; if we’re “Waiting on Customer” we should explicitly state we will resume updates when new info is available, but still keep a time-based check-in (e.g., every 2 hours) to avoid silence.
- Credits guidance: please add “credits decisions require CS leadership approval” (Jasmine or me) and should not be promised in the initial acknowledgement.

Otherwise approved.

Rachel Kim
Enterprise Customer Success

---

cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5
messages.body:
Approved with Rachel’s wording change on update cadence + credits.

One additional request: in the comms templates, add a single sentence that sets expectations on what we can share (“we’ll share mitigation steps and status; we may not be able to share full root cause immediately”).

Thanks for pushing this through.

Jasmine Liu
VP Customer Success & Support

---

to: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Escalation Playbook refresh — cross-functional review + sign-off by Thu 2/5
messages.body:
Thanks all — incorporated changes and closing out sign-off.

Updates made (v1.0 draft in Confluence now):
- Paging triggers updated per Sean: tier influences comms cadence; paging based on Sev thresholds / lack of workaround. (Also added required context: request IDs, region, model, start time, recent deploy.)
- Bridge naming standardized to: sup-<SUP#>-<region>-<shortdesc>
- Platform ownership updated per Priya (quota/rate limits + audit logs + private installer bold callout)
- Enterprise comms cadence clarified per Rachel (active mitigation vs Waiting on Customer) + added explicit “credits require CS leadership approval; do not promise”
- Added comms template line per Jasmine re: what w...

Please pick any of these windows this week:
• Thu 9/11 9:30–10:30am PT

messages.subject: Re: Initial discovery — Novus Cloud / Redwood Inference (ND-4821)
messages.body:
Thanks Karthik — sounds good. I looped in Ethan (engineering lead), procurement, and security so we have the right people on the thread.

Ethan — we chatted with Redwood and they're a candidate for our inference layer. Would you and a SE join the call to validate integration constraints? Procurement and security, heads up that we might need vendor intake items after the discovery.

Of the times Karthik proposed, Thu 9/11 3:00–4:00pm PT works best for me. Ethan, confirm?

Thanks —
Priya Rao
Senior Product Manager | Novus Cloud
priya@novuscloud.com

messages.subject: Re: Initial discovery — Novus Cloud / Redwood Inference (ND-4821)
messages.body:
Hi all — Thu 3pm PT should work. I can attend for the first 40 minutes; if we need deeper infra talk I can bring in one of our platform engineers.

Quick note on scope so the SE can prep: we run a mix of GPU-backed VMs in us-west and some on-prem (air-gapped) for regulated workloads. Our main questions:
- How does Redwood handle private deployments / VPC connectivity?
- Will we need a separate private control plane for on-prem?
- Typical per-token latency at 2k context for small-batch interactive traffic?

Thanks —
Ethan
Engineering Lead, Novus Cloud
ethan.miles@novuscloud.com

messages.subject: Re: Initial discovery — Novus Cloud / Redwood Inference (ND-4821)
messages.body:
Great — confirmed Thu 9/11 3:00–4:00pm PT. I’ve created a calendar event (link below) and attached the agenda + brief roles doc.

Soojin (SE) — can you prepare:
- short diagram showing private vs hosted options and VPC connectivity patterns we typically deploy
- latency profiles for 2k context on A100 and T4-class workers (example numbers for baseline)
- a 5-minute demo of Dedicated vs Private routing if time allows

Procurement — would you prefer we send a vendor intake packet ahead of time? We have a short SOW template and an SOC 2 attestation we can attach.

Attachments: nd-4821-multi-stakeholder-roles.docx
Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=novus-discovery-nd4821

Thanks,...

to: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Ethan Miles <ethan.miles@novuscloud.com>, Priya Rao <priya@novuscloud.com>
messages.subject: Re: Initial discovery — Novus Cloud / Redwood Inference (ND-4821)
messages.body:
Thanks Karthik — I’ll prepare the diagram and latency slides. Two quick clarifications for the discovery so we use the 60 minutes well:
1) Will the on-prem workloads require air-gapped installs (no outbound internet)? If yes, our private/on-prem package includes an offline installer and additional lead time for compliance paperwork.
2) Can you share a typical request pattern (RPS per endpoint, average tokens per request) or a recent trace? Even ballpark numbers let me prepare a realistic batching/quantization recommendation.

If you’d prefer not to share traces ahead of time, a short list of peak qps and average tokens will do. I attached a one-page security evidence checklist so security...

messages.subject: Re: Initial discovery — Novus Cloud / Redwood Inference (ND-4821)
messages.body:
Hi Karthik — thanks for the agenda. We’d appreciate if you can share the SOC 2 summary and your standard SOW template ahead of the call so procurement can start review. If there are pricing tiers or dedicated capacity minimums, include those in the packet.

We’ll be on the call Mon 9/15 3pm PT (Ethan confirmed updated time with eng team). Please send the calendar invite to procure@novuscloud.com as well.

Procurement Team
Novus Cloud

title: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups
body:
Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>: Team — quick leadership update on the CS/Support “known-issues sprint” (planning + execution + first adoption cleanup). We’ve finished publishing the initial batch and wired the cross-links into Support workflows.

messages.body:
Team — quick leadership update on the CS/Support “known-issues sprint” (planning + execution + first adoption cleanup). We’ve finished publishing the initial batch and wired the cross-links into Support workflows.

1) What shipped (public/internal docs)
- Known Issues landing/index (Support/CS entry point)
 - Confluence landing: /customer-success-and-support/known-issues-and-workarounds/known-issues-index
 - Tagging + “when to escalate” callouts + last-updated discipline
- 5 workaround articles published (curated from top Q1 themes)
 - Streaming timeouts/disconnects + reconnect guidance (links from SDK troubleshooting)
 - 429 quota mismatch / rate-limit headers interpretation
 - Dedicated...

to: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups
messages.body:
This is great progress.

Two notes from SRE side:
1) Please make sure the “when to escalate” section stays strict on paging thresholds vs. “customer unhappy.” The new wording is much better; we should hold the line so we don’t create an alternate incident process.
2) For ENG-4824 / audit log lag: we can commit to a first pass alert + dashboard this week, but we need Rachel to confirm the expectation we’re anchoring to (what we will and won’t message as an SLA breach).

Happy to review the final “support-safe dashboards” list once Allison shares it.

— Sean

to: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups
messages.body:
Thanks Jordan.

Eng follow-ups:
- ENG-4825: patch is in final validation now; we’re targeting staged rollout starting Wed (canary -> 25% -> 100%) assuming no regressions.
- ENG-4828: diagnostics bundle PR is ready for review; I’ll ask Ethan/Mina to prioritize so we can match the KB steps exactly.
- ENG-4829: we can land the guardrails, but defaults need SRE sign-off. Sean/Allison — can you propose threshold values that won’t spam pages?

Also: if we can capture “ticket resolved w/ KB link” as a structured field in Jira, we’ll have a much cleaner deflection metric. Dylan/Dev — is that feasible with the current automation work?

— Priya

cc: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups
messages.body:
Yes — feasible.

We can add a Jira field (checkbox or single-select) like “KB/Workaround Provided” + optionally “KB Article” (URL) and have automation prompt at resolution. That should also help with “repeat ticket” attribution.

I’ll sync with Dylan tomorrow and route it through INT-333 scope if it fits.

— Dev

cc: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups
messages.body:
Re: audit log expectations — aligned with what we’ve been messaging enterprise:
- We should avoid calling it an “SLA breach” unless it crosses the contractual language for that account.
- For the KB + alerting threshold, I’d anchor to “customer-impacting” at sustained lag > 30 minutes (with a separate earlier-warning threshold for SRE to investigate).

We can tighten the language in the article to be explicit: “This is a known issue under investigation; if lag exceeds X or you have a regulatory deadline, escalate.”

— Rachel

cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison.grant@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Known-issues sprint: results + early metrics (W07) — publishing complete, early deflection, eng follow-ups
messages.body:
Good outcome for a tight sprint.

Jordan — please do the 15-min staff walk-through this week, but keep it focused on:
1) the new “when to escalate” rules
2) the new SLA/update cadence language (and what we do NOT promise)
3) where the known-issues index lives + how we keep it current

Priya/Sean — thank you for committing to the follow-ups. Let’s treat the ENG items Jordan listed as the minimum bar so Support doesn’t carry the burden indefinitely.

— Jasmine
Jasmine Liu
VP, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

messages.subject: POC acceptance metrics + recommended deployment path for Vectorix Labs
messages.body:
Hi Jenna / Vectorix team —

Thanks again for a productive discovery call earlier. Per our conversation, I wanted to capture a concise recap, propose a POC plan that balances speed vs fidelity, and call out ownership and timelines so we can lock in kickoff next week.

Recap (key asks you shared):
- Evaluation goal: validate document-assistant accuracy for clinical protocols and triage guidance across ~5 representative workflows.
- Scale target: ~1k concurrent daily users during POC peak; production expected to be larger but unknown.
- Security: must support customer-managed keys and in-region data controls.
- Latency sensitivity: interactive experience — target 95th percentile under 400ms ...

messages.subject: Re: POC acceptance metrics + recommended deployment path for Vectorix Labs
messages.body:
Alex —

Appreciate the clear recap. Quick confirmation from our side:
- We'll provide 5 canonical prompts and a 2000-doc anonymized sample set by Thu 6/11 EOD.
- Network contact: Mark Liu (mark.liu@vectorixlabs.com) — he can handle the VPC questionnaire.
- Priority: we want to validate accuracy and safety first (Hosted kickoff is fine), but VPC/KMS support is a hard requirement before any production rollout.

One ask: can Redwood's hosted phase be configured to never persist raw docs to storage (even temporarily)? We need to ensure that the hosted trial meets our internal risk bar before we run our sample set.

Also — pricing sensitivity: we need a ballpark of POC token spend for the host...

messages.subject: Re: POC acceptance metrics + recommended deployment path for Vectorix Labs
messages.body:
All — quick infra notes based on Alex's plan and Mark's coming VPC info:

1) Hosted run (no persistence mode): we can run requests with transient-only logging. We will disable long-term storage of request payloads and only capture aggregated telemetry for the eval. Need Jenna's explicit confirmation to proceed with that data posture.

2) Dedicated sandbox sizing recs (ballpark):
 - 1 x 80GB memory GPU + 4 vCPU proxy for a 50 RPS sustained sim (model: gpt-open-13b-quant).
 - Estimated token cost for a 2-week hosted test (based on your sample size): ~$1,200–2,500 depending on prompt length; Dedicated sandbox infra cost estimate adds ~$4,500 for the reserved sandbox window (customer-visible ...

to: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: POC acceptance metrics + recommended deployment path for Vectorix Labs
messages.body:
Thanks team — that all sounds good.

Confirmations:
- We'll deliver the anonymized sample set + canonical prompts by today EOD.
- Mark Liu (network) will be in copy on the next note and will fill the VPC questionnaire.
- We confirm transient-only logging for the hosted test; we'll sign any trial addendum if needed.
- Budget: leadership approved up to $3k for the hosted run; please flag if estimates exceed that.

Action items I own: share prompts + sample set, connect Mark.
Action items Redwood owns: finalize hosted test harness, add transient logging confirmation to the Drive doc, Ben to post sizing + cost numbers.

Looking forward to kickoff 6/15.

Best,
Jenna Park
Head of Product
Vector...

body:
James Holloway <procurement@northridgehealth.org>: Alex —

Thanks for the updated SOW. Two things on our side that are non-negotiable for a Private deployment: 1) data residency — all customer data and backups must remain in our VPC in us-east-1 (no egress outside the account), and 2) audit & inspection rights — we need the ability to perform an onsite or remote audit of the runtime and logs at least once per year with 30 days' notice.

We also flagged telemetry a...

Why I'm escalating: their audit request as written gives them broad access (runtime, logs, personnel interviews) and no constraints on freq...

normalized.subject: Approval request: Northridge private deployment — audit carveout & data residency asks

messages.subject: RE: Statement of Work + deployment model questions
messages.body:
Alex —

Thanks for the updated SOW. Two things on our side that are non-negotiable for a Private deployment: 1) data residency — all customer data and backups must remain in our VPC in us-east-1 (no egress outside the account), and 2) audit & inspection rights — we need the ability to perform an onsite or remote audit of the runtime and logs at least once per year with 30 days' notice.

We also flagged telemetry and diagnostics in the contract; we need explicit language that no customer PII will be sent to Redwood telemetry systems without prior written consent.

Attaching the audit scope outline and redlines. Please escalate for approvals on your side — these are deal breakers for our co...

messages.subject: Fwd: RE: Statement of Work + deployment model questions
messages.body:
Forwarding Northridge's asks — their procurement is pushing hard on data-residency-in-VPC and a right to audit (onsite/remote once per year). They also want a clause preventing any PII telemetry without consent. Attached are their scope and our current SOW.

Why I'm escalating: their audit request as written gives them broad access (runtime, logs, personnel interviews) and no constraints on frequency or third-party auditors. Legal is concerned. They are also asking for absolute residency inside their VPC which we can do technically, but it changes our support/incident processes and may require a private deployment SKU.

Proposed trade: we can accept a scoped audit (defined windows, narrow...

messages.subject: Re: Fwd: RE: Statement of Work + deployment model questions
messages.body:
Alex — thanks for the flag and the docs. I reviewed their redlines. Summary of legal concerns:

- "Audit rights" as written are too broad: they request access to runtime internals, arbitrary personnel interviews, and unlimited third-party auditors.
- "No egress" language for backups & logs inside their account is acceptable technically but we need a clause that still allows Redwood to run monitoring agents for safety & support, and a carveout for incident response that can cross-account with explicit notice.
- Telemetry/PII: we should add explicit opt-in language and a technical appendix describing what telemetry is collected by default and how to disable it.

My recommended compromise (t...

messages.subject: Re: Fwd: RE: Statement of Work + deployment model questions
messages.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful notes. I reviewed the proposed compromise and approve moving forward on the following narrow exception path for Northridge Health:

1) Residency: approve VPC-only private deployment in us-east-1 with the standard support connectivity (mutual TLS) and a documented incident-response cross-account process.
2) Audit: approve one remote audit per 12 months scoped to access controls, configuration, and archival procedures. Allow one onsite audit in the first 12 months limited to the same scope. Third-party auditors permitted only with prior written approval and NDA; customer must cover reasonable travel and logistics costs. Any onsite requests require 30 days' notice a...

title: Renewal: EU data locality + APAC rollout + cross-region failover (SOW & pricing)
body:
Elena Petrova <elena@solushealth.com>: Hi Karthik —

Quick background: our annual contract with Redwood is up for renewal July 1. Our engineering and security teams want to confirm two items before we sign: (1) EU data locality for EU-hosted workloads (we need logs and inference data stored in eu-west-1 by default), and (2) a staged APAC rollout with cross-region failover to EU for DR.

We also expect to increase reserved capacity from 16->28 concurren...

karthik_iyer@redwood.ai: Hi Elena —

Thanks for the clear ask and timeline. We can accommodate EU data locality for inference and logs. For the APAC rollout we recommend a two-phase plan:

- Phase 1 (Q3): Provision APAC dedicated pool in ap-southeast-1 with canary routing for non-critical traffic
- Phase 2 (Q4): Promote APAC as primary for selected services; enable cross-region KV-cache replication and failover to EU with <30s cold-...

messages.subject: Renewal and region rollout questions (June renewal)
messages.body:
Hi Karthik —

Quick background: our annual contract with Redwood is up for renewal July 1. Our engineering and security teams want to confirm two items before we sign: (1) EU data locality for EU-hosted workloads (we need logs and inference data stored in eu-west-1 by default), and (2) a staged APAC rollout with cross-region failover to EU for DR.

We also expect to increase reserved capacity from 16->28 concurrent GPUs for committed throughput in Q3. Please share proposed SOW language (data locality appendix) and an updated pricing sheet showing the uplift and any term discounts.

Can we get a draft SOW and an estimated uplift by next Wednesday (18th)? Procurement is on the timeline.

Th...

messages.subject: Re: Renewal and region rollout questions (June renewal)
messages.body:
Hi Elena —

Thanks for the clear ask and timeline. We can accommodate EU data locality for inference and logs. For the APAC rollout we recommend a two-phase plan:

- Phase 1 (Q3): Provision APAC dedicated pool in ap-southeast-1 with canary routing for non-critical traffic
- Phase 2 (Q4): Promote APAC as primary for selected services; enable cross-region KV-cache replication and failover to EU with <30s cold-start failover SLAs

I’m looping in Rachel (account lead) and Marissa (renewals) to draft the SOW appendix and updated pricing.

We’ll target Wednesday 18th EOD with a draft SOW and a pricing workbook. If procurement needs an earlier meeting, let us know.

Regards,
Karthik Iyer
Head of...

messages.subject: FW: Renewal and region rollout questions (internal plan)
messages.body:
Karthik / Marissa —

Draft plan for internal alignment before we send to Solus:

1) SOW appendix: include explicit data residency clause (EU primary storage, audit logs retained in-region), encryption key residency via customer-managed KMS option, and a triage SOP for cross-region failover testing.
2) Pricing: show two scenarios — (A) 28 reserved GPUs committed for 12mo with 10% term discount, (B) 28 GPUs committed + 6-month burst pool at consumption rates.
3) Risk/Workstreams: Networking (VPC peering and route controls), Security (SOC2 evidence + penetration test window), Ops (runbook for failover & DR drills).

I started a SOW template and attached a gap doc. Planning to produce Solus-f...

messages.subject: Solus: Draft SOW + pricing (see attachments)
messages.body:
Hi Elena / Tom —

Attached are the draft SOW appendix for EU data locality, the proposed APAC rollout timeline, and a pricing workbook showing the committed capacity uplift. High-level summary:

- EU Data Locality: Inference outputs and logs for eu project namespaces will be stored in eu-west-1 by default. We offer optional customer-managed KMS for key residency.
- APAC Rollout: Phase 1 (Sept) — dedicated ap-southeast-1 pool for canary traffic (10%); Phase 2 (Dec) — full production promotion and automated failover policies configured to replicate KV cache and prefix caches.
- Capacity & Pricing: 28 reserved GPUs, 12-month term, list price +10% uplift vs current due to dedicated APAC infra...

messages.subject: Re: Solus: Draft SOW + pricing (see attachments)
messages.body:
Thanks Marissa — quick clarifications from Security & Procurement: 

1) Can you add explicit language that audit logs for EU projects are retained in-region for a minimum of 18 months and that log export endpoints are restricted to eu-west-1 only?
2) We need the failover SLA spelled out (target RTO / RPO) and the schedule for semi-annual failover drills with a 30-day advance notice window.
3) For KMS, our default is customer-managed keys in our AWS account; please confirm the integration steps and any limitations for automated key rotation.

Procurement also asked if there’s room to push the signature date to July 15 if we need more time for legal review — will the pricing hold if signatu...

messages.subject: Re: Solus: Draft SOW + pricing (see attachments)
messages.body:
Elena —

Thanks for the detailed notes, these are reasonable asks. Action items and responses below: 

- Audit logs: we will add an 18-month in-region retention clause and restrict automated exports to eu-west-1 by default; exports to other regions will require an approved request and justification.
- Failover SLA: propose RTO = 2 minutes for cached sessions, RTO = 15 minutes for cold start path; RPO = 5 seconds for KV cache replication. We’ll add semi-annual DR drills with 30-day notice in the SOW appendix.
- KMS: we support customer-managed KMS in the customer AWS account. Integration notes: we’ll require the customer to create the CMK with key policy that allows the Redwood service pri...

Hi Rachel,

Thanks again for sending Redwood's SOC 2 Type II report. Our security team reviewed the report and flagged two exceptions in the "change control" and "logical access" sections related to documented remediation items during the audit period (Q3–Q4 2025). We need a bit more color before we can proceed with procurement:

- Can you provide Redwood's management responses for each exception?
- Do you have evidence of remediation (change logs, deployment tickets, config snapshots) that we can review?
- Will you provide a bridge letter that covers the gap between the report end date and any remediation dates?

If possible, we'd like a short, customer-facing management response plus an...

Neha — FYI for procurement cadence and to help coordinate...

messages.body:
Rachel — notes below from engineering on the two items called out.

1) Change control exception (audit noted missing evidence for one out-of-window emergency patch):

- Owner: Dev Patel (eng services)
- Evidence available: PR #5721 (link in drive), CI pipeline run logs from 2025-12-18, deployment ticket TKT-9882.
- Status: remediation completed and validated via 2 smoke tests and one rollback test.

2) Logical access exception (audit found temporary elevated access for two support engineers without recorded approvals):
- Remediation performed: revoked all ...

Hi Laura / ProspectX team,

We have attached a management response that summarizes the root cause, ...

messages.subject: RE: Request: extended log TTL and deletion evidence for SIG/CAIQ
messages.body:
Alex / team — brief technical confirmation and what we can deliver: we can support per-tenant request-log retention up to 365 days via a Customer Retention Exception (CRE). Our standard default retention for request metadata is 30 days. Exception workflow and controls (high level):

- Storage and immutability: Logs are written to an append-only write path and replicated to a WORM-backed S3 bucket with object lock enabled for the configured retention period. For exceptions we can route tenant log objects to a longer-lived prefix and set lifecycle rules accordingly.
- Deletion workflow: A scheduled, authenticated deletion job (retention-worker) reconciles current TTL settings and removes ob...

Attached are: retention-exception-form.pdf (CRE template pre-filled), a small redacted deletion-audit-snippet.csv showing the deletion transaction records (metadata only), and a ZIP with the SIG evidence references (sig-evidence-cardinal.zip).

"We can export deletion transaction records (timestamp, object key prefix, deletion-job-id, initiating service principal) and a digest of pre- and post-deletion object counts. We will redact any payload-level PII — these are metadata-only records."

Reque...

messages.subject: Fwd: Customer impact — initial triage results (Streamlytics)
messages.body:
Marissa / Evelyn — quick triage from our side. Forwarding the logs bundle Anika sent and an initial summary.

--- Forwarded message from Anika Shah <anika@streamlytics.com> ---
(see attachments: logs_snapshot_20260310.zip)

Triage summary (observed via synthetic and live traffic):
- Errors coincide with new pod creation in the autoscaled pool: 502/504 responses during first 45-70s after pod creation.
- When requests hit an existing warmed pod, latency remains ~70–120ms (normal). When routed to a newly created pod we see +800–1200ms for the first few requests, sometimes resulting in 504.
- The model is ~7.4B parameter variant; we suspect the model loader + KV init is contributing to the de...

to: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Hosted API — noticed big p95/p99 latency spikes this morning
messages.body:
Thanks for the data — we reproduced the pattern in our staging autoscale test. Short summary of engineering findings:

What we observed:
- Cold-start delay arises during model artifact load + KV cache population; the model loader is CPU-bound initially and blockingly warms attention caches, which delays first tokens by ~800–1200ms for that pod.
- Autoscaler spins up N pods concurrently under burst conditions; concurrent loads cause queuing on the shared artifact store and transient CPU throttling on nodes.
- Some requests time out while the pod is finishing init, producing 502/504 visible to customers.

Immediate mitigations (1–2 hour timeline):
1) Route Streamlytics traffic to a warmed s...

cc: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: RE: Hosted API — noticed big p95/p99 latency spikes this morning
messages.body:
Hi Anika — thank you again for the fast signal and the logs. Short, concrete update and proposed remediation: 

What we'll do now (immediate, within 30 minutes):
- Enable warmed-pool routing for your account (we'll move % of traffic you nominate; default 100% for safety if you prefer).
- Set min replicas to 2 for your autoscaling pool to avoid scale-from-zero cold starts.
- Deploy the edge retry/backoff for the next 6 hours to reduce surface 504s while we roll permanent changes.

What we'll deliver in 72 hours:
- Root-cause fixes (parallel artifact fetch + staged init) and autoscaler ramp improvements (RDW-4721).
- Postmortem with timings and regression tests.

Commercial/credits: We will...

to: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Exec note — Streamlytics latency incident and proposed customer remediation
messages.body:
Quick executive summary for leadership:

Incident: Streamlytics (large PLG customer, Q2 launch) experienced p95/p99 regressions correlated with autoscale cold-starts on Hosted GPU pools. Root cause: blocking model loader + KV init coupled with concurrent pod creation -> artifact store contention and transient CPU load. (See RDW-4721 for tech details.)

Impact: Visible tail latency increases (+800–1200ms) and elevated 5xx rates (~2.6%) for ~30–90m around burst scale events. Customer routed some traffic to backup region; this is material to their launch timeline.

Remediation we approved:
- Immediate: warmed-pool routing + min-replica increase for account; edge retry/backoff deployed.
- Med...

- Manifest & HMAC: yes — we support HMAC using a customer-provided key. Manifest will include per-file SHA256 and a top-level HMAC envelope.
- Timezones: all timestamps normalized to UTC (RFC3339), plus an o...

cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
messages.subject: CSA: transfer of control ownership + evidence automation proposal
messages.body:
Hi Julie —

Thanks again for walking through the CAIQ last week. Per our discussion I pulled together a lightweight proposal to: (1) reassign a small set of control owners to your org for the Helios assessment, and (2) prototype an automated evidence collection flow to reduce manual handoffs.

Attached is a first-pass control map (redwood_caiq_control_map_v0.2.xlsx) that shows our suggested owner changes and an \"evidence grade\" column indicating which items we think are automation candidates. The goal is to minimize back-and-forth for runtime logs and config artifacts.

Proposed next steps:
- You confirm which control owners you want to accept by EOD Thu (2 July).

messages.subject: Service provider classification and telemetry subprocessors — initial ask
messages.body:
Hi Irene,

Thanks for taking this. As part of HealthyLabs' assessment for a pilot, our legal team wants a concise write-up of how Redwood treats telemetry/operational logs and whether you consider Redwood a "service provider" under CCPA/CPRA for the telemetry data we would send during inference. Specific asks: 

- Confirm whether Redwood will be acting as a service provider (limited use) versus a controller/processor role for operational telemetry and aggregated analytics.
- List current subprocessors that process telemetry (including cloud regions they operate in). We need onshore fallback options for telemetry in case of regulatory restrictions.
- Provide a draft clause we can propose t...

messages.subject: Re: Service provider classification and telemetry subprocessors — initial ask
messages.body:
Amir,

Thanks for the clear asks — helpful. High level answers up front, with attachments and recommended clause below. Full details attached (dataflow matrix + draft US addendum).

Summary:
- Redwood treats customer inference inputs and associated invocation metadata as "service provider" data under CCPA/CPRA when we are processing on behalf of a customer and are bound by contractual limited-use restrictions. That classification applies to telemetry that is operational-only (latency, error rates, per-route usage) so long as the telemetry does not include customer PII that the customer has not authorized us to process.
- We maintain a list of subprocessors; for telemetry we primarily use ...

messages.subject: Re: Service provider classification and telemetry subprocessors — operational note
messages.body:
Irene — quick operational note to add into the reply if helpful:

- For customers who require onshore-only telemetry we recommend Dedicated telemetry lanes (isolated metrics and log collectors) paired with our US-only monitoring stack. That requires a short change window and a simple config flag at onboarding. The cost delta is small but predictable; we can include a short optional SOW line item.
- For retention tighter than 90 days we can enforce automated deletion and snapshot purging; for legal holds we will honor customer instructions via a documented process.

If you want I can draft a one-paragraph ops description to paste into the addendum.

-karthik
Director, Platform Operations

...

messages.subject: Re: Service provider classification and telemetry subprocessors — initial ask
messages.body:
Amir / Greenshore procurement team,

Following up with the materials and a compact answers bundle for your counsel and procurement. See attachments (same as before), and below a short FAQ that we find helpful for legal reviewers.

FAQ — short answers for counsel:
Q: Will Redwood act as a Service Provider for telemetry?
A: Yes — when telemetry is operational-only and processed strictly on behalf of the customer under contractual limited-use terms, Redwood will accept Service Provider classification for US privacy laws (CCPA/CPRA).

Q: What about telemetry that inadvertently contains customer PII?
A: If telemetry contains PII that the Customer has not authorized Redwood to process, we will ...

messages.subject: Post-call: BrightLane POC — proposed success metrics & next steps

Hi Emily — thanks again for the call earlier. Appreciate you walking us through the BrightLane onboarding flow and the main friction points. As promised, I’m sending a concise recap + a proposed evaluation playbook for the POC so we can align on ownership and timelines.

Summary of objective:
- Validate Redwood can meet BrightLane’s production requirements for the recommendation microservice used in the publisher flow (real-time content suggestions).

Proposed success criteria (draft):
- Latency: p95 inference latency <= 150 ms on the production-like test harness (95th percentile across user-shaped traffic).
- Throughput: sustain 500 QPS with 100 token average per request while keeping la...

messages.subject: Re: Post-call: BrightLane POC — proposed success metrics & next steps
messages.body:
Thanks Alex — this is super helpful and aligned with what the product team wants to validate. Quick responses inline: 

- Data export: we can provide a sanitized S3 bucket with event-level logs + ground truth labels by Apr 27. Will send the IAM stub today.
- Manual review: 200 items works. We’ll add an internal rubric so reviewers score relevance/false-positive rate.
- Latency target: 150 ms p95 is aggressive but within expectations if you can show consistent results under load.

A few asks from our side: 
1) Can Redwood include an embedding-based reranker variant in the trial? We want to compare response quality vs. the dense-only model.
2) Please provide a short questionnaire for securi...

messages.subject: Re: Post-call: BrightLane POC — proposed success metrics & next steps
messages.body:
Emily / Alex — I can own the model/infra rubric. A few technical notes and confirmations: 

- We will provision a dedicated test pool (n1-standard equivalent + 1x A10G) to mirror your production footprint. Initial config will be: 2 workers, batching window 8–12ms, token limit 256.
- Variant plan: dense-only baseline + embedding-reranker variant + a quantized 4-bit candidate for cost runs. We’ll measure token/sec, p95 latency, and token-level cost for each variant.
- Measurement details: we’ll run a 6-hour continuous soak at 500 QPS and then 2 x 30-min ramp tests at peak to capture tail behavior.
- Telemetry: we will provide per-request traces, p50/p95/p99 latency, token counts, and cost b...

messages.subject: Re: Post-call: BrightLane POC — proposed success metrics & next steps
messages.body:
Quick update: 
- Emily — received the IAM stub and S3 path, thanks. Laura and I will review the rubric and the manual-review criteria once you share.
- Sergio — appreciate the soak plan; can you post the resource estimate into the shared Drive folder (link below) so BrightLane can confirm budget?

Proposed kickoff: Tue Apr 28 at 10:00 PT for a 30-min alignment (data ingress, test harness endpoint, dashboard access). If that works I’ll send calendar invites. 

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3C_BrightLane-POC

Thanks — aiming to keep the timeline tight so BrightLane can validate within May.

Alex

messages.subject: Re: Post-call: BrightLane POC — proposed success metrics & next steps
messages.body:
Alex / Sergio — kickoff confirmed for Tue Apr 28 10:00 PT (calendar accepted). A couple of final confirmations before we start: 

- We accept the proposed success thresholds for the initial run. If Reed (our Director of Data Science) has follow-ups, we’ll surface them during the 30-min kickoff.
- For the embedding-reranker variant: please include an evaluation script so we can reproduce NDCG numbers locally.
- Our InfoSec reviewed the questionnaire and requested a short SOC2 evidence bundle; I’ll upload that to the Drive by EOD Mon.

Looking forward to the tests and thanks for the clear plan.

Best,
Emily

messages.subject: Sizing for Dedicated + peak demo week — NovaCorp
messages.body:
Hi Vivek,

Thanks again for the walkthrough today. To get procurement moving we need a rough Dedicated sizing / cost picture and how Redwood would handle short, high-intensity spikes during our product launch demo week (expected: 24-hour ramp to peak).

Estimated workload: ~200 concurrent inference sessions at peak, average 6k tokens per session, model is 70B (you mentioned a q4-optim profile). Traffic profile: baseline 40% of peak for steady hours, sudden ramp to peak up to 6x baseline for 48 hours around launch.

Questions:
- Can Dedicated support a transient burst pool so we don't have to over-commit reserved GPUs?
- How fast can autoscaling add capacity (minutes)?
- Any recommended co...

cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Sizing for Dedicated + peak demo week — NovaCorp
messages.body:
Alex, thanks for the details — helpful. Looping in Marissa (CS) and Karthik (solutions) for the rollout specifics and cost ops. Quick answers up front and a short ask list so we can give a concrete proposal: 

Prelim answers:
- Yes, Dedicated supports a transient burst pool (reserved but idle-able GPUs that can be added to your pool for short windows).
- Autoscale speed depends on instance type and provisioning mode; typical cold add for GPU nodes: ~3–8 mins for node provision + model coldstart. With pre-warmed images and cached KV we can get effective capacity in ~2–4 mins for common sequences.
- To avoid cold-start tail latency we recommend a small pre-warm runway (scheduled or event-tr...

messages.subject: Re: Sizing for Dedicated + peak demo week — NovaCorp
messages.body:
Quick internal notes on customer guarantees and risk mitigation before we send the formal quote. Quoting Vivek below.

- SLA / SLO: For a launch like this we should propose a temporary SLA extension that explicitly covers burst attachment latency and max transient queues. Procurement will want a guaranteed response (e.g., <200ms median token latency at peak, allowances for up to 5% p99 degradation during attach windows).
- Canary + Rollout: We'll recommend a phased canary during the ramp: 10% of traffic to the new Dedicated group for the first 2 hours, then 50%, then full. This reduces single-point failure risk for the launch.
- Fallback policy: Auto-fallback to hosted Redwood models in o...

messages.subject: Re: Sizing for Dedicated + peak demo week — NovaCorp
messages.body:
Marissa / team — technical details to include in the proposal and the config we recommend for the Dedicated fleet: 

Autoscale & burst config (recommendation):
- Metrics: request_concurrency (per-model) + queue_depth + 1-min token throughput. Use a composite signal where concurrency AND queue depth must exceed thresholds to trigger burst attachment (reduces noisy scaling).
- Scale step: 4 GPUs. Max attach rate: 10 GPUs/min (backed by infra tests).
- Cooldown: 300s (5m) between scale actions to avoid oscillation.
- Pre-warm: scheduled pre-warm window 30–60 minutes before expected ramp (can be event-triggered via webhook), start with 8 GPUs pre-warmed (hot-image + cached KV).
- Burst pool p...

cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Proposal: Dedicated baseline + transient burst pool (NovaCorp)
messages.body:
Alex — attaching a short proposal and the AMP so Procurement can proceed. Highlights below and links at the bottom.

Summary offer:
- Baseline reserved: 40 A100-equiv GPUs (handles steady-state).
- Transient burst pool: 20 A100-equiv GPUs, auto-attach under the policy below, 48-hr default burst duration.
- Autoscale policy: composite trigger (concurrency + queue depth), step 4 GPUs, max attach 10 GPUs/min, cooldown 300s. Pre-warm runway: 30–60 mins, recommend scheduling a 1-hr pre-warm before your launch window.
- SLA exception: temporary addendum to cover burst attach windows and a 5% p99 degradation allowance; we'll prepare language for procurement.

Estimated cost (very rough): 48-hr b...

messages.subject: Re: Proposal: Dedicated baseline + transient burst pool (NovaCorp)
messages.body:
Vivek / Team — thanks, this is exactly what Procurement needed.

We confirm we will run the 70B q4-opt variant (quantized) and will upload the 1-week traffic CSV into the drive link by EOD today. Procurement wants the DocuSign link and a single cost line they can approve; happy to take the SLA addendum as a separate DocuSign (Priya copied).

Please go ahead and open the AMP and send DocuSign. We also accept the recommended pre-warm window (schedule to start 1 hour before the ramp).

We'll book the technical run-through for Monday 10am PT — invite sent.

Alex
Director of ML Platforms | NovaCorp

- The latest SOC 2 Type II report (redacted is fine)

messages.body:
Hi Maya / Carl —

Great conversation today — appreciate you walking us through the Clearpath summarization use case and the sample transcripts. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned: 

1) What we heard (short):
- Primary need: accurate, redaction-safe legal summarization for client transcripts and mixed doc types (emails, meeting notes, exhibits).
- Priority edge cases: PII/PHI spread across documents, inline legal citations, OCR artifacts, and very long nested threads.
- Success metrics: 95% redaction recall for PII; summary factuality within 2% error rate on our benchmark set.

2) Recommended evaluation approach (initial):
- Model shortlist: rw-instruct-large-quant (fas...

cc: Carl Huang <carl.h@clearpath.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Recap & Action: Prompt edge-cases collection for legal summarization pilot
messages.body:
Thanks Alex — this is perfect and captures everything we discussed. Quick clarifications from our side before we upload: 

- We will provide three categories of examples: (A) redacted real transcripts (we’ll replace names with <NAME>), (B) synthetic examples that reproduce tricky inline citations, and (C) OCR-noise samples (scanned exhibits).
- Redaction policy: we must remove social security numbers and full client IDs, but partial case numbers (first 4 digits) can remain. I attached our short internal note (redaction_guidance.pdf) — it lists fields that must be redacted vs. fields that can be preserved for context.
- Edge cases to highlight in the CSV: nested quoted emails inside transc...

messages.subject: Re: Recap & Action: Prompt edge-cases collection for legal summarization pilot
messages.body:
Hi Maya — thanks for the clarifications and for committing to the upload. Two quick notes on the technical side so you can share with your security/compliance folks: 

- KV caching/logging: for the evaluation we will run on a dedicated, ephemeral evaluation pool; masked tokens in uploaded artifacts will not be stored in persistent caches. We will also enable request-level redaction to strip fields marked as PII per your guidance. I’ll have Karthik send a short technical note with config flags.
- Recommended model/ops baseline: we suggest running a baseline comparison between redwood-phi-1 (safety-first) and rw-instruct-large-quant (cost baseline), with our standard safety filters enabled....

messages.subject: Re: Recap & Action: Prompt edge-cases collection for legal summarization pilot
messages.body:
All set on our side — we uploaded clearpath_prompt_upload_v1.zip to the Drive folder (https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Aq-example-secure-upload). Alex, please confirm once you pick up the files; we also added a short inventory CSV that maps sample IDs to case types.

Procurement note: procurement is targeting signoff the week of 1/27; if Redwood needs an entitlements form for the ephemeral eval pool we can get that to you.

Thanks,
Carl
VP, Integrations | Clearpath Analytics
carl.h@clearpath.ai | 650-555-2103

messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

We're starting the evidence collection for Northbridge's SOC 2 testing window (control family: CC6 / System Operations — retention & disposal). For scope we need: three example tenant deletion flows (one EU tenant explicitly), erasure logs showing deletion initiation and completion, retention configuration (TTL/archival rules), and any playbooks that describe how data deletion is orchestrated. If you can provide:

- a short narrative of the tenant deletion choreography (high level)
- scrubbed traces/logs for 3 sample tenants (timestamps + job ids are fine)
- the TTL/retention table or policy and the query you use to identify eligible objects for erasure
- the latest \"erasure ...

messages.subject: Re: Request: SOC 2 — evidence for retention + deletion controls
messages.body:
Hi Elaine — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Marissa (platform lead) and Monica (security/compliance) so we can assemble the package.

Quick clarifying Qs before we prepare:
1) For the EU tenant, do you need residency mapping evidence (region tags + storage location), or is the deletion trace sufficient?
2) For logs, redaction is fine — would a zip of CSV traces with UUIDs and timestamps meet audit needs?

We're targeting a consolidated packet by Friday EOD. We'll include a short narrative and an \"erasure matrix\" mapping policy → implementation (see attached draft).

Best,
Rachel Kim
Senior Manager, Security Programs
Redwood Inference
rachel.kim@redwood-inference.com
[Attachment: r...

messages.subject: Re: Request: SOC 2 — evidence for retention + deletion controls
messages.body:
Quick update — I pulled candidate traces for three tenants (tenant_ids: tn-eu-003, tn-us-112, tn-apac-047). The artifacts I can produce:

- tenant-deletion-traces-2024Q4.zip (contains CSV rows: event_time, tenant_id, operation, job_id, status)
- erasure-playbook-v2.pdf (internal runbook describing steps, notifications, and verification checks)
- cache-ttl-query-snippet.txt (the SQL used to surface objects eligible for deletion in the blob store)

Notes/constraints: the traces include internal job IDs and service node identifiers — we'll scrub node hostnames and redact IPs. Legal asked that tenant names be pseudonymized; logs will include tenant_id values only. Is that acceptable?

Marissa...

messages.subject: Re: Request: SOC 2 — evidence for retention + deletion controls
messages.body:
Agree with the plan. I'll review the artifacts for any PII before release and run the legal checklist. Two action items for me:
1) confirm the EU residency mapping is present in the metadata export (we keep region tag + storage cluster id),
2) sign an internal attestation confirming that the shared traces are redacted per the contract.

If both are green, I will provide the signed attestation as a short PDF. ETA: Thu EOD my side.

Monica Patel
Compliance Manager
[No attachments in this message]

messages.subject: Evidence package: retention / deletion artifacts (Northbridge SOC 2)
messages.body:
Hi Elaine,

Attached is the evidence package for the items you requested:
- tenant-deletion-traces-2024Q4.zip (CSV traces for 3 sample tenants; redacted per legal)
- erasure-playbook-v2.pdf (operational runbook describing deletion choreography)
- redwood_deletion_matrix.csv (policy → implementation mapping)
- cache-ttl-query-snippet.txt (SQL snippet used to identify TTL-eligible objects)
- attestation-monice-patel-signed.pdf (internal redaction/attestation)

I've also shared a Drive folder with read-only access: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c4d-deletion-evidence. The HubSpot record for this request is HS-Record-839201 if you need tracking.

Let me know if you need raw logs ...

messages.subject: Re: Evidence package: retention / deletion artifacts (Northbridge SOC 2)
messages.body:
Rachel — received, thanks. The package looks good on first pass. A small follow-up: can you confirm the TTL for ephemeral inference caches (the playbook references them but the CSV maps only long-term blob retention)? The auditor team needs the exact process/query used to identify cache entries for eviction so we can verify scope separation. If you can either point us to the query (or paste it) or provide a short narrative, we'll close this control.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Elaine

messages.subject: Re: Evidence package: retention / deletion artifacts (Northbridge SOC 2)
messages.body:
Hi Elaine — thanks for the note. The cache eviction query is included in cache-ttl-query-snippet.txt (attached). For clarity, here's the snippet we use internally:

-- SELECT keys FROM cache_meta WHERE last_access < now() - interval 'TTL' AND tenant_region = 'eu'
-- join to blob_index to ensure we do not evict pinned model artifacts

We also run a two-step verification: (1) dry-run listing with a non-destructive flag, and (2) a scheduled job that performs the actual deletion and emits completion events to the audit log. Marissa can share the job IDs for the examples if you need them.

If that satisfies the question, we can mark this control as closed.

Best,
Rachel

- Versio...

messages.subject: VPC upgrade curation & failsafe brief
messages.body:
Team / InfraOps,

I'm sharing a compact brief for the upcoming Private deployment patch wave (v2026.06.*). Goal: get alignment on the curation rules, version-pinning policy, maintenance windows, and the concrete rollback triggers we will hand to AcmeCloud ops. This is intentionally short — details are in the attached runbook. Please scan and call out any missing failure modes by EOD tomorrow.

Key points:
- Version curation: we will pin customer fleets to the nearest tested minor (e.g., 1.14.x) and allow a single hotpatch channel (1.14.1x) for critical fixes only. No automatic bumping across minor versions during the window.
- Maintenance window: target UTC 02:00-06:00 (US-West preferred)...

to: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, infraops@acmecloud.com
messages.subject: Re: VPC upgrade curation & failsafe brief
messages.body:
Thanks Alex — good concise brief. A few technical clarifications and one small ask:

1) NVMe/driver gating: I agree we should avoid firmware bumps during the slice unless it's emergency-only. If Acme insists, we must run a hardware compatibility sweep on the canary hosts first. I'll add a short script to the runbook to assert expected firmware versions and fail the stage if mismatch > 5% of hosts.

2) KV cache checkpointing: we need to snapshot and push KV cache checkpoints to object storage prior to node reboots for quick warm starts. I've added a step to the runbook (see upgrade_runbook_v2.pdf, section 3.2). If checkpoint push fails, the node should be marked for manual review and the s...

to: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, infraops@acmecloud.com
messages.subject: Re: VPC upgrade curation & failsafe brief
messages.body:
Quick check from telemetry side — I've run the proposed thresholds against the last 30 days for Acme's cluster. Summary attached (canary-slice-matrix.csv). Highlights:
- Baseline p99: 180ms median, spikes to ~310ms during their nightly backups (00:30-01:15 UTC). We should shift our canary window to start after their backup or use the adaptive baseline Karthik mentioned.
- KV hit ratio: healthy at 88-92% normally; slices that cause warm restarts drop to ~65% for ~6–8 minutes. Pin policy must account for that transient expense.
- Error-rate: steady under 0.6% except for one incident (third-party auth flake) that spiked to 2.4% for 4 minutes last month. This would have tripped the proposed 3...

to: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, infraops@acmecloud.com
messages.subject: Re: VPC upgrade curation & failsafe brief
messages.body:
Thanks everyone — this looks actionable. Two quick notes from the GTM/SLA side and final confirmation:

1) Customer comms: I'll draft a short advisory that calls out the scheduled window (02:30-06:30 UTC), the canary stages, and the expected transient latency and cache warm-up behaviors. We will offer a 4-hour credit if we exceed the stated SLOs for more than 30 consecutive minutes.

2) SLA exception: approved for this wave to allow hotpatch channel usage for critical fixes only. We will record the agreement in the customer portal and add a note to the incident runbook in case of escalations.

3) Final confirmation request: InfraOps, please confirm the 02:30-06:30 UTC window and your abil...

to: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: VPC upgrade curation & failsafe brief
messages.body:
Laura / team,

We can confirm the window and the two-step net quiesce (TCP drain + LB flip). Our ops lead will run a preflight on Monday to validate firmware compatibility and will push the results into JIRA INFRA-2248. We will also make the 5-minute per-node quiesce available as requested; if we see more than 4% host firmware mismatch we'll escalate before the canary.

- InfraOps (AcmeCloud)

messages.subject: Re: Request: onboarding & PO waiver for pilot
messages.body:
Rachel / Marissa —

Thanks for the heads up, Rachel. Short answer: our standard vendor onboarding requires a Purchase Order for any production invoicing. That said, we do have a limited temporary exception process for low-risk pilot engagements. Before I can approve an exception I need the following: 

1) Formal business justification (concise) explaining why a PO will materially delay the pilot (one paragraph is fine).
2) Confirmation that KiteWorks will sign Redwood's DPA and will use SSO for any admin console access.
3) Completed security questionnaire and any supporting artifacts (pen test summary, SOC report if available).
4) A capped spend threshold for the pilot (we typically limit...

to: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Samir Patel <samir@kiteworks.com>
messages.subject: Re: Request: onboarding & PO waiver for pilot
messages.body:
Markus — thanks for outlining the checklist. Quick responses from KiteWorks / Sales side so Procurement can triage fast: 

- Business justification: this is a technical validation tied to an active POC with a potential $120k ARR expansion if successful; delaying by routing a PO will push schedule and jeopardize the calendar-based integration slot.
- DPA & SSO: KiteWorks confirmed they will sign Redwood's DPA and support SSO provisioning.
- Security artifacts: Samir has attached the questionnaire and indicated a recent third-party pen test summary is available on request.
- Spend cap: pilot estimate is $4k total for 30 days. We request the PO-waiver for up to $5k to provide a small buffer....

messages.subject: Re: Request: onboarding & PO waiver for pilot
messages.body:
Team / Samir —

Finance can approve a time-limited PO-waiver for the 30-day pilot under the following controls:

Our security team needs a short write-up confirming two things before we proceed: 1) that Redwood does not use our tenant data to train any public or internal foundation models; and 2) exactly how long debug snapshots, backups, and cross-region replicas are retained after a tenant requests deletion.

Specifically: if FinHealth decides to stop using the service and requests fu...

Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>: Samir —

Great questions. Short answer: we do not use customer tenant content to train our public foundation models. For the retention bullets you listed I want to loop in Mina (privacy/ops) to give exact windows and the deletion runbook steps.

I'll follow up with a one-page attestation plus a short runbook snippet that you can include in procurement. Expect a doc and a summary in the next 48 hours....

Alex —

Thanks for the demo yesterday. Our security team needs a short write-up confirming two things before we proceed: 1) that Redwood does not use our tenant data to train any public or internal foundation models; and 2) exactly how long debug snapshots, backups, and cross-region replicas are retained after a tenant requests deletion.

Specifically: if FinHealth decides to stop using the service and requests full deletion, what happens to:
- KV caches/prefix caches used for inference performance;
- ephemeral debug snapshots captured for incident triage;
- backups and cross-region replicas (are they tombstoned immediately, or retained for a window?), and
- any telemetry that might be ag...

Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

messages.subject: RE: Quick question on ‘no training’ claim + backups/replicas
messages.body:
Hi Samir —

Thanks for the clear list. I outlined the operational behavior below; I'll attach the shorter attestation you requested and a matrix that maps each artifact to retention/erase behavior. If you want an even shorter single-sentence attestation for procurement, we can provide that as well.

Key points (operational detail):
- Training usage: Redwood does not feed tenant content into any general-purpose training pipeline for public or internal foundation models. We may use non-identifying, aggregated telemetry (e.g., request volume, error rates, latency histograms) to improve platform infrastructure; customers can opt out of telemetry aggregation per contract.

- KV cache / prefix ...

to: Samir Chaudhry <samir@finhealth.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
messages.subject: RE: Quick question on ‘no training’ claim + backups/replicas
messages.body:
Samir / team —

Two legal clarifications to help procurement: 1) we can commit contractually that \"Redwood will not use Customer Content to train foundation models,\" and 2) retention windows (snapshots/backups) are operationally described as Mina noted. If FinHealth needs expedited purge (e.g., reduction from 30 days to 0 days), we require the SOW addendum and acknowledgement of increased operational risk for debugging/recovery.

I'll drop suggested redline language for the Master Services Agreement and a short addendum for the retention carveout into the HubSpot record. Alex — can you confirm the procurement contact so we route the DocuSign correctly?

—Markus
Markus Klein
VP Legal, Re...

cc: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
messages.subject: RE: Quick question on ‘no training’ claim + backups/replicas
messages.body:
Samir —

Attaching the files Mina mentioned: 1) redwood-nontraining-attestation.pdf (one-paragraph attestation plus signature), 2) data-retention-matrix.xlsx (artifact-by-artifact mapping and purge windows), and 3) delete-runbook-summary.docx (high-level steps, SLAs for purge actions, and contact for legal hold requests).

Next steps:
- If procurement is happy with standard 30-day snapshot/backups purge window we will add the attestation and return the signed MSA with standard retention language.
- If you need 0-day purge we will prepare an addendum and updated SOW; that typically takes 2–3 business days to approve.

Let me know if you want a 1:1 call with Mina to walk through the runbook...

cc: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>

Samir

title: Production API 429s and latency spikes — Elantra (urgent)
body:
Alex Johnson <alex.j@elantra.com>: Rachel —

We're seeing elevated 429s and latency spikes against the Redwood inference endpoints for our production traffic since ~03:30 UTC this morning. Error rates spiked to ~15% (429s) on the /v1/generate and /v1/chat routes and latency p95 jumped from ~90ms to ~1.4s. This is impacting our real-time product and causing downstream timeouts in our orchestration layer.

Attached are a sampling of request logs and ...

Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>: Alex —

Thanks for the heads up and the logs — I'm on this immediately and have looped in infra and CS. Initial ask: would you permit us to apply a temporary soft-limit increase for your tenant (one hour) so we can absorb the burst while we investigate? We can also enable a fallback route to a slightly lower-cost model to preserve throughput with marginal quality tradeoff.

Team, I'm escalating — ple...

messages.subject: Fwd: Production API 429s and latency spikes — Elantra (urgent)
messages.body:
Forwarding Elantra's escalation. Proposed immediate actions (please confirm and execute):
1) Apply a temporary soft-limit uplift for tenant ELAN-4721: +2x token allowance for 60 minutes (to buy time).
2) Enable fallback routing to model variant rwd-embed-lite (reduced quality, preserved throughput) for read-heavy routes.
3) Infra: run quota bucket reindex and surface per-route token-bucket metrics via Console.
4) Sales/Finance (Marissa): we should prepare a goodwill credit (suggest 10% of May usage). Please advise if exec sign-off needed.

Karthik — need a short RCA and deployable mitigations; if this is policy/config drift we'll need a rollback playbook. Monica — please own customer comm...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Production API 429s and latency spikes — Elantra (urgent)
messages.body:
Quick technical summary (initial triage):
- Root cause (likely): overlapping quota policies + an incorrectly set burst window for the tenant. We see two active quota rules affecting ELAN-4721: a route-level token bucket and a tenant-level bucket. Both are configured to draw from the same backing credits without proper harmonization.
- Misconfiguration detail: tenant-level burst_window_seconds is set to 60s (should be 3600s for this account profile). That short burst window causes the token bucket to exhaust quickly under sustained bursts; when combined with route-level enforcement we effectively double-throttle concurrent requests.
- Symptom match: requests process initial tokens (one or ...

messages.subject: Re: Production API 429s and latency spikes — Elantra (incident update)
messages.body:
Alex —

Update and next steps: thank you for the logs, they made triage much faster. In short: we identified a policy configuration drift where a tenant-level burst window was set to 60s (too short for your traffic profile) and combined with a route-level enforcement this produced double-throttling behavior during the promotional spike.

What we did (completed):
- Applied a temporary soft-limit uplift (2x) while we validated the fix (60 minutes window enacted at 08:36 PDT).
- Hotfixed tenant burst_window_seconds 60 -> 3600 and reindexed buckets (rolling, <90s transient).
- Enabled fallback routing to rwd-embed-lite to preserve throughput for read-heavy flows (shifted ~22% of traffic).

Im...

We're on the Lythia private deployment (cluster: lythia-prod-vpc-3). Attached: trace log and a small snapsh...

- Short-term mitigations: enable per-route burst cap...

Hi Rachel / Redwood,

We just observed an abrupt QPS spike from our ingestion fleet starting ~07:50 PDT that has driven p95 latency from ~180ms to ~1.5s and 5xx rate up to ~7% on calls routed to our private VPC deployment. This is user-facing for a significant portion of sessions (estimated 18% of traffic).

We're on the Lythia private deployment (cluster: lythia-prod-vpc-3). Attached: trace log and a small snapshot of request headers. messages.subject: Re: Lythia VPC — sudden QPS surge, 5xx increase
messages.body:
Alex — thanks for the signal and for the artifacts. We've triaged the first pass and will treat as an urgent operational incident. Immediate plan (next 45 minutes):

- Triage: our on-call will pull a full KV/cache+accept-queue snapshot and CPU/GPU utilization for the Lythia cluster. Requesting you keep the trace attached alive for 2 hours.
- Short-term mitigations: enable per-route burst capacity (temporary) and flip traffic for the heaviest route to a quantized variant (m1-small-8q) to reduce per-token time.

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Lythia VPC — initial analysis and quick fixes
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel — quick summary from the runtime side after a first-pass on the trace and the limited metrics Alex sent:

- Symptom set: sudden spike to ~9k concurrent request attempts across the cluster, high small-payload concurrency (many < 60 token requests), accept-queue depth hit configured max, KV cache churn increased leading to higher cache miss rates and extra token decoding work.
- Immediate cause hypothesis: request shape changed (parallel small requests) exposing request scheduling and worker-thread contention in our VPC runtime path. This manifests as higher syscall/backpressure and transient 502/504s as accept queue overflows.

Immediate actions taken (done or in-progress):
1...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Alex Turner <alex.turner@lythiahealth.com>, Sophie Clarke <sophie.clarke@lythiahealth.com>
messages.subject: Re: Lythia VPC — status update and exec note
messages.body:
Quick status update and exec-friendly summary for Sophie's call:

- Timeline: incident detected 07:50 PT, mitigation toggles applied by 08:05-08:30 PT, measurable recovery by ~08:48 PT.
- Impact: ~18% of live sessions affected at peak; p95 improved from ~1.5s -> ~240-300ms; 5xx reduced from 7% -> ~0.6%.
- Root-cause (working hypothesis): traffic-shape change from partner ingestion producing many small concurrent requests exposing scheduler/accept-queue limits in the private runtime; not a model regression.

Actions taken (completed):
- Raised per-node concurrency cap temporarily.
- Enabled per-route burst and routed heavy paths to quantized model variants to reduce per-token compute.
- Pu...

title: Demo messaging: why Redwood (production economics) + which benchmark charts are OK to show
body:
Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>: Team — I’m tightening the “Why Redwood” / “Production economics” section of the demo deck + talk track.

Two asks:
1) Messaging: what’s the cleanest wording that doesn’t sound like generic “optimization,” but still lands with buyers? (I’m trying to avoid ‘we make it cheaper’ as the headline.)
2) Charts: which benchmark visuals are safe for *live* demos + which should be appendix-only / internal-only?

Context: AEs...

Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>: On messaging:

Recommend framing as “predictable, controllable inference economics” rather than “optimization.” The buyer pain is:
- unit cost volatility
- latency regressions during scale
- lack of visibility into why cost changed

Proposed headline (slide + spoken):
“Production economics: the controls + visibility to hit latency SLOs at the lowest unit cost—without guesswork...

messages.subject: Demo messaging: why Redwood (production economics) + which benchmark charts are OK to show
messages.body:
Team — I’m tightening the “Why Redwood” / “Production economics” section of the demo deck + talk track.

Two asks:
1) Messaging: what’s the cleanest wording that doesn’t sound like generic “optimization,” but still lands with buyers? (I’m trying to avoid ‘we make it cheaper’ as the headline.)
2) Charts: which benchmark visuals are safe for *live* demos + which should be appendix-only / internal-only?

Context: AEs keep asking for 1–2 crisp charts to justify the knobs we show (batching/caching, fallback policies, token-level cost breakdown). But we’ve been inconsistent and sometimes someone pulls a chart with competitor names or internal cost inputs.

Attached is:
- v2 slide copy draft
- P...

to: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo messaging: why Redwood (production economics) + which benchmark charts are OK to show
messages.body:
On messaging:

Recommend framing as “predictable, controllable inference economics” rather than “optimization.” The buyer pain is:
- unit cost volatility
- latency regressions during scale
- lack of visibility into why cost changed

Proposed headline (slide + spoken):
“Production economics: the controls + visibility to hit latency SLOs at the lowest unit cost—without guesswork.”

Talk track structure that’s been working:
1) “Cost and latency aren’t independent in production.”
2) “Most teams discover this late (after they’ve shipped).”
3) “Redwood gives you explicit levers and proves the impact at token-level granularity.”

On charts (high-level):
GREEN (OK to show live):
- Relative improv...

to: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo messaging: why Redwood (production economics) + which benchmark charts are OK to show
messages.body:
+1 to Soojin’s buckets.

Specific notes on the attached PDF (page numbers based on the file you sent):

GREEN
- p3 “Batching on/off: req/s vs p95 latency”: safe IF we remove the bottom note referencing A100 vs H100 cost delta (that note tends to trigger follow-up questions we don’t want).
- p5 “Cache hit rate vs effective $/output token”: safe; is fully synthetic and matches the demo tenant knobs. Please add footnote: “Synthetic traffic generator, seq lens distribution 30% short / 70% medium.”
- p7 “Cost breakdown waterfall (prompt/output/cache)” : safe if data is from demo tenant project only (seeded). Also: make sure the y-axis is ‘relative units’ not dollars.

YELLOW
- p9 “Throughput v...

to: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo messaging: why Redwood (production economics) + which benchmark charts are OK to show
messages.body:
DevEx angle: whatever we show needs to line up with what an engineer can reproduce in 5 minutes.

So I’d strongly prefer GREEN charts that map to:
- “toggle batching” (route setting)
- “toggle caching” (prefix/KV cache setting)
- “view token-level breakdown in Console”

If you keep the narrative as “controls + observability,” I can provide a 10-line code snippet that prints:
- request latency
- output tokens
- cache hit indicator

Also +1 on avoiding absolute $ claims live. People will ask “is that list price or effective?” and it becomes a pricing call.

If helpful I can add a callout/footnote: “Shown on demo tenant synthetic workload. Use Redwood Optimize recommendations + your prompt d...

to: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo messaging: why Redwood (production economics) + which benchmark charts are OK to show
messages.body:
Super helpful — thank you.

I’ll implement:

Messaging (updated slide headline):
“Production economics: explicit controls + token-level visibility to hit SLOs at the lowest unit cost.”

Subhead (spoken):
“Not a black box. You can see *why* cost changed and choose the tradeoff you want.”

Chart decisions for live demo:
LIVE (GREEN)
- Batching on/off: req/s vs p95 latency (remove A100/H100 cost note)
- Cache hit rate vs relative unit cost (add synthetic workload footnote)
- Token breakdown waterfall (relative units, demo tenant only)

Appendix-only (YELLOW)
- Throughput vs latency curve (only with pinned route/version labels)
- Indexed $/1M tokens (baseline=1.0; no absolute pricing)

Cut (R...

cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo messaging: why Redwood (production economics) + which benchmark charts are OK to show
messages.body:
Approved from Sales side.

Two small tweaks from what I’m hearing in the field:
- When you say “lowest unit cost,” some prospects immediately go “so you’re cheaper than X?” and we get dragged into competitor pricing.
 Suggest: “predictable unit cost” or “better unit economics” (keeps it about control + ROI).
- Please keep the live charts to 1–2 max in a 30-min demo. If we show three, we end up short on time for the reliability/fallback story.

Otherwise the buckets look right. Let’s lock this and standardize.

Stephanie
Sales Exec Sponsor (NA)
Redwood Inference
