body:
Tom Alvarez <security@medilink.com>: Rachel,

Thanks for taking this. We're preparing the security intake for the Medilink pilot and need a concise package that confirms Redwood's EU-only hosting option and VPC/private deployment controls. Specifically can you provide:

- A brief statement confirming that customer compute and persistent storage for the EU offering are restricted to EU region nodes (no cross-border replication by default).
- CAIQ snap...

Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>: Tom — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Marissa (Customer Ops) for the CAIQ and SOC2 excerpts, and Ben (Infra) for the peering diagram and operator manifest.

Short answer up front: yes, the EU \"privatelink/dedicated\" deployment path keeps customer data processing and persistent storage within EU region nodes by default; we do not replicate to non-EU regions unless a customer explicitly reque...

- CAIQ snapshot (answered) or equivalent SIG questionnaire sections for Data Location & Network Controls.

- A VPC peering/privatelink diagram showing egress paths and keystore custody (...

messages.subject: Re: Residency & VPC evidence request for Medilink pilot
messages.body:
Tom — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Marissa (Customer Ops) for the CAIQ and SOC2 excerpts, and Ben (Infra) for the peering diagram and operator manifest.

Short answer up front: yes, the EU \"privatelink/dedicated\" deployment path keeps customer data processing and persistent storage within EU region nodes by default; we do not replicate to non-EU regions unless a customer explicitly requests a cross-region failover. I'll let Marissa/Ben add the specific artifacts and any legal/contractual language.

Rachel Kim
Head of Customer Security Programs
Redwood Inference
rachel_kim@redwood.com

messages.subject: Re: Residency & VPC evidence request for Medilink pilot
messages.body:
Hi Tom — thanks, good checklist. Attached and summarized below:

Attachments (attached to this thread as stubs):
- redwood_caiq_snapshot_2026.pdf (answered CAIQ sections 1.2/3.4 Data Location; 5.1 Network Controls)
- soc2_excerpts_network_controls.pdf (SOC 2 report excerpts: network segregation + operator access mgmt + retention schedule)
- vpc_peering_diagram_eu.png (logical diagram w/privatelink endpoints, peering, and egress path annotations)
- operator_manifest_apac-eu.csv (redacted roster: role, region assignment, operator group id)

Notes:
- Data residency: Our EU dedicated/private control plane and compute nodes are deployed in EU cloud regions (Frankfurt, Amsterdam). By default, t...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Tom Alvarez <security@medilink.com>
messages.subject: Re: Residency & VPC evidence request for Medilink pilot
messages.body:
Forwarding technical notes and quick checklist for Medilink's infra/security team — see inline.

--- Forwarded message ---

messages.subject: Residency & VPC evidence request: EU hosting + CAIQ excerpts
messages.body:
Marissa / Rachel — attaching the peering diagram. Key points for Medilink security review:
1) Egress paths: Privatelink endpoints terminate in EU-only subnets. Outbound telemetry (health metrics) is routed to our EU telemetry cluster; agent-level telemetry that contains operational metadata is scrubbed of tenant identifiers unless flagged by a customer for debug.
2) Keystore custody: Customer-managed keys (KMS/HSM) are supported — we can show a diagram with the KMS partitioning. For Redwood-managed keystores, operator access is split by operator group id; the manifest shows grouping (no single operator has global keystore modify rights).
3) Operator access controls: We enforce JIT (just-i...

to: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Residency & VPC evidence request for Medilink pilot
messages.body:
Thanks all — this looks good. Two quick asks: 1) please send the DocuSign residency attestation (we need a signed copy for procurement), and 2) can you confirm in writing that operator access to EU tenant keystores requires a two-person approval (i.e., two distinct operator group ids) for key export/rotation? That second item is a gating control for our security architecture team.

If you can get the attestation link over and a one-paragraph confirmation on the two-person approval model, we can move the intake forward.

Best,
Tom
-- End of thread --

messages.subject: DPA redlines and a few clarifying Qs
messages.body:
Hi Monica,

Thanks for the demo last week — ProspectCorp has a lot of interest in Redwood’s latency profile. Our legal team attached a marked-up DPA and a short list of clarifying questions we need answered before we can sign. High-level items:

- Roles: We want explicit controller/processor language. Please confirm Redwood’s stance — will Redwood be a processor for API requests?
- Telemetry/logs: We need to understand what telemetry/diagnostic logs you retain, for how long, and whether they contain customer content.
- Embeddings: Are embeddings derived from customer text treated as customer data (subject to deletion/portability) or as Redwood-generated outputs?
- Subprocessors: Please pr...

messages.subject: Re: DPA redlines and a few clarifying Qs
messages.body:
Hi Sophie — thanks for sending this over and for the clear list. Looping in Rachel (legal) and Aditya (AE) so we can get you concrete answers and a proposed redline back quickly.

Quick answers up front:
- Controller/Processor: Redwood operates as a Processor for customer-supplied content processed through the API; customers are Controllers for their own data. We’ll have Rachel confirm the exact contract language that maps to that.
- DocuSign: yes, we prefer DocuSign for execution; we’ll share a signing link once the redline is agreed.

Rachel — can you take the lead on review and provide any alternate language for the processor/controller clauses? Aditya — happy to sync if ProspectCorp w...

messages.subject: RE: DPA redlines and a few clarifying Qs
messages.body:
Hi Sophie and team — I reviewed ProspectCorp’s redline. I’ve attached a one-page summary (Redline_Summary_v1.pdf) that addresses the high-priority items and proposes language we believe is consistent with Redwood’s product and controls. Key points below, with proposed contract text in the attachment:

1) Roles and definitions- We agree Redwood will be the Processor and ProspectCorp the Controller for customer-provided content processed via the API. Proposed clause: "Redwood acts as a Processor that processes Customer Data on behalf of the Customer, as directed by the Customer." (see attachment for exact redline).

2) Telemetry and diagnostic logs- We collect operational telemetry (request...

messages.subject: RE: DPA redlines and a few clarifying Qs
messages.body:
Rachel / Monica — quick engineering confirmation on the points you asked about. Short version below, happy to provide more detail or a SOW appendix with data flow diagrams.

1) Telemetry/logs- We collect: request metadata (timestamp, request size, model id, latency), error traces (stack/error ids), and infrastructure-level logs (node health). We do not persist raw customer inputs or outputs beyond the inference window unless the customer opts into logging for debugging. Retention: short-term request traces retained for 30 days, aggregated metrics for up to 2 years.

2) Embeddings- Embeddings generated via the embeddings endpoint are stored only if the customer calls the Redwood "persist/ ...

cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Final proposed DPA language + DocuSign link
messages.body:
Hi Sophie — thanks for the patience while we pulled in legal and engineering. Attached is a redline summary that reflects the changes Rachel proposed and the technical confirmations from Laura. Highlights:

- Roles: Redwood confirmed as Processor; we accept language that makes the Controller/Processor mapping explicit (see attached redline).
- Telemetry: We propose the following contract text: "Redwood may collect operational telemetry (request metadata, error traces) for service operation and security. Redwood does not persist Customer Content beyond transient inference buffers unless Customer enables logging; operational logs retained up to 30 days, aggregated metrics up to 2 years."
- ...

- Whether Redwood processes "personal information" of children under 13 when a customer (controller) uses the hosted API (clarify processor vs controller responsibilities).
- Confirmation of platform controls that support age-gating / assent flows (KV cache retention, prefix masking, logs retention).
- Any built-in facilities for verifiable parental consent or specific guidance you provide to c...

messages.subject: Fwd: Request: COPPA & children's data attestation
messages.body:
Team — forwarding the KidsApp request (attached). Sales asked for a concise attestation they can include in the security review packet. Key asks are around COPPA/age-gating, prohibited uses, and confirmation on training/data-retention for customers that opt out.

Neha: can you own coordination with the customer? Marissa: can you draft an attestation with the compliance language we already use (SOC 2 / processor statements)? Rachel: please review for legal nuance — we should make clear the boundary between Redwood (processor) and customer (controller).

I think a 2-3 sentence attestation + a short FAQ bullet list will close this. Target to Helen: EOD Mon (14th).

Thanks,
Karthik
Account Ex...

to: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.inference>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.inference>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Request: COPPA & children's data attestation
messages.body:
Karthik / Neha / Rachel —

Draft attestation below (first pass). I pulled language from our standard processor statement and SOC 2 report summary. Please flag any legal redlines.

DRAFT ATTESTATION (for review)
Redwood Inference provides an inference platform and operates as a data processor on behalf of its customers. Redwood does not knowingly direct customers' data collection practices. Redwood does not use customer prompt or result data to retrain models when a customer has opted out via contractually agreed DPA terms. Our hosting and runtime controls are designed to support customers implementing age-gating and parental consent flows: configurable KV-cache retention, request-scoped e...

to: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.inference>
messages.subject: Re: DRAFT ATTESTATION / DPA redlines
messages.body:
Thanks Marissa — this is great. A couple of legal clarifications and a short polished paragraph I'd recommend sending to the customer: one, emphasize we are a processor and do not have insight into why a customer collects data; two, confirm contractual options to opt-out of use for training; three, avoid absolute statements about enforcement of prohibited uses that are outside our visibility.

Suggested final paragraph for the attestation packet:
"Redwood Inference is a data processor that provides inference services to its customers. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children on behalf of our customers; control of collection, including any age-verification or parental...

cc: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.inference>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.inference>
messages.subject: Re: Request: COPPA & children's data attestation
messages.body:
Hi Helen —

Thanks for the clear list. Please find below our formal attestation and supporting docs attached. Rachel has approved the final wording and we've attached a redline of the DPA that includes an explicit training opt-out clause. If you need a signed copy of the attestation we can countersign and DocuSign the short statement.

FINAL ATTESTATION
Redwood Inference is a data processor providing inference services to its customers. Redwood does not knowingly collect personal information from children on behalf of our customers; control of collection, including any age-verification or parental-consent mechanics, rests with the customer (data controller). title: Consolidated office hours — Redwood group demo & Q&A (options)
body:
Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>: Hi all —

Thanks again for the interest in Redwood. A few teams have asked for a short group demo / office hours so we thought we’d consolidate into one 45-minute session focused on production inference (latency, cost, and integration patterns) plus a 15-minute open Q&A.

Proposed slots (pick the best for your group):
- Option A: Tue Mar 18, 10:00–11:00 AM PT (1:00–2:00 PM ET)
- Option B: Wed Mar 19, 8:00–9:00 AM ...

Sara Park <sara@plasmatech.com>: Thanks Sean — this looks great. A few notes from our side:
- Option A conflicts with our on-site engineering review (internal), but Option B would be tight for the APAC members on our team.
- We could do Tue Mar 18 at 1:00 PM ET (which is your Option A) if you can keep it to ~45 minutes — our lead infra engineer (Ravi) can join for the first 30 minutes.

Attendees from PlasmaTech: Sara Park (PM)...

messages.body:
Quick internal note — Jonathan on AeroSoft said their folks prefer afternoons ET; Emily from LumenDesk flagged earlier that mornings PT are tough. Given the mix, I recommend locking Option A (Tue Mar 18 at 10:00 PT / 1:00 ET) and aim for a 45-min core demo + 15-min Q&A drop-in. We'll record and send notes.

Alex — can you prepare a short slide on Dedicated capacity and another 5-min runthrough on security (VPC/KMS)? I’ll handle the Q&A moderation.

Vanessa
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai

body:

Thanks again for the demo last week. We’re moving toward a short POC and procurement asked me to pin down a couple of SLA items before we sign the pilot SOW: specifically the time-to-first-token (TTFT) for streaming endpoints and sustained token/sec expectations during peak processing.

Background: our use case streams a large initial system prompt (20–30k tokens) and then many short user turns; we ca...

High-level: Redwood’s baseline public SLAs differ for streaming vs sync (non-streaming) primarily because streaming delivers partial payloads and we optimize for steady emission. Typical expectations (hosted):

messages.subject: Initial token window and throughput expectations for FastPay POC
messages.body:
Hi Aditya —

Thanks again for the demo last week. We’re moving toward a short POC and procurement asked me to pin down a couple of SLA items before we sign the pilot SOW: specifically the time-to-first-token (TTFT) for streaming endpoints and sustained token/sec expectations during peak processing.

Background: our use case streams a large initial system prompt (20–30k tokens) and then many short user turns; we care about the first token arriving quickly for perceived latency, but we also have a bursty steady-state during peak reconciliation jobs (~10-15 concurrent streams).

Questions:
- What are Redwood’s standard commitments (p95) for TTFT on streaming vs non-streaming endpoints?
- Wha...

messages.subject: Re: Initial token window and throughput expectations for FastPay POC
messages.body:
Joni — thanks for the clear summary. Short answers up front, then a recommended next step.

High-level: Redwood’s baseline public SLAs differ for streaming vs sync (non-streaming) primarily because streaming delivers partial payloads and we optimize for steady emission. Typical expectations (hosted):
- Non-streaming (sync) TTFT (p95): ~250–350 ms for short-context requests.
- Streaming first-token (p95): ~350–600 ms depending on model variant and context size.
- Sustained emission: our engine can sustain thousands of tokens/sec across a Dedicated pool; per-connection sustained rates are gated by model and kernel (typical per-stream steady-state 50–500 tokens/sec depending on instance clas...

messages.subject: Re: Initial token window and throughput expectations for FastPay POC
messages.body:
Hi all — quick technical note and attachment list. I dug into similar pilots and sketched a conservative stepped-throughput profile below. Attaching a template SLO exception form and a sample latency bundle from a prior customer for reference.

Conservative stepped-throughput proposal (per-stream):
- Cold window (0–30s): no strict TTFT SLA; expect first-token p95 up to ~5–6s when serving massive system prompts (20–30k) unless prefix caching is pre-warmed.
- Warmed steady state (30–300s): target 75 tokens/sec p95 per stream on our medium-grade dedicated nodes (can be higher for high-end reserved GPU classes).
- Scale: we’ll size the Dedicated pool to ensure aggregate capacity for your 10–1...

messages.subject: Re: Initial token window and throughput expectations for FastPay POC
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel & Aditya — that stepped profile looks workable. We’ll:
- Share a 10-minute run (including the large system prompt) by EOD tomorrow.
- Mark qualifying requests with X-FP-Large-Context and include the context-length header.
- Procure the 30-day pilot SOW with the carveout language; Keisha will route the rider to legal.

Keisha — I’ll attach the trace and sign the DocuSign fallback agreement once I hear back from procurement. Aditya — please include the SLO exception form and the HubSpot deal link in the draft SOW.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Joni
Head of Engineering, FastPay
joni@fastpay.io

body:
Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@everblue.com>: Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As we discussed in the RFP, procurement needs a clear control map that ties Redwood's controls to our ISO 27001 scope and SOC 2 CCs. Two asks to move us forward: 

1) A control mapping (ISO clauses -> SOC2 criteria -> Redwood control IDs) so our audit team can reconcile differences.
2) An initial inventory of available evidence for each control (audit logs, design doc...

Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>: Hi Liam —

Great to hear from you. We can absolutely produce a mapping and an evidence index. Proposed approach and timelines: 

- Deliverable 1 (by Tue, Dec 2): a first-pass control map (ISO27001 clauses -> SOC2 CC -> Redwood control IDs) and a short summary of evidence available for each control.
- Deliverable 2 (by Fri, Dec 12): packaged artifacts (redacted logs, diagrams, sample rep...

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As we discussed in the RFP, procurement needs a clear control map that ties Redwood's controls to our ISO 27001 scope and SOC 2 CCs. Two asks to move us forward: 

1) A control mapping (ISO clauses -> SOC2 criteria -> Redwood control IDs) so our audit team can reconcile differences.
2) An initial inventory of available evidence for each control (audit logs, design docs, configuration screenshots, signed vendor attestations).

Can you confirm what you can deliver by Dec 2 and what will require additional work or third-party attestations? Our target is to complete reviews by Dec 15 to hit the year-end procurement window.

I've attached the RFP ...

We're scoping the LedgerBank SOC 2 interim and need a set of artifacts and exports tied to your data residency, TTL, and tenant deletion controls. Please provide the following by EOD Friday (6/12):

- Current retention policy document and a short summary of how TTLs are applied per tenant (CSV or PDF).
- A sample deletion log for three tenants (one of which should be ledgerbank-prod). The log should in...

Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>: Claire —

Thanks for the clear list. We can provide the retention policy, deletion playbook, and a sample deletion log for ledgerbank-prod plus two other tenants. I'm attaching the retention_policy_v2.pdf and deletion_playbook.md to this message for immediate review.

Michael will extract the sample CSV and the TTL export SQL; Karthik will validate redaction for PII fields. We aim to de...

messages.body:
Hi Priya —

We're scoping the LedgerBank SOC 2 interim and need a set of artifacts and exports tied to your data residency, TTL, and tenant deletion controls. Please provide the following by EOD Friday (6/12):

- Current retention policy document and a short summary of how TTLs are applied per tenant (CSV or PDF).
- A sample deletion log for three tenants (one of which should be ledgerbank-prod). The log should include timestamp, tenant_id, object_key, deletion_reason, and operation_id.
- The SQL or export command used to produce TTL mapping (schema/tables referenced).
- A short attestation template that we can use in the SOC 2 report demonstrating an executed deletion for ledgerbank-prod...

messages.subject: Re: Evidence request: residency/TTL/deletion exports for LedgerBank SOC2
messages.body:
Claire —

Thanks for the clear list. We can provide the retention policy, deletion playbook, and a sample deletion log for ledgerbank-prod plus two other tenants. I'm attaching the retention_policy_v2.pdf and deletion_playbook.md to this message for immediate review.

Michael will extract the sample CSV and the TTL export SQL; Karthik will validate redaction for PII fields. We aim to deliver the exports and a signed attestation by Friday 6/12 EOD. If anything shifts, I will escalate.

Attachments: retention_policy_v2.pdf (policy summary), deletion_playbook.md (choreography + owners)

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Security & Compliance — Redwood Inference

messages.subject: Re: Evidence request: residency/TTL/deletion exports for LedgerBank SOC2
messages.body:
Priya / Karthik —

I've queued up the extraction. Commands/notes below (internal only):

- TTL mapping export (Postgres): 
 psql -h analytics-host -U readonly -d infra_db -c "COPY (SELECT tenant_id, data_class, ttl_days, applied_at FROM tenant_ttl) TO STDOUT WITH CSV" > ttl_mapping_export.csv

- Deletion log sample: we will pull from audit_store.audit_events where event_type = 'tenant_data_deletion' and tenant_id IN ('ledgerbank-prod', 'test-tenant-17', 'partner-x'). Output column set: timestamp, tenant_id, object_key, deletion_reason, operation_id, initiated_by. Redact object_key where it contains customer PII.

I attached tenant_deletion_log_sample.csv (redacted) for initial review. It'...

messages.subject: Re: Evidence request: residency/TTL/deletion exports for LedgerBank SOC2
messages.body:
Priya / Team —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the samples so far. A couple of clarifying questions before we close the artifact set:

1) TTL mapping — can you include the table schema and a short note on when TTLs are evaluated (background job schedule / realtime enforcement)? We need the schedule to confirm retention windows align with policy.

2) Deletion logs — the sample is good. For ledgerbank-prod specifically we need an attestation that a tenant-level deletion was executed (not just object-level deletes). Can you provide a signed attestation PDF that references operation_id XYZ-DELETE-20260315-987 (example) or the real operation id in your logs?

3) Residency mapping — please...

cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Evidence request: residency/TTL/deletion exports for LedgerBank SOC2
messages.body:
Claire / LedgerBank security team —

Attached are the remaining items you requested: TTL schema and job cadence (ttl_mapping_export.sql), and a signed deletion attestation for ledgerbank-prod (deletion_attestation_ledgerbank.pdf). Summary below:

- TTL enforcement: Background job runs every hour (cron: 0 * * * *) on the retention-host fleet; final-stage synchronous checks occur during tenant shutdown flows. See ttl_mapping_export.sql for schema and sample rows.

- Deletion attestation: deletion_attestation_ledgerbank.pdf references operation_id: OP-DEL-20260315-2b7f, includes timestamp and scope (tenant-level erasure), and is signed by our Data Operations lead.

- Residency: ledgerbank-pr...

Proposed windows (all PT):

Hi Samantha (and Tom),

Thanks again for the time today — good conversation and helpful context on where Novapulse is heading with search & routing. Quick recap and proposed next step: a 30-minute product preview focused on the integration points we discussed (ingest, embeddings, and latency expectations).

Proposed windows (all PT):
- Option A: Thu May 7, 10:00–10:30 AM PT (convert: 1:00–1:30 PM ET)
- Option B: Fri May 8, 2:30–3:00 PM PT (5:30–6:00 PM ET)
- Option C: Mon May 11, 9:00–9:30 AM PT (12:00–12:30 PM ET)

Please pick the best window for your team and nominate the SME(s) who should join (engineering or data lead preferred). If none of these work, tell me two 30-minute bands that...

messages.subject: Re: 30-min preview: confirm a slot + SME nomination
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel — great talk.

Option B (Fri May 8, 2:30 PM PT) works best for us. I’m looped in with Tom (cc’d) who will be our engineering SME. Tom — are you able to join that time?

Quick answers to the prep asks:
- Dataset: ~12M documents (mostly text, mix of metadata)
- Concurrency: peak ~80 QPS, but typically 10–20 QPS
- Compliance: no special residency needs today; SOC2 is a check-box for procurement later

If you can share a short agenda and the deck ahead of time, I’ll circulate to the stakeholders. Also please send a calendar invite.

Best,
Samantha Ortiz
Director of Product, Novapulse
samantha.ortiz@novapulse.com | +1 (206) 555-3421

messages.subject: Re: 30-min preview: confirm a slot + SME nomination
messages.body:
Nice — looping in Sean to confirm we have the right deck and the engineer for the Fri 2:30 PT spot. Sam (external) asked for the deck ahead of time; do we want to use the standard 10-slide intro (demo-deck-v1.pdf) or a condensed 6-slide version?

Also, quick engineering ask: Tom mentioned concurrency ~80 QPS — Sean, can you confirm whether we should bring an infra engineer for the live Q&A or if product/solutions will cover it?

Marissa

messages.body:
Soren — thanks, this is great. A few clarifications before we provision the test account: 
1) Token budget: will the 120 req/s profile use the full 512-token average or do you expect shorter completions (~120 tokens)? We need to set the per-request token cap on the test key correctly to mirror cost and KV-cache behavior.
2) For the burst ramp (180 req/s), do you want Redwood to enforce an artificial queue/backpressure if our backend sees >150ms per-request queueing? We can enable an automatic fallback to a smaller model variant if queueing breaches that threshold. Please confirm desired behavior.
3) Streaming metrics: we will push per-connection metrics into the Console. During the rehear...

messages.subject: Re: Post-demo: Atlas AI customer-led stress run (k6) — next steps
messages.body:
Soren — great, thanks for the fast turn-around. Confirming the plan per Rachel's questions:
- Token profile: please assume avg response length of 140 tokens for the steady-state run; we will set the test key token cap to 220 tokens per request to buffer variability.
- Fallback behavior: enable automatic fallback to the smaller compatible variant when queueing exceeds 150ms; Redwood will mark that as a rollback trigger in the MCP and capture metrics for post-run analysis.
- We added your CIDRs to the template and have queued the allowlist change (54.85.12.0/24, 54.85.13.0/24).

Deliverables before the Fri window: 
1) Test API key and project details (will email creds in a separate secure t...

Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>: Hi Ava,

Thanks again for taking the demo today — appreciate the clear constraints from your side. Quick recap from our end so we have aligned goals before the customer-led run:

- SLO targets you confirmed on the call: p95 end-to-end latency <= 300 ms for text generation (typical request size ~ 64 tokens), sustained throughput target ~ 350 req/s for the gateway tier. For streaming endpoints we noted a target of <...

Ava Morrison <ava@cloudtide.com>: Thanks, Karthik — that summary is helpful. A few clarifications from our side: 

- We want to land on p95 <= 350 ms (end-to-end) rather than 300 to give some buffer for payload variance.
- Streaming concurrency: we expect realistic peak concurrency to be ~150; can you confirm Redwood can sustain 150 clients for the streaming endpoint with per-token latency below ~25 ms? The per-token <20 ms target...

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for taking the demo today — appreciate the clear constraints from your side. Quick recap from our end so we have aligned goals before the customer-led run:

- SLO targets you confirmed on the call: p95 end-to-end latency <= 300 ms for text generation (typical request size ~ 64 tokens), sustained throughput target ~ 350 req/s for the gateway tier. For streaming endpoints we noted a target of <= 20 ms per-token downstream latency under concurrency.
- Streaming concurrency target we captured as 120 concurrent clients, but flagged this for you to confirm (I called out 150 as an upper bound if you anticipate spikes).
- We prepared a starter k6 script (attached) that exerc...

messages.subject: Re: SLO readout and customer script transfer — CloudTide load-window coordination
messages.body:
Thanks, Karthik — that summary is helpful. A few clarifications from our side: 

- We want to land on p95 <= 350 ms (end-to-end) rather than 300 to give some buffer for payload variance.
- Streaming concurrency: we expect realistic peak concurrency to be ~150; can you confirm Redwood can sustain 150 clients for the streaming endpoint with per-token latency below ~25 ms? The per-token <20 ms target is optimistic for our worst-case clients.
- Throughput: we'll aim for 400 req/s as the peak probe for the gateway to validate autoscaling.

Preferred window: Jul 9 21:00–23:00 PT (off-hours for us). We will open a short maintenance window internally and route traffic accordingly.

I've attached ...

cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: SLO readout and customer script transfer — CloudTide load-window coordination
messages.body:
Ava — perfect, thanks for the direction. Short replies inline: 

- p95 <= 350 ms: noted and we'll target that for acceptance.
- Streaming concurrency 150: we can support that; Marissa will update the k6 script to set the streaming probe to 150 concurrent virtual users and extend the ramp to 15 minutes. That will also toggle the chunked streaming path to better reflect your client behavior.
- Peak probe throughput 400 req/s: pulling that into the test matrix. We'll include a steady-state 15-minute plateau at 400 rps and one 5-minute spike at 600 rps to validate autoscaling and graceful degradation.
- Short-term test API key: yes — we can provision a scoped test key for the run. Marissa, ca...

messages.body:

- I updated the k6 script and attached cloudtide_k6_script_v2.js. Changes: concurrency set to 150, ramp-up 15m, plateau 15m at 400 rps, 5m spike to 600 rps. Added chunked-streaming probe that emits per-token timings and a warmup phase for KV caching.
- Provisioned a staging API key for CloudTide: test_key_CLOUDTIDE_**** (TTL 48 hours). I'll share the masked key and one-time retrieval link via the Drive access note (for security reasons we won't paste the full key in thread).
- Dry-run on Redwood staging completed this morning; summary CSV is in the Drive folder (link above). Key observations: 95th percentile latency at plateau averaged 320 ms for text-ge...

Hi Ava & team —

Thanks again for the productive renewal discussion last week. As discussed, we've put together a concise Mutual Action Plan (MAP) to align on the renewal and the expansion add-on for dedicated capacity (10 additional reserved nodes). Primary goals for this MAP:

- Complete usage deep-dive to validate sizing and trial quota
- Surface any security/compliance evidence needed for procurement
- Target contract execution to enable April 1, 2026 start for expanded capacity

messages.subject: Intro + recap from today’s call — Redwood for Finlytics
messages.body:
Hi Alex —

Great to meet you and the Finlytics team today. Quick recap and suggested next steps so we’re aligned: 

What we heard (summary):
- Primary goal: reduce inference latency for batch scoring pipelines (current p95 ~1.2s) and get predictable per-token cost for real-time enrichment API.
- Data constraints: PII redaction required, must stay within Finlytics VPC for production; sandbox with representative data is ok for pilot.
- Scale expectation: ~100–150 reqs/sec during trading hours; occasional spikes to 400rps.
- Success criteria: p95 < 500ms (real-time), cost per inference target <$0.01, and SOC2 evidence for audit.

Proposed pilot (what Redwood can do quickly):
1) 2-week proof-...

messages.subject: Re: Intro + recap from today’s call — Redwood for Finlytics
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel — appreciate the recap and the level of detail. A couple quick notes: 

- Sandbox in our AWS account is fine; I’ll loop in our infra lead (sending separately).
- I attached a small sample payload (sanitized) in my calendar invite — see the file I just uploaded to the invite.
- Pilot kickoff on Tue 20 May 11:00 PT works for us.

I’ll try to get the security contact on the thread; otherwise I’ll introduce them Monday.

Best,
Alex
Head of Data Products | Finlytics

messages.subject: Checking in on sandbox + kickoff (quick follow-up)
messages.body:
Hi Alex —

Just bumping this to check whether you had a chance to introduce your infra/security lead. We’re holding the pilot slot on Tue 20 May in my calendar for now but can move if you need more time.

If the team prefers, Dev (cc’d) can run a short network checklist call with your infra lead — 15–20m to confirm VPC peering requirements and IAM setup.

Also, if you’d like an alternative date later that week, we have availability Thu 22 or Fri 23.

No rush — just want to keep momentum and make sure we don’t hit the end-of-quarter freeze on your side.

Thanks,
Rachel

messages.subject: Quick pulse — is this still a priority for Finlytics?
messages.body:
Alex —

Wanted to check in one more time. I know timelines shift at the end of the quarter; if now isn’t the right moment to run a pilot, totally understand. Two options: 

1) We pause and I’ll archive the opportunity — we can reopen when the team is ready.
2) We keep the pilot on the roadmap and I’ll send a short MSA and a kickoff checklist so your infra team has what they need.

If I don’t hear back by Tue 3 Jun, I’ll assume you want me to pause the file and I’ll close this out in our CRM. Either way, happy to re-engage when it’s timing for you.

—
Rachel

messages.subject: Closing the loop — should I shelve Finlytics?
messages.body:
Alex —

Closing the loop on this thread. We haven’t heard back and I want to be respectful of your calendar. If you’re still evaluating, we’re happy to:

- Hold an exploratory pilot whenever you’re ready (we can provide a short reserved window for Q3).
- Offer a small pilot credit to run the performance profile so you have concrete latency/cost numbers.

If you’d prefer we pause for now, I’ll mark the opportunity as shelved in our CRM and we’ll check back in Q3. Please reply with either “Pause” or “Keep open” and an optional target month.

Thanks for the time so far — appreciate it.

Best,
Rachel Kim
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood-pilot-proposal-v1.pdf

messages.body:
Samir —

Appreciate the ORB-INC id. Attached I’ve added the warmup metrics CSV (warmup_metrics.csv) and an evidence_manifest.json that maps the traces to timeline buckets. High-level preliminary timeline (short):
- 08:47:12 PT — autoscaler initiated parallel warmup attempts for 6 nodes in pool reserved-012; 2 nodes reported extended warmup > 45s.
- 08:48:01 PT — warmup coalescing triggered; a lock contention increased scheduler latency.
- 08:48:21 PT — eviction wave begins; KV cache misses spike causing tokenization and latency amplification.
- 08:50–09:02 PT — customer-facing errors observed; mitigation (increased headroom) applied 08:55 PT.

Preliminary cause (engineering hypothesis): a...

messages.subject: Re: Reserved capacity disruption: evidence handoff and scheduling a post-incident sync
messages.body:
Samir / team —

I’m sharing a short technical readout ahead of the postmortem. Key items:
- Root cause (TL;DR): race amplification in the warmup coalescer + transient driver restarts. The coalescer aggregates warmup for nodes that require similar model weights; under a skewed request distribution it held a scheduler lock longer than expected, allowing allocator queues to back up and triggering eviction heuristics. When a subset of nodes briefly restarted (driver), the pool entered reduced-capacity state and autoscaler attempts to spin replacements were slower due to the same warmup pressure.

Immediate remediation we’ve applied and will push to stable in 48–72 hours:
1) Raise warmup coale...

Rachel —

Thank you for the update. From a commercial/legal perspective we’ll need:
- A short formal credit note with calculation methodology and effective date.
- Confirmation of whether this incident triggers any SLO remediation clauses in our order form; if so, we will need the supporting evidence package attached to the credit note.
- Point of contact for invoicing/procurement to accept the credit.

I’ve looped procurement@orbitalai.com for handling credit acceptance.

Best,
Tina Verma
General Counsel (External) — OrbitalAI

cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, procurement@orbitalai.com

Thanks — confirming the postmortem call for Fri 2025-09-19 at 10:00 PT. Calendar invite and packet (folder link) sent via Google Calendar — link included in the invite and drive packet: https://drive.google.com/drive/fake-evidence-packet.

What we will bring to the call:
- Finalized timeline and annotated traces (Ben).
- Exact token/tenant impact mapping and final credit calculation (Marissa).
- Action plan and rollout timeline for the coalescer fix and autoscaler backoff (Ben/Kimberly).
- Signed evidence manifest that you can attach to an SLO remediation if needed.

Interim credit: we will generate a draft credit note (time-proportionate covering 50% of the impacted windo...

Key takeaways from today’s call:

Immediate recommendations (high level):
1) Run a lightweight eval against a curated prompt set (attached) covering top-50 product intents + 20 edge-case specs. We ran an initial pass (attached spreadsheet) comparing an op...

messages.subject: Re: Post-demo: ACME model-fit summary & recommended next steps
messages.body:
Thanks Alex — really helpful summary. A couple quick clarifications before we lock the deep-dive: 
1) Can you call out which open-model config you tested (HF repo / quantization flags)? We need to replicate.
2) For the acceptance thresholds: intent accuracy >= 88% is reasonable, but for spec extraction our product team wants hallucination <= 1.5% (we’re fairly strict there). Will that be achievable with quantized open models?
3) Curious about the p95 latency numbers you quoted — is that measured on your dedicated infra or a cloud baseline? We need realistic estimates for our front-end SLA.

Also copying Karthik and Lauren. If we can get the detailed runbook and the prompt-set in a reprodu...

messages.subject: Re: Post-demo: ACME model-fit summary & recommended next steps — eval details and parity notes
messages.body:
Jamie — thanks, great questions. A few concrete items to address each: 

Which model/config we tested
- Open candidate: OpenLLaMA-13B-ql (HF: open-llama/open-13b, quantization: 4-bit QL + kernel optimizations). We used a fixed-seed eval run, max context 1024, and disabled sampling (greedy) for deterministic accuracy metrics.
- Redwood variant (internal): RedCore-7B-optimized — tuned on product copy + distilled spec extraction head (lower cost option).

Acceptance thresholds & baseline parity
- Our initial numbers (see attached spreadsheet, sheet: "summary"):
 - Intent accuracy (top intent match): OpenLLaMA-13B-ql = 90.2% | ACME-baseline = 91.0%
 - Spec extraction F1: OpenLLaMA-13B-ql = 86...

messages.subject: Re: Post-demo: ACME model-fit summary & recommended next steps — scheduling
messages.body:
Jamie / Samir — looping in infra and ops: we’ve penciled the tech deep-dive for Wed 2/25 16:00 PT. I’ll send the calendar invite and include a short canary runbook (traffic sample format, expected metrics, and rollback criteria).

Quick ask: can Samir confirm a contact on your infra side who can provide a traffic sample (even 1 hour of anonymized logs) so we can size batching and KV cache behavior? Also confirm whether your CDN will sit in front of the widget (affects latency budgeting).

Thanks — Lauren
Director, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference
---

messages.subject: Re: Post-demo: ACME model-fit summary & recommended next steps — scheduling & procurement note
messages.body:
Thanks everyone — schedule works for us. Samir here: I’ll get our infra contact to share an anonymized hour of logs by EOD Tue. A few procurement questions we’ll surface ahead of the deep-dive:
- Can Redwood provide a short pricing estimate for a 12-month reserved Dedicated tranche sized for our projected peak (est. 120 qps sustained spike)?
- If open models hit parity after a canary, what are the standard SLA/credits for model-quality regressions (we need written commitments around availability and quality regressions during rollouts)?

I’ll compile these and share with Alex prior to Wed so the deep-dive includes the commercial side.

Best,
Samir
Head of Engineering | ACME Retail

1) Goals (what Watchtower needs to validate)
- End-to-end latency breakdown (ingest -> model inference -> postprocess) under expected load (100 rps peak).
- Token-level cost and latency distribution for the top-3 prompt templates.
- Error surface: <0.1% 5xxs...

- For the latency SLA we mentioned 100 rps peak, but our realistic production distribution has long tails from large prompts. Can we define the POC workload as: 70% short (<=128 tokens), 25% medium (129-512), 5% long (>512)? That's what we'll generate for the synthetic run.

- Regarding...

messages.subject: Weekly MAP — performance checkpoint: latency & throughput (MedSync)
messages.body:
Hi Elena / Tom —

Thanks for joining the MAP cadence — quick weekly checkpoint notes attached and a short recap of the perf benchmarking we ran this morning against the evaluation prompts you shared. Goal for today: confirm whether results meet the latency SLOs in the MAP and agree next tuning steps + owners so we can keep to the proposed close timeline.

High level:
- Test harness: 8k-token context snapshots, 1k-token responses where applicable (representative of production flows).
- Model variant: open-llm-2.2-16b (quantized int8x4) served on p4d-equivalent nodes in our Dedicated pool.
- Traffic shape: mix of chat and single-call generation; we ran 500 warm-up requests then sampled 2,00...

messages.subject: Re: Weekly MAP — performance checkpoint: latency & throughput (MedSync)
messages.body:
Alex —

Appreciate the summary and the files. Quick notes from our side after a skim:
- The p95 numbers will be the gating factor for our care workflows — anything consistently over 700ms for inference end-to-end will require us to revert to a pull model.
- We need a breakdown by route: triage (short prompts), summary (longer context), and batch embeddings (for nightly jobs). Can you confirm whether the 2,000 measurement calls include a route-level split?
- Also can Priyom attach the raw histogram and a short note on whether KV-cache warm times were included in the p95?

If we can get those details today that would help us decide on the next MAP milestone.

—Elena
VP Platform, MedSync Hea...

messages.subject: Re: Weekly MAP — performance checkpoint: latency & throughput (MedSync)
messages.body:
Hi all — attaching the summarized numbers and a couple of notes from the run this morning. See also attached tiny histogram snippets inside the latency_logs_20260601.tar.gz (top-level files: p50_p95_histograms.txt).

Benchmark summary (2,000 measurement calls after 500 warm-ups) — route split: triage=800, summary=900, embeddings=300. All numbers are end-to-end inference latency measured at the service edge (includes network hop to Dedicated nodes in us-west-2 but excludes post-processing on client).

- p50: 78 ms

messages.subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — IP and training language
messages.body:
Aditya,

Thanks for sending the draft. Our legal team reviewed the MSA and Order Form and flagged three areas that are blocking procurement: output ownership (Section 3.2), training/data-use (Section 7.1), and the feedback/telemetry license (Section 9).

High level asks from DotMetrics:
- Customer retains title to any Customer Output generated by the service (no assignment to vendor).
- No use of Customer content or outputs for training models without explicit opt-in (we need an express \"training opt-in\" rather than implicit rights).
- Any feedback or telemetry Redwood uses must be aggregated, de-identified, and limited to improving the service; cannot be used for model retraining that ...

cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — Handback, training opt-outs, and feedback license
messages.body:
Samir — thanks for the clear summary and the redlines. Looping in Laura and Ben from Legal and Rachel from CS so we can get this triaged.

Quick plan:
1) Ben and Laura will propose concrete clause language that preserves the Customer's title to Output while allowing Redwood to operate and improve the service under controlled limits.
2) We'll propose a training opt-out toggle for hosted accounts (customer-managed flag) and explicit language for Private deployments (no training).
3) We'll provide a separate feedback license that is: non-exclusive, royalty-free, limited to aggregated/de-identified telemetry and product improvements, and with a 12-month lookback restriction for retraining unl...

to: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: RE: MSA + Order Form — proposed clause language and fallback
messages.body:
Team / Samir — attaching proposed clause text (also pasted below) to address DotMetrics' concerns while protecting service operation. I tried to keep changes narrow and operational.

Key points:
- Title vs. License: customer keeps title to Customer Output (we admit they \"own\" the output). Redwood requests a limited license back only to the extent needed to provide the service to that customer and to perform troubleshooting/support. No assignment of title.
- Training / Model Updates: For Hosted accounts, default is: Redwood will NOT use Customer Content or Customer Output to retrain foundation models for third-party use unless Customer elects the \"training opt-in\". For Private (VPC / o...

to: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — feedback on proposed language
messages.body:
Ben, Aditya — thanks for the clear drafting. A couple of practical points from procurement/legal here at DotMetrics:

1) The 12-month limit helps, but we prefer a 6-12 month negotiable window. Can you make that a variable (\"not to exceed 12 months unless agreed\")?
2) We need the opt-in to be auditable for compliance. Can you add a clause that Redwood will maintain an audit trail (console flag + change history) and provide a signed attestation on request?
3) The phrase \"revocable license\" is good, but procurement wants explicit carveouts: no sublicensing to affiliates that would repurpose our outputs.

If you can accept those three, procurement is prepared to move to DocuSign and issue...

cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — next steps and action items
messages.body:
Samir / Priya — thanks, this is actionable and great progress. Confirming next steps from Redwood side: 

- Ben will update the clause to make the telemetry window \"not to exceed 12 months unless agreed in writing\" and will add language about maintaining an auditable opt-in flag and providing attestation on request.
- We will add a carveout prohibiting sublicensing of Customer Output to third parties for repurposing absent Customer consent.
- Laura will prepare a DocuSign-ready Order Form with the updated redlines attached; Rachel will add a short operations note describing the training opt-in toggle behavior for Hosted customers and the Private deployment guarantee.
- I'll send a calen...

messages.body:
Hey Alex — we’re at the point where AWS + GCP partner portals want “final” customer-facing URLs (security overview + onboarding steps) and they’re validating links during review.

Two asks where we need Eng help (docs site / routing):

1) Redirects / stability
- We have older / legacy URLs floating around from the previous listing and from sales collateral.
- Can we get 301 redirects in place so there are no 404s during partner review?

Top legacy routes I’ve seen in the wild:
- /aws-marketplace
- /gcp-marketplace
- /marketplace
- /docs/marketplace
- /security

2) Navigation/IA (minor)
- We need the marketplace onboarding pages discoverable under “Getting started” (or at least accessible ...

messages.subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms
messages.body:
Yep — agree we should treat these as “hard” URLs for partner validation.

Two quick clarifications:
1) Do we want /marketplace/aws and /marketplace/gcp to be simple landing pages (with “Subscribe” + “How it works” short version), or just keep /onboarding as the entry?
2) For /security/marketplace, are we comfortable having that fully public, or do we need a “public summary” page that links out to gated evidence? (I know Security had opinions here.)

Action on my side:
- I’ll file INT-771 with the redirect list + nav request and ask for a docs-site engineer to implement 301s + ensure canonical routing.
- We can keep the content PRs separate; redirects/nav should be low risk.

If you confir...

messages.subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms
messages.body:
On (1): yes, we should have landing pages.
- https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/aws
- https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/gcp

These can be lightweight and point to:
- onboarding
- FAQ
- (where relevant) security overview

On (2): we should publish a “marketplace security overview” publicly with careful language, and keep evidence links gated / “available on request.” That matches what AWS reviewers have been asking for + what Aisha/Olga have been aligned on.

Also +1 on redirecting /marketplace -> an index page (not a 404 or product marketing site). Partner reviewers seem to stay inside docs links.

Soojin

messages.subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms
messages.body:
Confirming:

(1) Landing pages — yes.
- /marketplace/aws
- /marketplace/gcp
- /marketplace (index)

(2) Security overview public — yes.
- /security/marketplace should be the “public summary” page.
- It can include: shared responsibility, data handling basics, encryption-at-rest/in-transit high level, audit log feature overview, retention defaults + “configurable,” and “evidence available on request.”
- No direct links to SOC 2 report, pen test, etc.

One more nuance: AWS form field is extremely picky. They asked for a “Security documentation URL” and then the reviewer clicked it live on the call.

Forwarding the relevant snippet below (from the AWS listing review sync notes):

---
On Tue,...

messages.subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms
messages.body:
Filed and linked: INT-771

Ticket summary includes:
- Create marketplace index + AWS/GCP landing routes in docs
- Implement 301 redirects for legacy routes in your CSV
- Canonicalization: enforce non-trailing slash or consistent trailing slash (whichever the site currently uses), plus a single canonical per page
- Nav update: add “Cloud Marketplace” under Getting started

Proposed redirect behavior (high level):
- /aws-marketplace -> /marketplace/aws
- /gcp-marketplace -> /marketplace/gcp
- /docs/marketplace -> /marketplace
- /marketplace/aws/onboarding and /marketplace/gcp/onboarding are the final “how it works” pages

I asked for an ETA by EOD tomorrow; will update once Eng confirms.

Alex

messages.subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms
messages.body:
Perfect.

One tiny change request before Eng starts (to reduce churn in partner forms):
- Please make sure the URLs we submit are EXACTLY these (no later renames):
 - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/aws
 - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/aws/onboarding
 - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/gcp
 - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/gcp/onboarding
 - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/faq
 - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/security/marketplace

Also request: /marketplace should not redirect to marketing site. It needs to stay in docs.

Once we have redirect + nav live, I’ll update the AWS/GCP submission drafts and ping Partnersh...

title: Scheduling: SOC 2 / DPA walkthrough for FinovateX
body:
Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>: Hi Jordan —

Thanks again for the great conversation yesterday. Per our discussion, we'd like to schedule a combined SOC 2 controls walkthrough and DPA review so your audit team can validate evidence transfer and retention controls before the pen test.

Proposed windows (Pacific time):
- Tue Jul 8: 09:00–10:30 PT (90 min)
- Thu Jul 10: 16:00–17:30 PT (90 min)

We can compress to a 60 min slot if you prefer a narro...

Jordan King <security@finovatex.com>: Hi Alex —

Thanks for the options. Our security team is tight that week (Q2 close) and the audit lead is OOO Jul 9–12. A couple of notes:

- Thu Jul 10 at 16:00 PT conflicts with our monthly all-hands — can't do that one.
- Tue Jul 8 at 09:00 PT *might* work for the core security folks, but our external auditor (Marta Lopez) said she prefers an afternoon slot to overlap with her East Coast schedule.
...

Hi Alex —

Thanks for the options. Our security team is tight that week (Q2 close) and the audit lead is OOO Jul 9–12. A couple of notes:

- Thu Jul 10 at 16:00 PT conflicts with our monthly all-hands — can't do that one.
- Tue Jul 8 at 09:00 PT *might* work for the core security folks, but our external auditor (Marta Lopez) said she prefers an afternoon slot to overlap with her East Coast schedule.

Two alternative windows that would work on our side:
- Mon Jul 7: 13:00–14:00 PT (60 min) — we can keep this tight to controls + evidence handoff
- Wed Jul 9: 14:00–15:30 PT (90 min) — broader attendance possible

Also copying procurement (procure@finovatex.com) to flag that we may need a sho...

cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Scheduling: SOC 2 / DPA walkthrough for FinovateX
messages.body:
Jordan / Marta —

Appreciate the quick reply and the constraints. We can absolutely meet your afternoon preference so Marta can join. To keep momentum and avoid multi-round rescheduling, proposing the following consolidated options (PT):

Option A (preferred for auditor overlap):
- Wed Jul 9, 14:00–15:30 PT (90 min) — we’ll include an evidence transfer demo (SFTP/S3 examples) and a Q&A block for auditors.

Option B (compact):
- Mon Jul 7, 13:00–14:00 PT (60 min) — controls + evidence overview, follow-up deep-dive if needed.

Logistics: we will provide a Zoom link and an ops bridge. Ben, Vanessa — we'll need a brief pre-call dry run to ensure the evidence demo (SFTP keys and sample logs) c...

to: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Scheduling: SOC 2 / DPA walkthrough for FinovateX
messages.body:
Locking in the ops ask — I can join the dry run Mon Jul 7 at 10:00 PT. Vanessa, can you confirm whether ops needs any special access (temp SFTP creds) ahead of the dry run?

Laura — tagging procurement question: if FinovateX prefers the 60-min Option B, we typically cover a one-hour briefing as part of pre-sales support for trials; anything beyond that (90-min auditor walkthrough with a live evidence demo) is usually billed as a professional services block. If you want, I can draft a very short addendum SOW and loop procurement for signature — that will keep things tidy and avoid a surprise invoice.

I’ll stand by to send the calendar invite when Alex confirms.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior ...

body:
Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>: Hi Michael — great to speak earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side for the account erase/RTBF scope you described: 

- Scope we captured: user PII (name, email), model interaction logs, embeddings for support transcripts. You indicated retention rules for analytics (90 days) and a stricter requirement to purge PII within 30 days for terminated accounts.
- Proposed Redwood approach:
 * Immediate...

Michael Harris <michael.harris@finplatform.com>: Ben/Rachel — thanks for the recap. Confirming:

- Yes, PII must be purged within 30 calendar days from the deletion request for terminated users.
- Analytics buckets (aggregated events) may persist up to 90 days; embeddings used for search must be deleted with PII.
- Backup situation: our long-term snapshots (on our object store) are immutable for 120 days due to audit controls. We need...

Hi Michael — great to speak earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side for the account erase/RTBF scope you described: 

- Scope we captured: user PII (name, email), model interaction logs, embeddings for support transcripts. You indicated retention rules for analytics (90 days) and a stricter requirement to purge PII within 30 days for terminated accounts.
- Proposed Redwood approach:
 * Immediate: create a scoped erase runbook for your account (we'll map object keys + retention buckets).
 * KMS/key policy: handoff plan to rotate customer-managed key on purge day so tokens stored encrypted with customer key are irrecoverable.
 * Backups: we noted you have immutable snapsh...

messages.subject: Re: Follow-up & proposed erase kickoff timeline
messages.body:
Ben/Rachel — thanks for the recap. Confirming:

- Yes, PII must be purged within 30 calendar days from the deletion request for terminated users.
- Analytics buckets (aggregated events) may persist up to 90 days; embeddings used for search must be deleted with PII.
- Backup situation: our long-term snapshots (on our object store) are immutable for 120 days due to audit controls. We need to understand how Redwood's purge pipeline handles these snapshots and whether you can provide attestation once the in-prod data is removed.

I’ve looped in our security team (security@finplatform.com) — they’ll share our data retention annex and an initial list of snapshot object prefixes. Attaching the r...

cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Follow-up & proposed erase kickoff timeline
messages.body:
Michael / Security team — thanks for confirming. Looping in Soojin (Privacy Ops) and Marissa (Customer Reliability) so we can align on both the technical and evidence/attestation pieces.

High-level plan we propose (requested confirmations in bold):

1) Scope & object mapping (this week)
 - Soojin will run a quick discovery to map which keys correspond to \"PII\" vs \"analytics\". Please share the snapshot prefixes when convenient. **Action: security@finplatform.com to share list by EOD Wednesday.**

2) KMS handoff & key rotation (erase event)
 - Options: (A) Redwood rotates a customer-managed key in the KMS and triggers an immediate rewrap/purge; (B) Customer performs a rotate+revoke and...

cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Follow-up & proposed erase kickoff timeline
messages.body:
Michael / security team — putting the operational summary in one place for the kickoff call: 

- We will deliver a scoped erase matrix (CSV) that maps Redwood object keys to your retention buckets. Expect the CSV via HubSpot link within 48 hours.
- For immutable snapshots: Redwood will provide an attestation artifact and a manifest for your team to run to erase snapshot prefixes once your immutable window closes. We will not delete objects in customer-managed snapshot stores without explicit authorization.
- KMS: we recommend option (A) — Redwood triggers a rotation+revocation event under a customer-managed key policy to produce a single-step irrecoverable state for our copies. If you pre...

Thanks again for the call earlier — great to hear about the workload profile and your latency targets. Quick recap and a proposal for next steps so we can lock the technical deep-dive with our ML + infra folks:

title: Private deployment: Networking & VPC peering kickoff with Acme Corp
body:
Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>: Hi Tom, Priya —

Congrats again on the agreement. I'm taking the lead on the CS handoff and implementation for Acme's private deployment (VPC peering + private endpoints + on-prem packaging). To get the network side scheduled smoothly, can you share:

- Preferred VPC CIDR(s) and whether you want a single peering or multiple (prod/staging)
- Any restrictive egress/ingress rules (blocked IP ranges, outbound proxies)...

Tom Hargreaves <tom.hargreaves@acme-corp.com>: Hi Marissa —

Thanks — excited to get this moving. For now we plan a single peering to our primary VPC. Details below:

- Primary VPC CIDR: 10.40.0.0/16
- Staging VPC (if needed later): 10.41.0.0/16
- We have an outbound proxy for internet egress; most hosts cannot reach arbitrary external IPs.
- Our NetOps point: Priya Saini (priya.saini@acme-corp.com), NetOps Lead.

Preferred 2-hour...

messages.body:
Hi Tom, Priya —

Congrats again on the agreement. I'm taking the lead on the CS handoff and implementation for Acme's private deployment (VPC peering + private endpoints + on-prem packaging). To get the network side scheduled smoothly, can you share:

- Preferred VPC CIDR(s) and whether you want a single peering or multiple (prod/staging)
- Any restrictive egress/ingress rules (blocked IP ranges, outbound proxies)
- NetOps contact and a couple possible 2-hour maintenance windows for the peering work

I've attached our standard \"redwood-vpc-peering-checklist.pdf\" and a sample network config YAML to help your team prepare. We'll propose a mutual action plan once we have your CIDRs and net...

messages.subject: Re: Private deployment: networking kickoff and info requests
messages.body:
Hi Marissa —

Thanks — excited to get this moving. For now we plan a single peering to our primary VPC. Details below:

- Our NetOps point: Priya Saini (priya.saini@acme-corp.com), NetOps Lead.

Preferred 2-hour maintenance windows (PST): Tue 2026-03-17 22:00-00:00 or Thu 2026-03-19 00:00-02:00. Either works but prefer the 17th.

I'm attaching our security questionnaire (acme-security-questionnaire_v2.docx) — a few items might drive decisions on private endpoints vs on-prem packaging.

—Tom

messages.subject: Re: Private deployment: networking kickoff and info requests
messages.body:
Marissa —

Thanks for the heads up and for Tom's details. Quick checklist items from the infra side:

1) Peering model: we support both VPC peering and PrivateLink-style private endpoints. If Acme's outbound proxy blocks traffic, PrivateLink (AWS NLB + interface endpoints) is usually more robust for control-plane + telemetry.

2) Route tables: we need the target route table IDs where the peered routes will be created and confirmation that route propagation is acceptable.

3) Security groups: please confirm which ports you want open to the Redwood subnets. Minimum: 443 (control plane TLS), 10250-10255 if you plan to allow metrics/agent scraping (optional).

4) If they plan to use our on-pr...

cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>; Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>; Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Private deployment: networking kickoff and info requests

3) Week of 2026-03-17 — Network change window (we tentatively hold 2026-03-17 22:00-00:00 PST).
4) 2026-03-18 — Post-change validation: connectivity tests, token sync, telemetry checks.

Action items for your team: share route table IDs and confirm whether hosts behind the outbound proxy can reach our control-plane endpoints (api.redwood-inference.com) over TLS 443. If they cannot...

Maintenance window: we can confirm Tue 2026-03-17 22:00-00:00 PST. NetOps will join the call. We'll also provide a jump host IP for pre-change connectivity tests.

Hi Diego — thanks for checking. BrightMed is fine participating in a short, off-the-record briefing with Gartner. I've signed the NDA and attached the signed PDF.

Best windows for us:
- Wed 17 Mar 14:00–14:30 PST (works well)
- Thu 18 Mar 09:00–09:30 PST (backup)

We'll have our Head of AI Ops (Martin K.) join for the engineering details and myself for the business outcomes perspective. Let me know what preparation materials you need.

Thanks,
Lucia Moreno
VP, Digital Transformation | BrightMed Health
lucia.moreno@brightmed.com

Attachments: BrightMed_NDA_signed.pdf (application/pdf)

messages.subject: Re: Request: BrightMed consent for 3/15–3/19 analyst briefing (Gartner)
messages.body:
Thanks all — great to hear BrightMed is willing to help. Attached is a 1-page briefing agenda and a short doc with suggested talking points. Plan: 10-minute customer context + 15-minute engineering Q&A + 5-minute close.

Suggested items for Lucia and Martin:
- Platform topology and deployment (private control plane + on-prem inference cluster)
- Typical 99th-latency and average tokens/seq for the workflows discussed
- Observability: which Redwood Console dashboards you check daily and an example of a regression we caught with alerts
- Cost story: before/after numbers and how batching/caching helped unit economics

I'll also capture 2 anonymized latency graphs to share during the call. If ...

messages.subject: Re: Request: BrightMed consent for 3/15–3/19 analyst briefing (Gartner)
messages.body:
Priya / Lucia — thanks for confirming. Booking Wed 17 Mar 14:00–14:30 PST. Zoom link below and we'll send a calendar invite shortly.

Zoom: https://zoom.us/j/123456789
Pre-reads: https://redwood.app/drive/briefing-brief.pdf

Lucia — thanks again for signing the NDA. We'll circulate Alex's one-pager and the short question set so you have the prompts up front. Diego will send the calendar invite and attendee list.

If anything changes, ping me directly. Appreciate BrightMed's help on this.

Best,
Vanessa Ortiz
Enterprise Account Exec | Redwood Inference
vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: none

title: Lumenlane: sudden latency spike + 504s — urgent
body:
Jacob Morris <jmorris@lumenlane.com>: Rachel,

We started seeing a sudden spike in tail latency on our primary inference calls to your hosted endpoint (model: redwood/opt-13b) around 07:50 PT. Error rate climbed to ~15% (504 / gateway timeout) and p95 jumped from ~180ms to ~2.2s. This is affecting our checkout fraud checks and creating significant user impact. Our engineers see a pattern of backend TCP resets and some DNS lookup failures in the client...

Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>: Marissa / Ben — forwarding urgent customer thread. Jacob reports elevated 504s + p95 spike starting ~07:50 PT. They see TCP resets and DNS failures on their side. Packet capture attached.

High level: Lumenlane is on Dedicated (us-west) with peering through our transit partner (EdgeOne). Their traffic profile is steady; this looks like an intermittent path or resolver issue rather than model slowd...

messages.subject: Lumenlane production — sudden latency spike + 504s
messages.body:
Rachel,

We started seeing a sudden spike in tail latency on our primary inference calls to your hosted endpoint (model: redwood/opt-13b) around 07:50 PT. Error rate climbed to ~15% (504 / gateway timeout) and p95 jumped from ~180ms to ~2.2s. This is affecting our checkout fraud checks and creating significant user impact. Our engineers see a pattern of backend TCP resets and some DNS lookup failures in the client logs.

Immediate asks:
- Can you confirm whether anything changed on your side (deployments, routing, peering updates)?
- Any recommended mitigations while you investigate (fallback models, region routing, temporary throttling)?

I attached a short packet capture and our SLO das...

messages.subject: Fwd: Lumenlane production — sudden latency spike + 504s
messages.body:
Marissa / Ben — forwarding urgent customer thread. Jacob reports elevated 504s + p95 spike starting ~07:50 PT. They see TCP resets and DNS failures on their side. Packet capture attached.

High level: Lumenlane is on Dedicated (us-west) with peering through our transit partner (EdgeOne). Their traffic profile is steady; this looks like an intermittent path or resolver issue rather than model slowdown. They are on an active deal (HL-2345) and are pretty sensitive here — Vivek, flag for exec awareness (they're pushing for credits if this drags).

Please triage:
- Marissa: run BGP/peering checks, verify recent eBGP flaps or route changes for the affected POPs. Check proxy pool and connection...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Lumenlane production — sudden latency spike + 504s
messages.body:
Rachel / team — taking ownership of network triage. Initial findings (08:38 PT):
- No planned infra deploys in the last 24h.
- EdgeOne reports transient BGP withdraws for one transit peer at 07:46–07:53 PT that correlated with increased AS path length for traffic from Lumenlane ASN to our us-west POP.
- Our DNS resolver pool saw elevated latency (average resolve 700ms for a 5m window) likely due to a misconfigured health check ping upstream.
- Proxy queue depth on proxy-az-west-3 hit 85% briefly; we tripped a backpressure heuristic that routed new sessions to a slower fallback proxy pool in us-west-2 for ~6 minutes.

Immediate mitigations applied (08:35–08:45 PT):
- EdgeOne applied emerge...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Lumenlane production — sudden latency spike + 504s
messages.body:
Thanks for the quick triage and the clear mitigation steps. Jacob — I appreciate your patience. We'll keep Lumenlane informed and are prepared to offer a one-time credit if the final RCA shows the impact originated on our transit/peering layer (we have margin in the pilot SLA).

Next steps on my side:
- Rachel: prepared a short customer-facing summary and timeline for exec review.
- I'll escalate to SVP Sales and Ops with the ticket link (NET-9812) for visibility.

Jacob — we'll send a consolidated update by 12:30 PT with root-cause and proposed credits / goodwill compensation. If Lumenlane needs direct escalation to our VP of Customer Success, let me know and I'll loop them in.

Cheers,
...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Lumenlane production — sudden latency spike + 504s
messages.body:
Appreciate the fast response and the interim fixes. Our SREs confirm the transient BGP withdraws and the resolver latency in their trace align with what you reported. The checkout path is stable now; error rates returned to baseline after ~09:00 PT.

Please keep me posted on the EdgeOne RCA and any compensation details. We'll want a written summary for our internal postmortem.

Thanks,
Jacob
CTO, Lumenlane

High-level results (baseline vs Redwood inference runtime):
- Median p95 latency reduced from 320ms -> 190ms (≈40% improvement)
- Cost per 1k inferences decreased ...

Really appreciate the clear recap and the deck — review went well on our side. Quick notes: the latency and cost numbers match what our infra team measured during the steady-state runs.

Happy to provide a short two-line quote and join a quick call to confirm wording. I do need to run any public attribution by our legal team, but that should be fine. Liam (cc'ed) will he...

messages.body:
Hi Samira —

Thanks again for partnering with us on the two-week POC. I wanted to share a compact wrap-up and a quick ask that would help our launch efforts. Attachment: poc_results_deck.pdf (5 slides) which includes the test harness and dataset notes.

High-level results (baseline vs Redwood inference runtime):
- Median p95 latency reduced from 320ms -> 190ms (≈40% improvement)
- Cost per 1k inferences decreased ~3x after batching + prefix cache
- Sustained throughput improved 2.5x on the same GPU profile
- No functional regressions observed across test prompts; see slide 4 for examples

Would it be possible for you to share a two-line quote we can use in a short case snippet? Something ...

1) 200 GPU-hour top-up (one-time) at a 15% discounted rate off our list pilot-topup price + surge priority routing for the 3-week window. We’d include a brief SLA addendum (p95 latency target aligned to your current baseline) for the top-up period.
2) Extend the timebox by 3 weeks — no...

messages.subject: Pilot capacity increase + pricing ask
messages.body:
Hi Rachel —

Quick context: the ML team ramped up a new batch of production-ish tests this week and we’re burning through the pilot capacity faster than expected. We still want to finish the defined eval but need either a short top-up of concurrent capacity for 3 weeks OR a 3-week extension to the timebox.

Two asks:
- Can you send pricing for a one-time top-up (extra 200 GPU-hours) vs a time extension (3 weeks) and the net cost/discount you could offer?
- If we do the top-up, can Redwood guarantee the same latency profile (p95) for the top-up window? Our infra team will need an SLA note for procurement.

We’d like to decide by EOB Monday if possible.

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Patel
Head of Ap...

messages.subject: Re: Pilot capacity increase + pricing ask
messages.body:
Hi Samir — thanks for the heads up and congrats on the fast progress.

High-level options we can offer quickly:
1) 200 GPU-hour top-up (one-time) at a 15% discounted rate off our list pilot-topup price + surge priority routing for the 3-week window. We’d include a brief SLA addendum (p95 latency target aligned to your current baseline) for the top-up period.
2) Extend the timebox by 3 weeks — no additional GPUs reserved, same monthly pilot rate, plus a 10% goodwill credit on the incremental usage during the extension.
3) Hybrid: small top-up (100 GPU-hours) + 2-week extension + 10% discount on the top-up.

I’m looping Ben for the technical confirmation on latency and Marissa for approval ...

messages.subject: Re: Pilot capacity increase + pricing ask
messages.body:
On Fri, Jan 9, 2026 at 11:04 AM Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai> wrote:
> I’m looping Ben for the technical confirmation on latency and Marissa for approval on the commercial side (discount authority). I’ve attached a pricing worksheet and a short SOW addendum draft.

Samir — quick technical note: the top-up (option 1) can be routed to the same Dedicated cluster family you’re on. We can preserve the p95 latency target by prioritizing your queue and allocating burst GPUs. We’ll need a rough daily QPS profile for the 3-week window to size the burst policy; the CSV Samir attached helps but if you can share expected peak hours that’d be ideal.

Also: if InsightAI wants guaranteed isolation ...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Samir Patel <samir.patel@insightai.com>
messages.subject: Re: Pilot capacity increase + pricing ask
messages.body:
Thanks team — I reviewed the numbers from the worksheet. A few commercial constraints to flag: our standard pilot uplift discount cap is 15% without executive approval. For anything >15% we need VP sign-off and likely a revenue commitment.

Offering we can approve today (no exec sign-off required):
- Option 1 (200 GPU-hours top-up): 15% one-time discount if InsightAI signs the addendum and pays the top-up invoice within 10 days. We’ll include a temporary SLA clause matching current p95. Reserved capacity surcharge applies if you want dedicated isolation.
- Option 2 (3-week extension): 10% credit on incremental usage during the extension, payable via standard invoice terms.

If InsightAI w...

messages.subject: Re: Pilot capacity increase + pricing ask
messages.body:
Thanks everyone — appreciate the quick review. Option 1 with the 15% discount works for us. We’ll sign the addendum and push the top-up invoice through procurement. Two small asks before signature:
1) Can you include the SLA sentence as plain text (single paragraph) that our procurement team can drop into the purchase order?
2) We prefer to have the reserved isolation (safety) for the top-up window — please include the reserved surcharge line item.

If you send the DocuSign link and the final addendum I’ll run this across procurement.

Best,
Samir

messages.subject: Re: Pilot capacity increase + pricing ask
messages.body:
Samir — thanks, agreed. Attached is the SOW addendum (InsightAI-Pilot-SOW-v2.pdf) and the pricing workbook with the reserved-surcharge line (insightai-pricing-breakdown.xlsx). The SLA paragraph we’ll include in the addendum reads: "For the 3-week top-up window Redwood Inference will prioritize InsightAI traffic and targets a p95 generation latency not to exceed the baseline measured during the pilot. In the event of unplanned infra constraints Redwood will notify InsightAI and provide remediation steps; reserved capacity will be used to maintain the stated target."

I’ve initiated the DocuSign envelope here: https://www.docusign.com/Signing/?ti=EXAMPLE-INSIGHTAI-ADDENDUM — please route to...

title: On-prem tiers, billing runway and migration credit — Acme RFP
body:
Tom Blake <tom.blake@acmehealth.com>: Ava —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. Attached is a short excerpt from our RFP (Acme_RFP_2026.pdf). Two asks from our procurement + infra teams:

1) We need a commercial summary that separates the one-time on-prem license (if any), the recurring platform fee (monthly/annual), and estimated per-token or per-inference metering. Our finance lead needs line items for capex vs opex justification.

2) We're parti...

Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>: Tom —

Appreciate the RFP excerpt and timeline. I'll pull together a concise commercial and a support-tier mapping and get that to you by EOD Friday. Quick questions while I assemble:

- Do you prefer pricing presented as (A) annual committed spend with an amortized platform fee, or (B) metered + smaller platform fee? We can show both.
- Is the pilot chargeable under the same commit as prod, ...
