Ava —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. Attached is a short excerpt from our RFP (Acme_RFP_2026.pdf). Two asks from our procurement + infra teams:

2) We're particularly interested in understanding whether Redwood will consider a migration / onboarding credit to offset professional services and initial GPU churn. If you have a simple rule (e.g., X% credit up to $Y with a minimum commit), share it.

Timing: Acme's fiscal Q3 starts July 1; ...

messages.body:
Tom —

Appreciate the RFP excerpt and timeline. I'll pull together a concise commercial and a support-tier mapping and get that to you by EOD Friday. Quick questions while I assemble:

- Do you prefer pricing presented as (A) annual committed spend with an amortized platform fee, or (B) metered + smaller platform fee? We can show both.
- Is the pilot chargeable under the same commit as prod, or do you want a separate pilot allowance?
- Any constraint on invoice cadence (monthly vs quarterly vs annual)? You mentioned FY start — we can align billing to July if a signature happens before 7/15.

I'll loop in our solutions lead (Vivek) and commercial lead (Rachel) to propose a clean option set...

Forwarding Acme's RFP excerpt and Tom's note. They want: a one-page commercial separating license / platform fee / metering, and a clear migration/onboarding credit rule. Target sign by 7/15 if we want to align with their FY.

Quick requirements pulled from the RFP: pilot -> prod phased rollout; 24/7 tier-2 coverage during pilot; stricter SLA for prod; likely GCP us-west region for the pilot.

Suggested asks from our side that we should confirm:
- Commit bands: $250k, $500k, $750k annual committed spend — each with tiered discounts to list price.
- Platform fee: option to amortize an annual platform fee ($X) into the monthly invoices vs. invoice as a single annual line.
- Migratio...

to: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP
messages.body:
Folks —

Thanks for the context. Below is a pragmatic commercial structure that balances Acme's capex/opex asks and our unit economics. Numbers are rough — I can refine if you want a playbook table.

1) Commitment bands (annual committed spend):
 - Band A: $250k/year — list discounts: 10% off hosted list; platform fee $40k/year (or $3.3k/mo amortized).
 - Band B: $500k/year — list discounts: 18%; platform fee $30k/year (or $2.5k/mo amortized).
 - Band C: $750k+/year — list discounts: 25%; platform fee $20k/year (or $1.7k/mo amortized).

2) Pilot treatment:
 - Offer a 60-day pilot with up to $25k usage credit that does not count against the annual commit if they sign within 45 days of pilo...

messages.subject: Re: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP
messages.body:
Tom —

Thanks for the detail and timeline. Per our conversation and internal alignment, attached is a concise commercial summary and proposed support mapping for the Acme pilot -> prod path. Highlights below:

- Commitment bands (annual committed spend): Band A $250k (10% off hosted list), Band B $500k (18%), Band C $750k+ (25%).
- Platform fee: option to amortize over 12 months (we'll reflect both amortized and upfront invoice rows in the attached PDF).
- Pilot: 60-day pilot with up to $25k usage credit. Pilot credit will be waived from the commit if contract is executed within 45 days of pilot completion.
- Migration / onboarding credit: conditional credit up to 25% of PS fees (capped a...

- Do you prefer pricing presented as (A) annual committed spend with an amortized platform fee, or (B) metered + smaller platform fee? We can show both.
- Is the pilot chargeable under the same commit as prod, ...

messages.subject: Pilot exchange: phased testcase & promptpack access
messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

Thanks again for the run-through yesterday. BrightCart is keen to run Redwood against a small, representative set of our customer support prompts and testcases before the pilot. We want to avoid a full dataset handoff up front for compliance reasons.

Proposal (high level):
- Phase 1 (week 0-2): provide a ~500-row prompt sample + limited testcases (read-only) in a sandbox with token limits.
- Phase 2 (week 3-6): expanded promptpack + additional testcases under an MPA with usage bands.
- Phase 3 (post-acceptance): full evaluation corpus on contract/escrow terms.

Can you confirm Redwood's constraints around ephemeral usage, token retention, and whether we can get a MAP/acceptan...

messages.subject: Re: Pilot exchange: phased testcase & promptpack access
messages.body:
Clara — thanks, this is a great approach. Attaching a draft MAP and a staircase-access checklist that maps to the three phases you outlined. Highlights below:

- Ephemeral access: Phase 1 and Phase 2 artifacts are provisioned to a sandbox with ephemeral credentials; KV/prefix caching is disabled for those lanes. Retention window is 7 days for Phase 1 (extendable to 30 days in Phase 2 by agreement).
- Usage bands: we’ll cap token consumption per day for Phase 1 (25k tokens/day) and Phase 2 (200k tokens/day). We’ll include cost estimates per band in the MAP.
- Acceptance gates: we propose two primary gates — 1) QA pass rate >= 92% on prioritized testcases, 2) no PII surfaced by our automate...

messages.subject: Re: Pilot exchange: phased testcase & promptpack access
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel / Clara — quick operational notes from the test harness side:

- For Phase 1 we will provision a sandbox project (eu-west/us-west as preferred region — please confirm).
- We will accept the sample promptpack as a tarball (sample_promptpack_v2.tar.gz). Please provide a manifest.csv with the input/label mapping.
- We will run the first automated privacy scan within 48 hours of ingest. If any flagged items appear, we will quarantine the specific records and provide remediation guidance.

Gating checkpoints (operational):
- Day 3: ingest complete + privacy scan report
- Day 7: first QA run completed + pass-rate summary

If BrightCart needs earlier retention than our default 7 da...

messages.subject: Re: Pilot exchange: phased testcase & promptpack access
messages.body:
Clara — quick note on commercial and docs:

- I’m sending a Phase 1 MPA (low-value, time-boxed) via DocuSign. It covers the access bands above and includes the acceptance gates Rachel described. Link: https://app.docusign.com/BC-7832/lease
- Phase 1 pricing: flat trial fee $6,500 (covers infra & ops for the 2-week window) + token overage at $0.0006/token beyond the 25k/day cap.
- If procurement needs contract language edits, we can accept redlines limited to SOW and schedule; no changes to export controls or security commitments.

I’ll hold the e-sign send until you confirm the regional preference and manifest.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

messages.subject: Re: Pilot exchange: phased testcase & promptpack access
messages.body:
Ben / Rachel — thanks. A couple of procurement asks before we sign:
1) Can you clarify the exact deletion proof we provide after Phase 1? Procurement wants an irrevocable confirmation that ephemeral rows were removed (hashes or a small script output).
2) We may need a short NDA addendum for specific customer PII rows (we’ll flag them in the manifest).

Regional preference: US-WEST for latency.

If you can add the deletion proof language as a short clause in the MPA, procurement will likely sign quickly. Also, what’s the turnaround on DocuSign redlines?

Best,
Clara

messages.subject: Re: Pilot exchange: phased testcase & promptpack access
messages.body:
Clara — we can do both asks. Quick summary and updated asks: 

- Deletion proof: after Phase 1 takedown we’ll provide a signed deletion statement and a short script output showing SHA256 hashes of the removed files (no plaintext). We’ll include a retention & deletion audit record in the MAP.
- NDA addendum: we can include a 1-paragraph addendum that restricts Phase 1 rows flagged as PII to the ephemeral lane and disallows retention beyond the agreed window. Marissa will mark the manifest rows as "PII-flagged" and we’ll treat them with the 3-day ephemeral option unless you request otherwise.
- DocuSign turnaround: Ben can accept minor redlines within 48 hours; anything material will requir...

Quick ask: who must be on the call for the first hands-on session (technical deep-dive vs procurement intr...

Appreciate the options. For the initial session we need: myself (customer engineering), Matt (infra lead), and security lead (security.lead@novagrid.com). Procurement only needs to join the session where we discuss timing and purchase cadence — not the technical deep-dive.

Can we avoid anything that starts before 07:00 PT for our EMEA folks; they prefer mornings GMT. Ideally the first call is a lon...

Hi Emma / Matt —

Thanks again for the interest and for lining up your cross-functional attendees. To make this productive for everyone (AE + SE + your engineers + procurement + security), I sketched two options for a rotating demo cadence that hits reasonable overlap windows across the US / EU / APAC teams.

Quick ask: who must be on the call for the first hands-on session (technical deep-dive vs procurement intro)? Knowing that will help us pick the right time slot.

Option A — Weekday rotation (short, targeted):
- Session 1 (Americas-friendly): 10:00 PT / 13:00 ET / 18:00 GMT
- Session 2 (EMEA-friendly): 08:00 PT / 11:00 ET / 16:00 GMT
- Session 3 (APAC-friendly): 22:00 PT / 01:00 ET (...

messages.subject: Re: Hands-on demo: aligning windows across US/EU/APAC — NovaGrid
messages.body:
Sergio —

Appreciate the options. For the initial session we need: myself (customer engineering), Matt (infra lead), and security lead (security.lead@novagrid.com). Procurement only needs to join the session where we discuss timing and purchase cadence — not the technical deep-dive.

Can we avoid anything that starts before 07:00 PT for our EMEA folks; they prefer mornings GMT. Ideally the first call is a longer tech deep-dive (60–75m) then we can do a shorter procurement sync later in the week.

If you can propose 2 specific slots that match that constraint, I’ll coordinate internally and confirm.

Thanks,
Emma Nguyen
Principal Cloud Engineer — NovaGrid
emma.nguyen@novagrid.com

messages.subject: Re: Hands-on demo: aligning windows across US/EU/APAC — NovaGrid
messages.body:
Thanks Emma —

Based on the constraint (no < 07:00 PT for EMEA), two concrete options that work from our side for a 75-minute hands-on:

- Option 1: Wed 25 Mar, 11:00 PT / 14:00 ET / 19:00 GMT — good EMEA evening, tolerable for APAC w/ early morning local attendees
- Option 2: Thu 26 Mar, 08:30 PT / 11:30 ET / 16:30 GMT — EMEA late afternoon, Americas morning

I’ll include a suggested runbook in the calendar invite: 15m intro + 45m deep-dive + 15m Q&A + 5–10m action items.

Also: attaching the agenda and the mutual action plan with suggested owners; I added two optional handoff slots (30m) after each demo so that SEs can capture follow-ups and procurement can join the shorter sync.

Attac...

messages.subject: Re: Hands-on demo: aligning windows across US/EU/APAC — NovaGrid
messages.body:
Hi Sergio / Lauren —

Procurement here. For the procurement sync later this week we need the following before the call: standard pricing PDF, a short note on data residency/retention defaults, and the redlines we typically expect (term lengths, liability cap). Can you drop those in a shared folder and confirm negotiable vs non-negotiable items?

We’ll aim to join a 30–45m slot after the technical demo — local availability looks best Friday 27 Mar at 08:00 PT / 11:00 ET / 16:00 GMT. If that conflicts with Redwood's runbook, flag alternatives.

Best,
Procurement Team
procurement@novagrid.com

messages.subject: Re: Hands-on demo: aligning windows across US/EU/APAC — NovaGrid
messages.body:
Hi all —

Thanks for the clarity. We can accommodate both constraints. Final suggested plan:

1) Technical deep-dive (75m) — Wed 25 Mar, 11:00 PT / 14:00 ET / 19:00 GMT — Host: Alex (SE)
2) Procurement + terms (30–45m) — Fri 27 Mar, 08:00 PT / 11:00 ET / 16:00 GMT — Host: Lauren (Sales Ops)
3) Weekly 30m handoff (standing invite) — rotating ownership (Redwood / NovaGrid) to capture action items and QA

I created the three calendar invites (rotating instances) and attached the runbook. If anyone needs the times adjusted by 30 minutes to fit local calendars, reply with preferred offsets and we’ll adapt.

One note on security: Alex, please prepare a short slide listing the SOC2 scopes and da...

messages.subject: Re: Hands-on demo: aligning windows across US/EU/APAC — NovaGrid
messages.body:
Emma / Matt / Team —

Great — confirmed: we’ll run the 75m technical deep-dive tomorrow (Wed 25 Mar) at 11:00 PT / 14:00 ET / 19:00 GMT. Lauren, calendar invites look good and I accepted the rotating handoff.

Action items I’ll own before the demo:
- Finalize the demo environment so session is hands-on (Alex + Ben)
- Upload SOC2 scope + retention note to the shared drive (Alex)
- Drop standard pricing and redline summary to shared folder for procurement (Lauren)

If anything changes, I’ll send an update with a short buffer window. Looking forward to it.

Thanks,
Sergio
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
sergio_alvarez@redwoodinference.com

Calendar invites: CalendarInvite_Rotation.ics

Quick recap and one request from our side: ShopFlux would like to add a lightweight product-recommendation reranking path into the trial scope in addition to the chat/search intents we discussed. The reasoning: we expect the rerank path to be a low-latency, high-value lift for search-to-cart conversion and it exercises a slightly different token pattern.

Key asks from Shop...

Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>: Thanks Olivia — really helpful recap. Short answer: we can support adding the rerank use case mid-POC with a slight re-baseline and an abbreviated eval window. High-level proposal below; I attached an updated POC plan (redwood-poc-plan-v2.pdf) and a small scope-change patch.

Proposed changes (summary):
- Scope: add rerank path with the following acceptance gates:
 * Quality: relative top-10 click upli...

messages.subject: Post-kickoff items — add rerank use case?
messages.body:
Alex — thanks for the call this morning. Quick recap and one request from our side: ShopFlux would like to add a lightweight product-recommendation reranking path into the trial scope in addition to the chat/search intents we discussed. The reasoning: we expect the rerank path to be a low-latency, high-value lift for search-to-cart conversion and it exercises a slightly different token pattern.

Key asks from ShopFlux: 
- Add rerank as an explicit POC use case (sample traffic ~2k requests/day).
- Success criteria: top-10 relevance lift >= 6% over current model in our A/B; model response P95 latency <= 120ms; inference cost delta < 1.5x.
- Ownership: we can deliver anonymized event feed an...

messages.subject: Re: Post-kickoff items — add rerank use case?
messages.body:
Thanks Olivia — really helpful recap. Short answer: we can support adding the rerank use case mid-POC with a slight re-baseline and an abbreviated eval window. High-level proposal below; I attached an updated POC plan (redwood-poc-plan-v2.pdf) and a small scope-change patch.

Proposed changes (summary):
- Scope: add rerank path with the following acceptance gates:
 * Quality: relative top-10 click uplift >= 5% (we propose 5% as a minimum threshold given short eval window)
 * Latency: P95 <= 120ms (same as your ask)
 * Cost: inferred token cost delta <= 1.6x (to leave buffer for quantization variants)
- Eval approach: run a 7-day live A/B on a sampled 20% of traffic for rerank, plus an off...

messages.subject: Re: Post-kickoff items — add rerank use case?
messages.body:
Alex — thanks, that plan looks sensible. A couple of clarifications: 
- ShopFlux can accept the 5% interim threshold for the mid-POC add, but we'd like Redwood to run a second, short verification sweep at the 6% bar before final handoff.
- We'll provide the catalog slices and an anonymized event feed by EOD Wed (2025-11-05). We prefer S3 staging (same bucket we used for the demo).
- One additional ask: can Redwood include a basic compliance checklist for any PII/data handling related to the event feed? Our security team will want that before we put data into staging.

If you can confirm the compliance checklist and final timeline, we’ll mark the HubSpot deal as 'POC scope updated'.

Thank...

messages.subject: Re: Post-kickoff items — add rerank use case? (infra note)
messages.body:
Quick infra check-in and ETA:
- We can stage the catalog in ShopFlux's S3 bucket and pull into our staging cluster with read-only credentials. Ben will coordinate the connector (ETA: 48 hours after we receive credentials).
- Throughput/latency: to meet P95 <= 120ms we recommend using the 4-bit quantized variant for the rerank model with dynamic batching tuned to 8-12 requests per batch (benchmarks attached in the PDF). That keeps cost increase near the 1.5x target.
- Compliance checklist: I've attached a short (one-page) handling checklist we can include in the staging handoff (scope-change-patch.docx). It enumerates allowed fields, masking guidance, and retention window (30 days for the ...

Thanks again for the demo last week. As we begin legal procurement, our procurement and SRE leads have pushed back on a credits-only SLA remedy for repeated high-latency incidents given the scale of traffic we plan to route. Acme is willing to make a 12‑month committed spend, and in exchange we are asking Redwood to accept a bounded, cash-based safety mechanism beyond credits. High-level as...

Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>: Jane — thanks for the clear write-up and for sharing the snapshot. We appreciate the seriousness of Acme's request and the commitment you're offering. High-level response: Redwood is open to a bounded, cash-centric remedy in parallel with credits, but we'll need to structure it to preserve predictable risk and operational fairness across customers. A few immediate points and questions: 

1) We can conside...

messages.subject: Service Continuity Guarantee — escrowed safety pool & graduated repurchase proposal
messages.body:
Marcus / Redwood team,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As we begin legal procurement, our procurement and SRE leads have pushed back on a credits-only SLA remedy for repeated high-latency incidents given the scale of traffic we plan to route. Acme is willing to make a 12‑month committed spend, and in exchange we are asking Redwood to accept a bounded, cash-based safety mechanism beyond credits. High-level ask: 

- An "escrowed safety pool": Acme funds an escrow equal to 3 months of committed spend at contract start.
- Trigger & remedy: If monthly P95 latency exceeds the SLO threshold for 3 of 4 consecutive weeks, Acme may draw against the escrow per a graduated repurchase schedule (...

messages.subject: Re: Service Continuity Guarantee — escrowed safety pool & graduated repurchase proposal
messages.body:
Jane — thanks for the clear write-up and for sharing the snapshot. We appreciate the seriousness of Acme's request and the commitment you're offering. High-level response: Redwood is open to a bounded, cash-centric remedy in parallel with credits, but we'll need to structure it to preserve predictable risk and operational fairness across customers. A few immediate points and questions: 

1) We can consider an escrowed safety pool or a capped cash buyback. Our legal/finance typically prefers a capped-liability model with severity tiers, cure periods, and objective measurement.

2) Measurement & scope: we need to agree exact metric (P95 latency on tenant-scoped API path), sampling period, a...

messages.subject: Re: Service Continuity Guarantee — escrowed safety pool & graduated repurchase proposal
messages.body:
Marcus / Marissa —

Thanks for the quick reply. Per your points:

- Measurement: Agreed to tenant-scoped P95 on the 'generate' route (regionalized; we only route to us-west). We can accept Redwood telemetry with 5-business-day export windows for audit.
- Cure window: 7 business days is acceptable.
- Cap: Acme's preferred cap is 6 months committed spend (we proposed 6x to match business risk). If Redwood can only accept a lower multiplier, we need a clear trade (e.g., longer escrow term or stricter routing SLAs).
- Escrow: preference is neutral custodian (we can propose our bank's escrow agent) but a Redwood-segregated reserve is acceptable if Redwood can provide monthly balance statements...

messages.subject: Re: Service Continuity Guarantee — escrowed safety pool & graduated repurchase proposal
messages.body:
Jane — appreciate the clarity and the committed timeline. Next steps from Redwood: 

1) We'll circulate a redlineable short-form addendum today that includes two options: (A) neutral-custodian escrow; (B) Redwood segregated reserve. The addendum will include: scope (tenant/API path/region), measurement definition, 7-business-day cure, graduated draw schedule (severity tiers), cap (we'll show a 3x and 6x example), and replenishment/renewal mechanics.

2) Rachel will run the exposure model and confirm whether we can accept 6x for the initial term or whether we need a lower multiplier with additional operational commitments.

3) Marissa will update the commercial sheet to reflect any potenti...

title: Re: Dedicated conversion credits & sizing plan
body:
Sophie Lang <sophie.lang@neohealth.ai>: Hi Karthik,

Thanks for taking the time to speak yesterday. As discussed, NeoHealth is evaluating a migration from our current hosted usage on Redwood to a Dedicated GPU pool (private VPC). Before we loop procurement I need a clear mapping of how our existing hosted credits would convert to Dedicated commits and what bridge coverage you can offer while we finalize an annual commit.

messages.subject: Production degradation overnight — immediate assistance requested
messages.body:
Hi Rachel / Redwood team,

We had a severe degradation between 02:30–03:15 UTC this morning where requests to the model endpoint for our realtime scoring pipeline experienced 10–12s tail latency and several timed-out requests leading to failed transactions. This hit our fraud scoring pipeline (P1 impact).

I've attached a small bundle of logs and traces (logs_20250618.zip). Our initial postmortem indicates a KV cache miss storm when a rolling deploy started during the low-traffic window. The net effect was elevated token latencies and a small increase in error rates.

We're asking for two things immediately:
- Confirmation on short-term mitigation steps and an on-call contact for the next...

to: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Production degradation overnight — immediate assistance requested
messages.body:
Thanks Morgan — sorry you hit that. Acknowledged and taking lead.

Summary of state:
- We see a cache warming/eviction pattern in the traces Ben pulled; correlates to a small rolling-worker restart during the window.
- Impact: elevated tail latency (P1 for AlphaFin) and ~0.6% request errors during the 45-minute window.

Immediate asks I need from internal:
1) Ben — can you stand up a short-term bridge (on-call overlap) model: two engineers covering 22:00–04:00 UTC for next 48 hours, with automatic paging to internal P1 list?
2) Irene — we need an SLA carveout/addendum drafted that (a) documents the temporary bridge, (b) clarifies credit eligibility if we miss the temporary guardrails, and...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Production degradation overnight — immediate assistance requested
messages.body:
Confirmed — I can put the bridge on for the next 48 hours starting tonight. Operational notes and constraints below:

Operational plan (short-term):
- Coverage: 22:00–04:00 UTC (two responders, primary/secondary) for 18–20 Jun nights (48 hours).
- Actions: enable aggressive prefix/KV warmup on restart, bump replica readiness checks to avoid immediate traffic ingress, enable temporary request-level retry with jitter for the AlphaFin route.
- Paging: existing P1 paging -> PST on-duty -> escalate to Infra (Irene) if not acked in 30m.

Conditions & approvals needed:
- Approval from Irene on fall-back plan that allows us to failover AlphaFin to a reserved dedicated pool should latency re-spike...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Production degradation overnight — immediate assistance requested
messages.body:
Thanks — appreciate the quick responses and the bridge being stood up. We accept the temporary plan with the following asks before sign-off:
- A short written addendum (one page) that lists: exact bridge hours, escalation ladder with contact names/numbers, and the credit-trigger threshold if latency/timeout reoccurs.
- Confirmation that Redwood will proactively failover to a dedicated pool only after an internal approval (Irene) and with a planned warmup to avoid the same cache storm.

We can sign the addendum; please send the document and we will route to procurement. Also, please include an estimate for any potential charges if the dedicated-pool fallback is invoked.

Call notes: 10:30 ...

- Cost volatility on peak days: current per-1M-token spend fluctuates and makes forecasting hard.
- Many short-lived requests (chat + small completions) so batching is underutilized; current batching kills la...

messages.body:
Cassandra — thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our call so we have aligned next steps and success criteria. I've kept this high-level; happy to expand any section.

Pain points you described
- Cost volatility on peak days: current per-1M-token spend fluctuates and makes forecasting hard.
- Many short-lived requests (chat + small completions) so batching is underutilized; current batching kills latency for real-time flows.
- KV/prefix cache not in use across most endpoints due to multi-tenant routing constraints.
- Procurement needs a predictable pilot SOW and an estimate of cost delta vs current infra.

Current stack (as you described)
- Self-hosted endpoints on GCP (K8s...

messages.subject: Re: Recap: inference cost & pilot scope for Hexware — workload economics
messages.body:
Rachel — thanks for the clear recap. A few quick data points to help with the manifest and sizing:
- Average request payload (prompt only): ~85 tokens; average response length ~120 tokens.
- 75% of requests are under 128 tokens.
- Peak sustained QPS observed during business hours: ~150–180; nightly low ~10–20.
- Embeddings traffic spikes after batch index jobs; embeddings are sensitive to throughput but not strict latency.

Quantization: we are open to int8 for inference as long as it passes a quality baseline. 4-bit is a maybe; procurement will require a side-by-side evaluation on a representative prompt set.

Procurement lead: Jordan Myers (jordan.myers@hexware.ai) — loop them in for SO...

cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Myers <jordan.myers@hexware.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Recap: inference cost & pilot scope for Hexware — workload economics
messages.body:
Appreciate the quick turnaround, Cassandra — this is perfect. Confirming items and next steps on our side:

1) Quantization: noted — we'll run an int8 baseline for the pilot and include a 4-bit evaluation only if the int8 results meet your quality bar. We'll include clear regression metrics in the pilot report.

2) Procurement contact added (Jordan) — Jordan, welcome. We'll attach a short SOW and an NDA to the HubSpot deal (link below) and route the SOW via DocuSign once we have your confirmation on the pilot window.

3) Data/sample: we'll use the S3 placeholder you gave for the initial upload. Jonas, can you coordinate the ingestion scripts and give them the manifest format?

Action item...

title: RetailCo: 502/503 surge tied to upstream proxy — customer impacted
body:
Tom Hayes <tom.hayes@retailco.com>: Rachel —

We started seeing widespread 502/503 responses on our checkout routes around 08:50 PST. Customers are reporting timeouts and duplicate charges due to client retry behaviour. Attached is a small sample of errors and a brief trace excerpt (trace-excerpt-10feb.txt).

High-level:
- Error rate: ~12% 5xx across /checkout and /confirm endpoints between 08:50–09:05
- Latency: median p95 +1.2s, spikes to 8–12s fo...

Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>: Tom —

Thanks for the heads up and the samples. We’re escalating immediately. Monica and our on-call infra team (Vivek, Jonas) are looped in. Initial ask from our side: can you share the request IDs and any customer-facing timestamps for a few failing requests? That helps map traces across systems.

Immediate next steps (internal):
- Vivek/Jonas to validate whether this is originating from our upstre...

messages.subject: Spike in 502/503s — retail checkout errors (started 08:50 PST)
messages.body:
Rachel —

We started seeing widespread 502/503 responses on our checkout routes around 08:50 PST. Customers are reporting timeouts and duplicate charges due to client retry behaviour. Attached is a small sample of errors and a brief trace excerpt (trace-excerpt-10feb.txt).

High-level:
- Error rate: ~12% 5xx across /checkout and /confirm endpoints between 08:50–09:05
- Latency: median p95 +1.2s, spikes to 8–12s for affected requests
- Appears to coincide with a cluster of upstream connection resets to fastproxy-10.8.2.4

Can you confirm whether you’re seeing anything on Redwood’s side (inbound latencies or proxy failures)? Our engineers are standing by; this is impacting a rolling release...

cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Spike in 502/503s — retail checkout errors (started 08:50 PST)
messages.body:
Tom —

Thanks for the heads up and the samples. We’re escalating immediately. Monica and our on-call infra team (Vivek, Jonas) are looped in. Initial ask from our side: can you share the request IDs and any customer-facing timestamps for a few failing requests? That helps map traces across systems.

Immediate next steps (internal):
- Vivek/Jonas to validate whether this is originating from our upstream proxy pool or an external dependency.
- Monica to coordinate customer comms and confirm scope of impacted customers.
- I’ll prepare an incident statement and route to our execs if we need credits/SLA action.

We’ll reply with findings within 30 minutes.

—Rachel
Account Executive, Redwood I...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Spike in 502/503s — retail checkout errors (started 08:50 PST)
messages.body:
Folks —

Quick findings from the on-call surface:
- We’re seeing a burst of upstream connection resets originating from the proxy pool node fastproxy-10.8.2.4 (10.8.2.4).
- GCP LB healthchecks for that host showed transient failures starting at 08:48 and recovered at ~09:10. During that window our runtime observed increased queue times and a retry amplification pattern (clients re-sending while backend returned 5xx).
- Sample log (redacted):
 [2026-02-10T08:49:02Z] upstream_connect_reset remote=10.8.2.4 id=abc123 route=/checkout status=502
 [2026-02-10T08:49:04Z] request_timeout id=abc125 ttfb=0ms

Hypothesis: an external proxy node experienced transient networking or TCP-level instabilit...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Spike in 502/503s — retail checkout errors (started 08:50 PST)
messages.body:
Executing the recommended mitigations now. Quick notes:
- I’ve marked fastproxy-10.8.2.4 UNHEALTHY in the routing pool; traffic is draining.
- Applied account-level retry budget: max-1 retry, backoff 100ms for RetailCo routes.
- Throttling rule pushed to edge(temporary): 60rps per-account burst.

Early telemetry (09:28): 5xx dropped from 12% -> 2.1%, latency returning toward normal p95. We’re keeping an eye on duplicate-order reports.

One outstanding item: RetailCo is asking for remediation costs / a goodwill credit for customer-facing impact. Monica and Rachel, do we want to propose a credit? If so, I’ll prepare the technical impact notes for finance/legal.

Jonas
On-call Engineer, Runt...

to: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Exec note: RetailCo incident impact + recommended customer resolution
messages.body:
Markus —

We had a production incident affecting RetailCo between 08:48–09:10 PST today. Summary: transient upstream proxy instability (fastproxy-10.8.2.4) caused connection resets; client retries amplified the failure, producing a peak 5xx rate of ~12% on critical checkout endpoints. Mitigations were applied (host marked unhealthy, retry budget, edge throttling) and error rates are back to baseline.

Impact:~1.7% of RetailCo's customer traffic saw failed checkout attempts during the window. RetailCo has reported duplicate-order concerns; they are triaging customer charge/rollback issues on their side.

Recommendation for customer resolution:
- Offer a one-time goodwill credit: 25% of Feb...

messages.subject: Re: RetailCo: 502/503 surge tied to upstream proxy — customer impacted
messages.body:
Rachel —

Thanks for the quick response and the mitigation steps. Our telemetry aligns with what you reported — error rate is back down and checkout throughput is stable again. RetailCo accepts a 25% goodwill credit and would like the joint post-mortem tomorrow at 10:00 PST.

Please include your traces and the short remediation timeline in the PM. Also copying our platform lead to make sure we coordinate database rollbacks if needed.

Tom Hayes
Senior SRE, RetailCo

Attachments: retailco-5xx-sample-0800-0900.csv

messages.body:
Rachel,

Thanks for the proposal and the SOW — we reviewed internally. One open item: clinical availability is mission critical for several of our staging environments and the board is pushing for a 99.99% uptime target in the contract.

I know your standard commercial SLA is 99.9% with standard service credits. Can Redwood consider a firm 99.99% uptime for production (paying for a 12-month commitment) with a cap on credits at no more than 10% of monthly fees?

If Redwood can sign to 99.99% and the credit cap, we’re prepared to move to a pilot next week and expedite legal. Otherwise we may need to keep evaluating alternatives.

Best,
Elaine
Dr. Elaine Smith
VP, Clinical Ops, Zenith Health...

messages.body:
Rachel — good writeup. A few notes from deal desk perspective:

1) 99.99% is materially different — that’s ~4.38 hours of downtime/year -> significant exposure on credits. We need a hard cap and a remediation playbook in the SLA.
2) Finance will want revenue protections for prepayment; if they commit 12 months we can be flexible on cap but not unlimited liability.
3) Proposed counter: Approve a conditional 99.95% production SLO (annual) with the following binding terms:
 - Credit cap = 5% of monthly fees for any single month, aggregate cap = 12% for the contract year
 - Formal remediation plan where we get 30 days to implement fixes before credits apply beyond a threshold
 - Customer must...

messages.body:
Dylan — quick ask for executive sign-off. Summary below:

Customer: Zenith Health (clinical ops) — potential expansion to enterprise pilot across 3 product lines.

Hi Rachel —

Quick context: HeliosPay is going live with payments ML features next month. We expect a staggered rollout across three regions; first batch is US production pilot on March 3. Key asks: 
- Temporary rate limits for 3/3–3/10 (target sustained...

Irene: can you confirm product constraints (max sustained per-tenant qps, recommended model fallback, cache warm-up guidance)?

If we can get a rough yes/no today, I'll finalize the warm-up...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: HeliosPay launch week traffic profile and warm-up
messages.body:
Thanks for the details — I reviewed the workbook. TL;DR: feasible with a few guardrails.

Constraints & recommendations:
- Per-tenant sustained QPS (recommended): 25 QPS on m-medium model family; sustained above that requires dedicated pool or lower-latency SKU.
- Bursts: we can support bursts to 80 QPS for short windows if we place them on the burst pool and enable pre-warm (30–60m) with prefix cache priming.
- Canary strategy: 5–10% traffic routed to canary variant for the first 30 minutes after each cohort flip. Add a rollback trigger if 5xxs >1% or median latency > 600ms for 5 consecutive minutes.
- Fallback model: use gpt-lite-10b as fallback for heavy tails; it reduces per-token cos...

2) Pool assignment: reserve 2x burst nodes (n1-burst-8) for the US pilot window and enable pre-warm starting Mar 2 18:00 PDT.
3) Monitoring & alerts: ops will enable elevated traces for the canary and add alerts per Irene's rollback thresholds. Ops on-call is cc'd.
4) Ops handoff doc: Rachel/Priyom please add the hourly ramp slices into the sheet and add the signed temporary exhibit (consumption dates + limit). I'll only push the quota once the exhibit is uploaded an...

Hi Tom / Priya —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so everyone has a single source of truth.

Call summary (30m):
- Skyward's primary goal: improve semantic search relevance for short user queries (~3–10 tokens) across ~12k product documents; current recall@10 feels inconsistent across categories.
- Constraints: strict PII handling (no raw export outside AWS us-west-2), target latency <= 60ms p99 for single-vector queries, and pilot budget constrained to small-run proofs.
- Current infra: ElasticSearch + legacy dense vectors (768d), limited monitoring for drift.

Preliminary hypotheses / what we want to test in the pilot:
1) Increasing embedding dimensi...

messages.subject: Re: Search quality hypotheses and demo plan
messages.body:
Thanks Vanessa — good notes. A couple quick questions before we lock the deep dive: 

1) Dimensionality concerns: can you quantify the expected index size change and rough p99 latency impact moving from 768d -> 1024d? We host in Elastic + a managed vector DB and need ballpark numbers for procurement.
2) Drift monitoring: what signals does Redwood surface for detecting semantic drift vs. natural category shift? We want to avoid chasing false positives when catalog mix changes weekly.
3) Data residency / PII: you mentioned pilot data can stay in our VPC — can you confirm no embeddings or raw text will be copied to Redwood's hosted environment during the pilot?

Alan (procurement) — looping ...

cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Alan Chu <alan.chu@skyward.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Search quality hypotheses and demo plan
messages.body:
On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 9:14 AM Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@skyward.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Vanessa — good notes. A couple quick questions before we lock the deep dive: 
> 
> 1) Dimensionality concerns: can you quantify the expected index size change and rough p99 latency impact moving from 768d -> 1024d?

Good Q. Very rough ballparks (depends on quantization and index type):
- Raw float vectors: ~33% larger storage going 768->1024.
- With OPQ + 8-bit quantization: size delta typically ~10–18% depending on codec.
- Typical p99 query latency delta (vector lookup only) on comparable hosts: +5–12ms for 1024d vs 768d; end-to-end p99 including kNN re-ranking depends on infra but we expect to stay under ...

to: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@skyward.ai>, Priya Desai <priya@skyward.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Search quality hypotheses and demo plan
messages.body:
Vanessa / Tom — quick note from the engineering side with proposed evaluation checklist we can run during the pilot (technical detail):

Evaluation checklist (pilot):
- Sampling: stratified 10k docs into train/holdout and ensure category proportions match production.
- Baselines: compare (A) current 768d legacy vectors (Elasticsearch), (B) Redwood-embed-small-v2 (768d), (C) Redwood-embed-dense-v3 (1024d).
- Tests: recall@10, MRR, per-category delta, paired bootstrap for significance (alpha=0.05).
- Dimensionality checks: PCA explained variance (first 20 components), average silhouette score across category clusters, and pairwise distance distribution plots to detect concentration.
- Quant...

We'd like to lock Tue 5/19 09:00 PT for the tech deep dive. Alan — if procurement is ok to start a short SOW for a pilot (30-day retention, VPC-hosted, fixed pilot fee), please advise and we'll move forward.

Tom

--
Tom Hargreaves
Head of Search, Skyward Analytics
tom.h@skyward.ai | +1 (206) 555-8742

We're interested in the idea of splitting the red team across two focused sprints (thin-slice sprint A: auth/SSO & infra; sprint B: core model infra and API surfaces) to limit blast radius and let our teams remediate between waves. A few quick questions before we loop procurement: 

- Pricing: can you break the SOW into per-sprint line items (hours + flat travel/coordination fee) so we can budget incremental spend?
- Evidence handling: we need an evidence playbook that states how artifacts are transferred, retention timeline (90 days preferred), and redaction process for customer data. Can you confirm you support encrypted artifact bundles delivered t...

2) Data handling: please add explicit language around PII redaction and an acceptance process for redactions (we need a hashed manifest + an on-call contact for post-release clarifications).

Thanks Luke — perfect, appreciate the CSV. I’ll take the 11:30–12:15 PT slot on Tue May 5. Call agenda (high level):

1) Quick restatement of workload profile (5m)
2) Warm-cache behavior and KV cache assumptions (10m)
3) Sizing walk-through with rough cost/throughput tradeoffs (20m)
4) Q&A / next steps for a POC or Dedicated pool proposal (10m)

Prep items for Quest: if you can share a small sample trace (headers anonymized is fine) or an SLO matrix, that will let me bring an initial pool recommendation. I’ll post the calendar invite with a Google Meet link and include the HubSpot deal id.

—Marcus

marcus_lin@redwood.com

One ask from our infra lead: can the sizing call also touch on cold-start behavior (start-up latency for infrequent model variants) and any recommended autoscaling buffers? We run a couple of low-traffic models that still need sub-second cold start on first token.

If you can include a sentence or two in the calendar description about items we need to provide, that will help us come prepared. Looking forward to the call.

—Priya
Priya Shah
ML Infrastructure Lead | Quest Analytics
priya@questanalytics.com

1) Target SLOs you requested we qualify:
 - p95 E2E (request → final token): 120ms (interactive \"lite\" path)
 - p99 E2E: 350ms (for normal streaming workloads)
 - Sustained throughput target: ~250 req/s with 8 concu...

Ellen Gray <ellen.gray@bluepraxis.com>: Thanks Rachel — that matches our notes. A couple clarifications: 

- Our p95 target of 120ms is for the end-to-end path including our CDN and ingress rules. If you measure before our edge we’ll need to add a deterministic offset — is that ok?
- Streaming concurrency: our typical sessions keep connections open ~12–15s and chunks average 18 tokens every 300ms. Burst pattern is mostly 40% of traffi...

messages.body:
Hi Ellen / Sam —

Great conversation earlier — thanks for walking through the BluePraxis latency envelope and streaming use cases. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have the same checklist: 

1) Target SLOs you requested we qualify:
 - p95 E2E (request → final token): 120ms (interactive \"lite\" path)
 - p99 E2E: 350ms (for normal streaming workloads)
 - Sustained throughput target: ~250 req/s with 8 concurrent streaming sessions per second

2) Primary unknowns we agreed to validate:
 - Whether the hosted API can meet p95 at the 250 req/s envelope with your real token distributions
 - Streaming concurrency behavior when many open KV/longer context windows coexist
 - Tail amplifica...

messages.subject: Re: BluePraxis SLO probing — post-call action items & test artifacts
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel — that matches our notes. A couple clarifications: 

- Our p95 target of 120ms is for the end-to-end path including our CDN and ingress rules. If you measure before our edge we’ll need to add a deterministic offset — is that ok?
- Streaming concurrency: our typical sessions keep connections open ~12–15s and chunks average 18 tokens every 300ms. Burst pattern is mostly 40% of traffic in 10-minute spikes during morning syncs.

We’ll fill out the traffic template and share by 7/9 EOD. Also, please include a short list of the exact kernel/hardware categories you’ll run the hosted-check on — we want to know GPU family and region.

Ellen
Director of Platform Engineering
BluePraxis...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: BluePraxis SLO probing — post-call action items & test artifacts
messages.body:
Rachel / team —

Thanks for the notes. Quick engineering read: the two biggest risks for hitting p95=120ms at 250 req/s are (1) streaming multiplexing pressure on the KV cache and (2) prefix-based batching skew when token lengths vary. Proposed technical plan:

- Run a hosted shadow harness across two kernel families: A100-like and H100-equivalent setups in us-west and us-east to show both latency envelope and tail behavior.
- For streaming, we'll instrument per-chunk RTTs and report token-level latencies in the CSV.
- We'll run two traffic slices: steady-state 250 req/s for 30m and a spike window matching the 10-minute morning delta from BluePraxis.

Prep ask for BluePraxis: provide toke...

to: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Ellen Gray <ellen.gray@bluepraxis.com>

messages.body:
Nice — that plan works. Ben, please include the quantized run (we should show a 16-bit quantized variant) for cost/latency comparison; Ellen, we will report both kernel families and region breakdowns so you can map to your traffic split. 

Logistics:
- Internal shadow run (Redwood-only) scheduled: Thu 7/10 09:00–10:30 PT — we’ll validate harness and upload artifacts by EOD 7/10.
- Customer-facing demo + discussion: Thu 7/10 14:00 PT (Zoom link to follow) — in the demo we’ll walk through the CSV of per-token RTTs and p95/p99 calc methodology.

Ellen — thanks for sending the token distribution; if we see any large tail mass we’ll propose an additional caching tweak.

Regards,
Rachel

See you this afternoon.

Ellen

- RBAC matrix (roles -> permissions) and a brief least-privilege rationale for service accounts.
- Evidence of ...

messages.subject: Re: SIG intake — initial list of access artifacts we need
messages.body:
Hi Elena — thanks for the clear checklist. We can deliver the package and will include a short index (README) mapping each file to the SIG/CAIQ questions. High-level plan below: 

1) Canary bundle (anonymized 10-user sample) by Thu 31 Jul EOD PST — includes: SCIM sample, one SAML assertion sample per role, and an exported RBAC slice.
2) Full package (zipped, with index.md) by Tue 4 Aug EOD PST — includes full SCIM delta (30d) + snapshot, RBAC matrix v2, saml_assertions_sample.zip, provisioning runbook excerpts, and session/MFA metrics.
3) Redaction: we will redact user-visible emails in the canary bundle but keep NameIDs and saml timestamps intact; full package will contain a manifest ind...

messages.subject: Re: SIG intake — initial list of access artifacts we need
messages.body:
Marissa / Elena — quick technical notes on the SCIM export and redaction so expectations match: 

- Export type: we can produce both a full snapshot (CSV/JSON) and a 30-day delta (SCIM change events). The delta is exported as a consumer-friendly JSON array; filename will be SCIM-export-20250728.json for the sample.
- Service accounts: by default our SCIM export includes service account objects; we can tag them with "service:true" in the sample and exclude sensitive secret fields. Please confirm whether you want service accounts omitted entirely from the canary or just flagged.
- Provisioning timestamps: the export contains created/updated timestamps and a provisioning_owner attribute (ema...

messages.subject: Re: SIG intake — initial list of access artifacts we need
messages.body:
Elena / team — quick note from RBAC side: we'll include the v2 matrix and a one-page \"least-privilege rationale\" that highlights scope for service accounts and justifications for elevated roles. For the canary we'll include only role names + permission tags (no user assignments) to avoid exposing account lists. Full RBAC export will include role-to-user mappings in the complete package behind the manifest.

Ben Carter
Customer Security Manager | Redwood Inference

messages.subject: Re: SIG intake — initial list of access artifacts we need
messages.body:
Thanks team — that plan works. Two small asks before you upload the canary bundle: 

1) For the canary SCIM sample please flag service accounts (do not omit) so we can validate provisioning ownership patterns.
2) Please include a tiny README that explains any transformations you applied (PII redaction, omitted attributes).

We'll review the canary quickly and revert with questions by Mon Aug 3. Delivery target Aug 4 EOD is acceptable. Once we confirm the canary we'll send a DocuSign for a data transfer addendum to expedite the full upload.

Best,
Elena Ortiz
InfoSec Lead | AlphaMed

---
Acknowledged: will review canary and follow up

messages.subject: Re: Draft MAP & Stakeholder RACI — OrbitPay pilot (target 2026-05-12)
messages.body:
Quick technical note after reading the MAP: two infra items we should account for that affect the POC provisioning date.

1) Network peering + firewall rules: OrbitPay needs to provide CIDR ranges and any egress restrictions. Setup + validation usually takes 3 business days after we receive info.
2) Test dataset delivery: we need a representative (anonymized) sample to validate tokenization & privacy checks — ideally by 2026-04-08 so we can run full smoke tests.

Given those, I'm suggesting we push "POC infra provisioned" from 2026-04-22 to 2026-04-26 to allow for security scanning and a small buffer. If OrbitPay can provide CIDRs and a point of contact for VPN configuration by 2026-03-24...

messages.subject: Re: Post-signature: Implementation roadmap & MAP for HemisphereAI
messages.body:
Amanda, Rachel —

Thanks — looping in some implementation detail so we can lock the MAP during Monday's sync. I've attached the CanaryTestPlan.md and the EvalPromptSet.json referenced in Rachel's note. Below are the technical gates and evaluation plan I propose.

Canary plan (detailed):
- Day 0 (provision): Dedicated pool sized for expected concurrency + 15% buffer. Baseline telemetry collection (latency distribution, token counts).
- Week 1 (ramp): 5% traffic for 48h, then 10% for 72h. Mid-canary quality review at Day 7 using the eval set.
- Week 2 (stress+validation): extend to 20% traffic for 3 days if mid-canary passes. Run synthetic load to validate autoscaling behavior.
- Rollback p...

messages.body:
Hi all,

Attached is the redacted executive summary from the December 2025 external assessment we performed for Redwood's GreenBank tenant. Attachment: redacted_pentest_summary_2025-12.pdf (contains executive findings and high-level repro steps only; hostnames and exact IPs removed per agreement).

High-level: 3 medium findings, 4 low findings. None of the medium findings provided evidence of lateral movement into GreenBank-managed systems. We flagged one configuration issue related to a container runtime that allowed excessive capabilities on a helper container (PN-2025-047), which Redwood remediated in patch v2025.12.3 (see our remediation note).

We can provide a scoped CSV of findings...

messages.subject: Demo examples: SDK snippets for talk track (pin versions + streaming/tool calling/structured output)
messages.body:
Hey all — aligning on the exact snippets we’ll use in the live demo so we don’t keep re-litigating “which client / which endpoint / which params” every week.

I attached draft snippets for Python + TypeScript. These are intended to match the talk track beats:
1) Chat completions w/ streaming
2) Structured output (JSON) for a “support triage” example
3) Tool calling (simple function) + retry

Proposal (for stability + familiarity): use the OpenAI-compatible endpoint for the core code blocks, but call out Redwood-only knobs as optional headers.

Pinned versions:
- Python: openai==1.54.4 (works with our /v1/chat/completions) + requests for the one curl fallback
- TS: openai@4.67.3

Key rule:...

cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo examples: SDK snippets for talk track (pin versions + streaming/tool calling/structured output)
messages.body:
+1 on OpenAI-compat for the “feel familiar” angle.

Two asks from the SE side:
1) Can we include *one* Redwood-native concept in the code without it getting weird? E.g., a route selector header / routing policy hint. Otherwise the reliability + fallback story feels like it only lives in slides.

2) Please add a 10-line curl fallback for each step (streaming and non-streaming). When someone’s laptop Python env is cursed 5 min before a call, curl saves the day.

Also: can you confirm the exact model identifier we’ll pin for the demo tenant? I want to avoid folks copying “rw-demo-chat-pinned” and then it 404s.

— Markus

---

cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo examples: SDK snippets for talk track (pin versions + streaming/tool calling/structured output)
messages.body:
These look good and are short enough.

Messaging nits:
- In the structured output example, can we avoid “JSON schema” wording on the slide + snippet heading? Some prospects interpret that as “you need to build a bunch of schemas.” Maybe call it “guaranteed structured response” and keep the schema detail in speaker notes.
- Can we include one code line that ties back to “production economics” (batching/caching) without turning the snippet into config soup?

Also +1 to Markus’ point: one small Redwood-only line makes it feel less like we’re just a proxy.

Soojin

---

cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo examples: SDK snippets for talk track (pin versions + streaming/tool calling/structured output)
messages.body:
Got it.

Re: “one Redwood-only line”: we can do it as a header and keep it explicitly optional.

Example (works with OpenAI client — it supports extra_headers):

stream = client.chat.completions.create(
 model="rw-demo-chat-v2025-12-01",
 stream=True,
 messages=[...],
 temperature=0.2,
 extra_headers={
 "x-redwood-route": "demo-chat-primary",
 "x-redwood-cache": "prompt-prefix" # optional: makes the cache story concrete
 }
)

That gives Markus a tangible hook to say “this route has a pinned model + a safe fallback policy behind it” while keeping the code minimal.

On “production economics” — I’d rather not promise that a single header instantly halves cost. The safest line is the cache hi...

cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo examples: SDK snippets for talk track (pin versions + streaming/tool calling/structured output)
messages.body:
This is exactly what I need.

Can we please:
1) Put the pinned versions right in the snippet doc (top) + a one-liner: “do not upgrade for demos without re-running the script.”
2) Confirm the TS streaming snippet doesn’t rely on the event names that changed a couple releases ago. (I got burned last week trying to copy/paste from old docs.)
3) Add one “if streaming fails” note (regional disconnects, etc.) with the quickest workaround (switch to non-streaming request + use Console screenshots).

If we freeze this, I can stop using my Frankenstein version of the code.

Avery

---

cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Demo examples: SDK snippets for talk track (pin versions + streaming/tool calling/structured output)
messages.body:
Yep to all three.

1) I added a “Demo pin” block at the top of both docs:
- Python: openai==1.54.4
- TS: openai@4.67.3
Plus: “Do not upgrade without re-running the demo scenario runner.”

2) TS streaming: updated to the current `for await (const part of stream)` pattern in openai@4.67.x. No legacy event names.

3) Streaming failure workaround: added a “Backup mode” section:
- Switch to non-streaming request (curl + SDK)
- Use the pre-seeded Console dashboards (cost/latency/errors) + annotated screenshots
- Talking point: “streaming is transport-dependent; the core reliability story is route-level fallback + retries.”

Next step: I’ll open a small PR to redwood-examples to keep these snipp...

title: Clarify credit calculation: CDN maintenance overlap and partial degradations
body:
Sarah Lopez <sarah.lopez@acmepay.com>: Hi team,

We saw sustained 5xxs and p95 latency jumps between 07:42-08:10 PT this morning across a subset of requests routed through the us-west region. Our telemetry shows ~18% of requests experienced errors or timeouts during that window. This impacted our checkout flow for ~20 minutes and caused friction with a few merchants.

Can you confirm whether this is eligible for an uptime credit? We noticed a maintenan...

Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>: Acknowledged — looping in infra and SLO leads.

Quick asks from my side:
- Can infra confirm whether Redwood's active maintenance windows overlapped this timeframe?
- Can we produce per-probe availability and the p95/tokenized latency breakouts for 07:30-08:30 PT?
- Any third-party provider notices (CDN, peering) that we should consider for apportionment?

Marissa/Kim — can you triage an...

normalized.subject: Clarify credit calculation: CDN maintenance overlap and partial degradations

messages.subject: Spike & partial failures during morning window (requesting SLA credit)
messages.body:
Hi team,

We saw sustained 5xxs and p95 latency jumps between 07:42-08:10 PT this morning across a subset of requests routed through the us-west region. Our telemetry shows ~18% of requests experienced errors or timeouts during that window. This impacted our checkout flow for ~20 minutes and caused friction with a few merchants.

Can you confirm whether this is eligible for an uptime credit? We noticed a maintenance notice from ClearWave around the same time but it's not clear whether that should exclude the window. Please advise next steps and what data you need from us.

Thanks,
Sarah Lopez
Head of Ops – AcmePay

messages.subject: Re: Spike & partial failures during morning window (requesting SLA credit)
messages.body:
Acknowledged — looping in infra and SLO leads.

Quick asks from my side:
- Can infra confirm whether Redwood's active maintenance windows overlapped this timeframe?
- Can we produce per-probe availability and the p95/tokenized latency breakouts for 07:30-08:30 PT?
- Any third-party provider notices (CDN, peering) that we should consider for apportionment?

Marissa/Kim — can you triage and share an initial timeline/attachment by EOD PST? I want to provide Acme a clearer response with the raw numbers and a proposed credit calculation.

Rachel
Rachel Kim
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com

messages.subject: Re: Spike & partial failures during morning window (requesting SLA credit)
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel — initial findings attached. Summary:
- Redwood probe fleet shows elevated p95 latency and a spike in 5xxs between 07:41:12 and 08:06:54 PT.
- Overall request success rate across probes dropped from 99.97% to 98.12% for that window (we include the CSV extract attached: redwood_probe_log_2026-05-11.csv).
- ClearWave (our CDN) posted a scheduled maintenance starting 07:30 PT; their notice (attached) indicates a subset of POPs had a rolling config change until 07:55 PT.
- However, our edge telemetry indicates an additional 11-minute tail of elevated latency after 07:55 that is internal to our routing layer (likely cache rehydration and failover churn).

Interpretation: partial ...

messages.subject: Re: Spike & partial failures during morning window (requesting SLA credit)
messages.body:
Thanks Marissa — good write-up. A couple of clarifying points from the SLA/contract side:
- The AcmePay contract excludes vendor-initiated scheduled maintenance from creditable downtime only if Redwood had prior notice and routing fallback prevented customer impact. ClearWave's notice was posted to their status page at 07:28 PT; we picked it up in our ingest at 07:29.
- The SLA language allows apportionment when a third-party contributed but didn't fully cause the impact. We generally compute "credit_eligible_fraction = (customer_error_fraction) * (1 - third_party_share)" — but we need a defensible way to estimate third_party_share.

Proposal:
- Use probe distribution across POPs to estim...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Spike & partial failures during morning window (requesting SLA credit)
messages.body:
I reviewed the telemetry and probed POP distribution. Key points:
- ~64% of probe samples that experienced errors were routed through the three affected ClearWave POPs.
- AcmePay's traffic concentration to those POPs was ~58% in the 06:00-09:00 window (their geographic footprint).
- If we apportion by traffic overlap, third-party share estimate = 0.58 * 0.64 = 0.371 (~37.1%).

Given the additional internal tail (11 min), I recommend stamping the main overlap minutes (07:41–07:55) as 75–90% third-party responsibility (conservative), and the tail minutes (07:55–08:06) as 25% third-party. Blended across the full incident window that yields an estimated third-party share of ~42%.

I ran a qui...

cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: RE: Spike & partial failures during morning window (requesting SLA credit)
messages.body:
Hi Sarah — thanks for your patience while we completed the investigation. Summary and next steps below: 

Summary:
- Incident window: 2026-05-11 07:41:12 PT to 2026-05-11 08:06:54 PT.
- Root contributors: rolling maintenance at ClearWave POPs (notice posted 07:28 PT) and subsequent routing/cache churn inside Redwood (11-minute tail).
- Estimated third-party share: ~42% (detailed breakdown available on the Drive link below).

Proposed remediation/offer:
- We propose a prorated credit equal to 42% of the calculated downtime-based credit for the incident window against ACME-3245 monthly commit.
- We will also waive the next scheduled contracted maintenance window penalty for one rostered cha...

body:
Jing Li <jing.li@acme-corp.com>: Hi Sean —

We're seeing sustained high latency (p95 ~ 1.8s) and a spike in 5xx errors from Redwood's endpoints in us-west-2 starting ~08:50 PST this morning. Impact: our customer-facing search queries are timing out and the backlog on our message queue is growing. We attached a short traceroute and application logs showing timeouts to redwood-usw2-01.redwood.net.

Immediate asks: can you confirm whether this is on...

Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>: Jing —

Sorry for the hit this morning. We're prioritizing this. I've opened INC-20260302-4287 and looped in our SRE (Elliot) and your CS contact (Rachel). Elliot will run an initial diagnostics pass and share findings in the next 30–45 minutes.

messages.subject: High latency and 5xx spikes from Redwood in us-west-2
messages.body:
Hi Sean —

We're seeing sustained high latency (p95 ~ 1.8s) and a spike in 5xx errors from Redwood's endpoints in us-west-2 starting ~08:50 PST this morning. Impact: our customer-facing search queries are timing out and the backlog on our message queue is growing. We attached a short traceroute and application logs showing timeouts to redwood-usw2-01.redwood.net.

Immediate asks: can you confirm whether this is on Redwood's side or our network? We need a root cause and ETA for mitigation.

Attaching: acme_traceroute_20260302.txt, app_error_snippet.log

— Jing
Jing Li
Director of Infrastructure, Acme Corp
ops+infra@acme-corp.com

messages.subject: Re: High latency and 5xx spikes from Redwood in us-west-2
messages.body:
Jing —

Sorry for the hit this morning. We're prioritizing this. I've opened INC-20260302-4287 and looped in our SRE (Elliot) and your CS contact (Rachel). Elliot will run an initial diagnostics pass and share findings in the next 30–45 minutes.

Could you also paste the traceroute output directly into the ticket (we'll need full TTLs) and confirm whether you have any firewall/proxy rules that could be asymmetrically routing traffic to our IP block?

I'll keep you updated on the bridge time.

— Sean
Sean Gallagher
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwood.com

messages.subject: Re: High latency and 5xx spikes — initial SRE findings
messages.body:
Jing / Sean —

Quick diagnostics summary (INC-20260302-4287):
- We ran parallel traceroutes from redwood-usw2-01 and from a synthetic client. Both show an unexpected hop through a third-party firewall/proxy owned by Acme's networking partner before reaching your gateway; packet loss spikes where the path transits that box.
- DNS: we observed intermittent NXDOMAIN responses for internal PTR lookups which caused some of our health checks to fall back to public egress and trigger connection resets.
- Behavior: asymmetric routing + intermittent DNS failures correlate with increased time-to-first-byte and occasional TLS handshake failures (manifesting as 5xx on our side).

Attachments: pcap_su...

messages.subject: Fwd: ACME network path degradation — exec attention requested
messages.body:
Dev / Vivek —

FWD: ACME is experiencing production impact. Elliot's diagnostics point to an Acme-network-side firewall/proxy that is causing asymmetric routing and intermittent DNS failures, producing latency and 5xx spikes for their traffic to our us-west-2 endpoints. Acme has a large enterprise contract (deal ACME-ENT-2025-09).

Ask: can we authorize an emergency workaround (temporary route bypass or allowlist on their partner proxy) and approve an SLA credit if needed? Rachel and I will coordinate customer comms. We also need legal/finance to prepare an interim credit (approx. 10% of March committed spend) if the workaround needs to be extended beyond 4 hours.

Attachments: pcap_summa...

messages.subject: Re: Fwd: ACME network path degradation — exec attention requested
messages.body:
Thanks for the heads-up Sean. Good catch from Elliot.

Directive:
- Approve emergency bridge with Acme NOC now. Sean / Rachel, schedule a 30-min bridge in the next 15 minutes and add Dev and Elliot.
- Approve temporary network configuration change (bypass partner proxy for ACME -> redwood-usw2-01) for up to 4 hours while we validate stability. Dev, please coordinate on rollback plan and make the change auditable.
- Finance/legal: prepare a proactive offer of a one-time credit capped at $X (finance to confirm) and a formal postmortem within 72 hours. Neha, please own customer comms with the offer.

This looks like a network path issue on the customer side, but we must move fast to limit bu...

messages.subject: Re: ACME: VPC path degradation causing high latency/5xx — mitigation in place
messages.body:
Jing —

Quick update: per Vivek's directive we coordinated a temporary bypass of the partner firewall/proxy and validated DNS PTR stability. Since the change (13:40 PST) we've observed p95 latency down to 220ms and 5xx rates back to baseline. We've attached a short postmortem draft and the action playbook.

Next steps:
- We'll share a full postmortem (root cause, timeline, remediation, and preventive checks) within 72 hours.
- Neha will follow up on the proactive credit offer and formal terms via HubSpot (deal ACME-ENT-2025-09).
- Recommend a joint network runbook with Acme and their partner to prevent recurrence; propose a 1-hour review next week.

Attachments: postmortem_draft_20260303....

messages.subject: Re: Questions on platform fee treatment + support tiers (Skyline RFP)

Hi Evan —

Thanks for the clear list. Answers below at a high level; happy to jump on a quick call if you prefer.

1) Platform fee CAPEX: We can provide an itemized invoice that many customers capitalize. Redwood treats the platform fee as a one-time professional service / license line item on the invoice; your finance team can depreciate/amortize per your internal policy. We can also spread invoicing into up to 4 installments (e.g., deposit + quarterly draws) to help with cash flow; however, contractually the fee remains due per the payment schedule in the SOW.

2) Support tiers / SLAs:
- Standard: 9x5 email support, P1 response within 8 business hours, no guaranteed on-call.
- Enterpris...

messages.subject: Re: Questions on platform fee treatment + support tiers (Skyline RFP)

Rachel — quick clarifications from finance & legal: 

- Finance prefers amortizing the platform fee over 36 months (helps our CAPEX approval). They asked whether Redwood can provide a single supporting invoice labeled "Platform license & deployment" with the $75k migration credit reflected as a line-item discount.
- Legal (Nichols) would prefer the migration credit applied to the platform fee (instead of monthly credits) — avoids having to reframe the payment schedule in the SOW. They may propose simple language: "Migration credit of $75,000 applied to the platform fee invoice upon successful handover."

Given that, we are leaning toward: amortize platform fee (customer-side), apply migra...

2) Confirmed: migration credit to be shown as a line-item on the platform fee invoice; finance will amortize on their books. Invoice cadence: 40% deposit, 60% on handover.

Nichols: please go ahead and attach the brief redline we discussed.

If you can circulate the redline and final price sheet by end of day Wednesday, we'll route for internal signatures. Appreciate you both — looking forward...

Quick question as we prep our TCO model: in your hosted API ratecard, how are prefix-cache ("warm cache") hits billed? Specifically: if a request matches a cached prefix and the runtime returns from cache only, is the customer charged full input+output token counts, or is there a discounted "cache-hit" token rate?

We expect ~60% of our requests to be identical prefixes (templated prompts + variable us...

Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwoodinference.com>: Evan — thanks for the logs and the clear question. Short answer: cache hits still count toward billed tokens in the hosted API, but there are several levers and commercial concessions we typically use for committed customers to reflect the reduced work on the runtime. Below is a concise breakdown:

normalized.subject: Warm-cache crediting and commit exceptions for prefix caching

messages.subject: Question: when does Redwood charge for prefix-cache hits?
messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

Quick question as we prep our TCO model: in your hosted API ratecard, how are prefix-cache ("warm cache") hits billed? Specifically: if a request matches a cached prefix and the runtime returns from cache only, is the customer charged full input+output token counts, or is there a discounted "cache-hit" token rate?

We expect ~60% of our requests to be identical prefixes (templated prompts + variable user text) after a warmup period. If cache hits still bill full tokens, that changes our commitment math.

Can you share the baseline behavior and whether Redwood provides any credits or discounted units for cache-hit traffic? Also attaching a small sample of recent request logs fo...

messages.subject: Re: Question: when does Redwood charge for prefix-cache hits?
messages.body:
Evan — thanks for the logs and the clear question. Short answer: cache hits still count toward billed tokens in the hosted API, but there are several levers and commercial concessions we typically use for committed customers to reflect the reduced work on the runtime. Below is a concise breakdown: 

- Baseline hosted behavior: token billing is measured on the request as seen by the model runner (input+output). If a request is served entirely from prefix cache (no model execution), the system still records the request and counts billable units at the standard rate.

- Why that happens: our metrics/usage pipeline needs a consistent accounting surface. Cache serving is optimized to reduce la...

messages.subject: Re: Question: when does Redwood charge for prefix-cache hits?
messages.body:
Evan — glad to be in the loop. Based on similar deployments, here's a pragmatic commercial we typically propose: 

- Commit: $120k/month (12-month) for a baseline token rate of $0.0018 per token.
- Cache-hit addendum: up to 40% of billable tokens can be considered "cache-advantaged" and invoiced at a 35% discounted rate (effective $0.00117/token) — this is layered on top of the commit and recognized in the monthly invoice as a separate line item.
- Ramp credit: first 90 days we apply a warm-cache credit equal to the difference between full price and discounted price for cache-advantaged tokens, capped at $25k.

This structure preserves a deterministic invoicing baseline while acknowledgin...

messages.subject: Re: Question: when does Redwood charge for prefix-cache hits?
messages.body:
On Tue, May 13, 2025 at 10:05 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Evan — glad to be in the loop. Based on similar deployments, here's a pragmatic commercial we typically propose: ...

Thanks Karthik — helpful. A couple clarifying points from our side: 

- Expected steady state: ~150M billable tokens/month; ramping from 20M -> 150M over 3 months. Estimated ~55-65% of requests will hit a warm prefix cache after ramp.
- Prefetch behavior: we do a nightly precompute that can generate a large number of server-initiated requests to warm the cache. Would Redwood treat those as normal billable requests? We can tag those calls separately if needed.
- Ramp credit ask: can t...

messages.subject: Re: Question: when does Redwood charge for prefix-cache hits?
messages.body:
Evan — quick finance/legal note: when we agree discounting or credits we need the following in the SOW/Order Form: precise metric (e.g., "cache-advantaged tokens" defined as requests with exact prefix match and 0 model calls), audit log access for verification, and a cap on exclusions. We generally prefer a discounted-line-item approach (Karthik's proposal) over total token exclusions because it maps cleanly into monthly revenue recognition and audit trails.

I've attached a simple billing matrix CSV with examples of line-item math for a $120k commit and 150M tokens/month. Also: if you want the front-loaded ramp credit, we can structure it (larger credit month 1) but the total credit must...

messages.subject: Re: Question: when does Redwood charge for prefix-cache hits? — agreed next steps
messages.body:
Evan — thanks for the logs and the quick replies. To summarize and confirm next steps so legal/procurement have a clear record: 

1) Baseline billing: hosted API bills on request tokens as observed by the runner; cache hits count as billable tokens by default.
2) Commercial concession: Redwood will offer the 12-month $120k/month commit with the cache-advantaged line-item discounted 35% (as Karthik outlined).
3) Ramp/warm-cache credits: Redwood will apply a front-loaded ramp credit schedule: $20k (month 1), $5k (month 2), $0 (month 3). Total credit $25k, applied on invoice 1 then reconciled over 90 days.
4) Prefetch exception: Redwood will review server-initiated prefetch jobs if Nimbus ta...
