messages.subject: Notice orchestration & subprocessor handover map
messages.body:
Hi Marta —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Per our conversation, I wanted to put a lightweight proposal on timeline and roles for incident notices that involve subprocessors and cross-border handover. This is intended to make sure your legal and forensics teams have a practical playbook we can operate against (not contractual language yet).

High-level proposal (attached: redwood_dpa_incident_clause_proposal_v1.docx):
- Initial triage notice: observable incident status and scope, within 6 hours of detection (internal severity >= P2).
- Customer-facing notification (summary of scope + immediate mitigations): within 24 hours of confirmed data exposure affecting customer assets.
- D...

messages.subject: Re: Notice orchestration & subprocessor handover map
messages.body:
Thanks Ava — this is helpful and matches the tone we want. A few clarifying Qs from our side before we loop in internal counsel:

1) For the 6-hour triage notice: do you mean 'detection' by your monitoring, or 'confirmation' after you validate exposure? Our legal team generally expects a notification window that starts on confirmation.
2) For subprocessor handback: what level of PII mapping will you provide in the initial 72-hour disclosure? Full record-level maps can take time; a scoped summary is often fine as long as forensic access is available.
3) Cross-border concerns: our users are largely EU-based — any transfer steps or additional controls we should expect?

Marta
Head of Securit...

to: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Notice orchestration & subprocessor handover map
messages.body:
Ava — quick legal POV before we reply to Marta / legal@healthcorps.io:

- Start of clock: contractually we typically define the notification clock as \"confirmed incident affecting Customer Data\" (i.e., after internal verification that customer data was accessed/exfiltrated). Operationally we can send an early 'holding' triage email at detection to avoid surprises, but C-level timings should reference \"confirmed\" to avoid false-positive liabilities.
- 72-hour subprocessor disclosure: we can commit to an initial subprocessor list and high-level role+region within 72 hours. Full technical index (per-record mappings) is best-effort and dependent on customer's scope and search parameters —...

to: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Notice orchestration & subprocessor handover map
messages.body:
Ava/Rachel — operational notes from forensics/ops side:

- For the 6-hour detection holding: our SOC automation can open an incident ticket + provide initial telemetry within 4 hours of automated detection. Confirmation normally requires at least a minimal manual review; we should be explicit to customers about detection vs confirmation.
- For initial 72-hour subprocessor disclosure: we can provide a table of affected subprocessors with timestamps and the types of processed data (e.g., logs, model cache, user content). We will not provide full raw logs publicly; we will share them through an authenticated SFTP/secure drive with chain-of-custody notes and an agreed scope.
- For EU customer...

cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Notice orchestration & subprocessor handover map
messages.body:
Marta / HealthCorps legal —

Thanks for the clarifying questions. Short answers below; attached is a one-page summary we can hand to your counsel and a suggested operational SLA table.

1) \"Detection vs confirmation\": we will do an automated holding notice at detection (aiming within 6 hours) and a formal customer confirmation when we have validated that customer data is impacted. The contractual clock should reference \"confirmation\"; the holding notice is a courtesy to help you stand up internal triage.
2) \"PII mapping in 72 hours\": initial 72-hour disclosure will include: affected subprocessor names, roles, data categories (e.g., user identifiers, content, metadata), and the provi...

messages.subject: Annual ops playbook: scenario lattice & OKR gap assessment
messages.body:
Team —

Kicking off a lightweight playbook to structure annual planning work across product, eng, GTM, and infra. Goal: give the execs a crisp set of scenarios and a prioritized set of asks for the QBR in 4 weeks. I sketched a first pass (attached). High-level asks: 

- Synthesize OKR gaps (team-level -> company-level) and highlight where we either need more investment or a scope pullback.
- Produce three budget/staffing scenarios: Conservative (cost-first), Base (status-quo), Growth (opportunity capture).
- For each scenario, include critical hires (role, month of ramp), unit cost delta, and two contingency triggers.
- Timeline: pre-read by 2026-06-17, working session week of 6/22, final...

to: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Annual ops playbook: scenario lattice & OKR gap assessment
messages.body:
Marcus —

Appreciate the structure. Two asks from my side: 

1) Please surface the critical hires first (by role and month) — we need a short list of \"must-fill\" vs \"nice-to-have\" so Finance can simulate the cash impact quickly.
2) Add ramp timelines and expected time-to-impact for each hire (e.g., 0-3 months: onboarding only, 3-6 months: measurable feature/throughput contribution). If we can attach a simple FTE-to-revenue/cost metric that would speed approvals.

On contingency triggers: include a very short set of measurable signals (e.g., 10% drop in avg response latency, MRR slip of X) that flip us between scenarios.

I'll ask Strategy to prioritize a headcount run with two FTE pha...

- Can we lock a pre-read deadline of 2026-06-17 COB? I want org leads to sign off before the working session.
- On staffing: call out any hires that require external recruiting partners — those take longer.

If possible, Marcus, can you include a one-line ask per leader (e.g., \"Eng: 3 ICs for model infra by Sep - ask for 3 FTE\") so I can pull that into a short slide.

Thanks —
Lauren

Forwarding my notes — I added a short risk register and a worst-case runbook sketch. See inline comments in the attached CSV. 

Top items I flagged: 
- Capacity risk if we delay infra hires beyond Q3. Point estimate: 12% increase in per-token latency under growth scenario.
- Contracting/renewal timing for a major partner in September; that swings revenue in Growth case.
- Ops coverage for on-call + paging increases with additional model endpoints — may need +1 SRE in the Base case.

Attachments: [Attachment: risk_register.csv (CSV, 34KB)]

Team — escalating Acme AI (enterprise) streaming timeouts/disconnects in us-east. They’re requesting (a) an exec-owned written update they can forward internally, and (b) clarity on potential credits given the customer impact.

I need approvals by 10:00am PT on:
1) Customer-facing language (attached draft) — specifically what we can state re: cause + “resolved” wording.
2) Whether we should proactively offer any goodwill / service credits vs. wait for their formal request + post-incident report.

Summary (details in PDF; ticket: SUP-1842):
- Customer: Acme AI (Corp)
- Symptom: SSE streaming responses resetting mid-generation (connection resets / truncated streams). Non-streaming less affe...

to: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>

messages.subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed
messages.body:
Adding SRE technical color for customer-safe wording.

What happened (customer-safe):
- We saw an elevated rate of mid-stream disconnects for streaming requests in us-east, concentrated on longer-lived connections.
- Non-streaming requests had normal success rates; this was primarily a streaming transport stability issue.

Most likely cause (keep high level):
- An interaction between a gateway config change and connection handling under elevated concurrency in us-east led to increased connection resets for long-lived streaming responses.

What we did:
- Rolled back the relevant gateway config and applied a conservative keepalive/connection-handling adjustment.
- Confirmed streaming reset ...

cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed
messages.body:
+1 to Rafael’s guidance.

On the comms draft:
- Approve if we remove/soften any “we identified the root cause” phrasing. I suggest:
 - “We identified a likely contributing factor in gateway connection handling under load and applied a rollback + configuration adjustment.”
 - “Streaming stability metrics have returned to baseline; we’re continuing to monitor.”
- Keep the “RCA within 5 business days” commitment.

On credits:
- Recommend we do not proactively promise credits in the same note as mitigation.
- Suggest language: “If you’d like to request service credits per your agreement, we can evaluate once we finalize the incident timeline and impact assessment.”

Reason: avoids creating a ...

cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed
messages.body:
Customer context + what they’re asking for:

- Morgan Lee (Acme Eng) is the point; they looped IT Ops because their internal dashboards lit up as “app instability.” They want a note they can forward to their VP Eng.
- They explicitly asked: “Are we going to get credits? We had a customer-visible incident.”
- They also asked whether they should pin traffic away from us-east for now.

My suggestions for the email:
- Include one sentence recommending temporary routing away from us-east *only if* they can do so without violating their internal requirements. Otherwise, we keep the guidance focused on retries/timeouts.
- Re: credits: agree with Sean — don’t promise. But we should reassure we’ll...

cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed
messages.body:
Adding Sales POV.

Acme is in the middle of expanding usage this quarter. If we go too “legalistic” on credits, we may take a hit in the expansion motion.

Question: are we comfortable with a *goodwill* credit (separate from contractual service credits) once stabilized, e.g., a one-time usage credit applied to next invoice, pending Jasmine approval?

I’m aligned with not committing in writing today. But if Morgan pushes on the call, I’d like a clear internal decision tree:
- If they ask verbally: do we say “we’ll evaluate and get back by X date” (what date)?
- Who signs off (Jasmine vs. Finance)?

Avery

cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwoodinference.com>
messages.subject: Re: Exec escalation: Acme AI — streaming timeouts/disconnects (us-east) — approval needed
messages.body:
Thanks all — incorporating edits now.

Decision / approvals:
1) Comms language: approved with Sean + Rafael’s edits.
- We will NOT state “root cause confirmed” or “permanently fixed.”
- We WILL state: mitigation applied, streaming stability metrics back to baseline, continued monitoring, RCA to follow.
- We WILL include temporary mitigation guidance (timeouts/retry; optional HTTP/1.1 note) and keep it explicitly “temporary.”

2) Credits:
- We will not proactively offer credits in today’s email.
- Verbal guidance if pressed: “We take this seriously and will evaluate service credits per your agreement once we finalize the incident timeline and impact assessment. We’ll get back to you by Wed...

messages.body:
Hi Grace,

Thanks again for the walkthrough last week. Our legal team attached a redline of the Order Form and MSA with a few outstanding asks specifically on SLA/support:

- Uptime banding: We proposed a three-tier model with step-down credits kicking in below 99.95% instead of 99.9%.
- Maintenance windows: We requested that monthly maintenance windows be capped at 8 hours and that emergency maintenance be limited to 2 unplanned events per quarter.
- Response times: Tiered response commitments for P1/P2/P3 with a 15-minute P1 response and 2-hour remediation target.
- Service credits: Request to remove the aggregate cap and allow credits to apply per-incident up to 50% of monthly fees.

A...

- Laura/Rachel: ops feasibility on 15-minute P1 response and monthly 8-hour maintenance cap.

I've added a short meeting slot Wed 5/6 10:30am PT to finalize la...

messages.subject: RE: Fwd: Final SLA redline — uptime banding & maintenance carveouts
messages.body:
Thanks Grace — quick summary of recommended positions after syncing with Ops and Finance: see attached 'sla_redline_comments.docx' with inline suggestions. High-level notes: 

1) Uptime & credits
- We can accept a three-band model, but propose thresholds at 99.99% / 99.95% / 99.0% with correspondingly smaller credit percentages. Cyclone's request to trigger at 99.95 (instead of 99.9) is acceptable only if credit multipliers are reduced to protect revenue exposure.
- We cannot remove the aggregate cap on credits. Recommended: per-incident credits subject to a monthly aggregate cap equal to 100% of the monthly recurring fee (MRR). This balances customer protection with runaway exposure.

2)...

cc: Cyclone Health Legal <legal@cyclonehealth.com>, aditya_rao@redwood.com, laura_bennett@redwood.com, rachel_kim@redwood.com, lauren_bishop@redwood.com, markus_klein@redwood.com
messages.subject: RE: Final SLA redline — uptime banding & maintenance carveouts
messages.body:
Hi Elaine — thanks for the redline and for being flexible on timing. Per our internal review (summary below) we can move forward with a consolidated counter-proposal that aligns on both sides and keeps the deal moving.

Summary of Redwood's consolidated positions (high level):
- Uptime: Accept a three-band model. Proposed thresholds: 99.99% / 99.95% / 99.0% with reduced credit multipliers at each band.
- Service credits: We cannot remove an aggregate cap. Propose per-incident credits that apply up to the monthly cap equal to 100% of MRR. This is intended to limit financial exposure while giving Cyclone meaningful protection.
- Maintenance windows: We can commit to a 6-hour monthly schedul...

messages.subject: Re: Executive workshop — availability and travel questions
messages.body:
Alice — great to hear leadership is on board. Wed love to run this. Quick proposal below so your team can start travel approvals and calendar holds.

Proposed week options (please confirm preferred):
- Option A: Tue/Wed Mar 16-17 (preferred by our delivery team)
- Option B: Tue/Wed Mar 23-24 (backup)

Prelim schedule (90% rough):
- Day 1 morning: Exec town-hall (90 min) — Redwood product + SLOs + partnership outcomes

messages.subject: Onboarding stuck at consent step
messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

We started the supplier profile for WSCC Supplies this morning after receiving the Workday invite, but we hit a hard stop on the second screen — the final consent checkbox is visible but the "Accept and Continue" button stays greyed out.

Steps we took:
- Followed invite link 
- Completed company & tax info (W-9 attached on our side)
- Reached final consent screen; checkbox can be toggled but button inactive
- Tried Chrome (latest), Safari, and a mobile browser — same result
- Cleared cookies and retried, no change

I attached a quick screenshot of the page and a console log snippet (wscc-onboard-steps-log.txt) that shows a 403 on POST /portal/consent. Our security team thinks...

messages.subject: Re: Onboarding stuck at consent step
messages.body:
Thanks for the clear repro, Liam — really helpful. Looping in Ben (sales) and Marissa (Procure Ops) + Karthik (Integrations) so we can triage quickly.

Initial ask: if possible can you forward the browser console output (network tab) showing the 403 request headers (particularly Origin/Referer and the request payload)? That will help us narrow whether it's an SSO redirect or a cookie attribute issue.

Short-term workaround we can try: Karthik, would you be able to generate a one-time "direct invite" link that bypasses the SAML/SSO handoff? If we can get them to complete via the sso-less flow we can move the PO. If not, Marissa, can your team reissue the invite from the legacy invite flow?...

messages.subject: Re: Onboarding stuck at consent step
messages.body:
Thanks for the tag, Rachel — quick triage from Integrations/Runtime.

What I see from the logs (we pulled the request trace for Liam's invite id) is a rejected POST to /portal/consent returning 403 with message: "invalid origin". The flow is doing an external redirect from our SAML callback domain (sso.redwood.com) back to the tenant portal (wscc.redwood-portal.com). In several browsers the Referer header is being stripped during the cross-site redirect and our CSRF check (we rely on Referer + token) is rejecting the request. This looks like a SameSite cookie + redirect boundary problem rather than a user error.

Workarounds (ranked):
1) Generate a one-time "sso-less" onboarding link (tok...

messages.subject: Re: Onboarding stuck at consent step
messages.body:
Hi all — quick update. Karthik's direct link worked: I opened the sso-less invite in a private window, completed the consent, and the portal accepted the submission. Profile now shows "Active" and I could view the pending PO #12345.

Action items to close:
- Ben/Marissa: please confirm the vendor is fully visible to finance and that PO routing is enabled (I can see the invoice upload tab but not payment terms)
- Karthik: happy to share the full network trace if helpful for the infra ticket INF-3421

Thanks for the fast help. Please send the PO next steps and any missing paperwork we need to upload.

Best,
Liam
VP Procurement
WSCC Supplies

Attachment: wscc-w9-signed.pdf (application/pdf)

- We need a financial remedy beyond service credits for repeated or prolonged outages. Credits alone don’t make our security and patient workflow risk appetite comfortable.
- Requesting a fee‑at‑risk escrow equal to 15% of the ...

High level response: we want to be helpful and keep the pilot moving, but several of the items you asked for are material changes to our standard commercial posture (especially liquidated damages and escrowed fees).

We can do the following as...

messages.subject: Fee exposure and exit mechanics for pilot - draft asks
messages.body:
Rachel / Marissa —

Thanks again for the demo last week. ClearHealth legal and procurement reviewed the proposed SLA and we have a few non‑negotiable asks before we can sign the pilot SOW:

- We need a financial remedy beyond service credits for repeated or prolonged outages. Credits alone don’t make our security and patient workflow risk appetite comfortable.
- Requesting a fee‑at‑risk escrow equal to 15% of the three‑month committed spend, payable to ClearHealth if SLA tiers are missed per the schedule below.
- If Redwood has more than two P1 incidents (full service unavailability for affected endpoints) within a 90‑day rolling window, ClearHealth must have a right to terminate for conv...

messages.subject: Re: Fee exposure and exit mechanics for pilot - draft asks
messages.body:
Samir —

Thanks for the clear summary and the redline. We received the PDF and the service impact log; appreciate you laying out the requirements. High level response: we want to be helpful and keep the pilot moving, but several of the items you asked for are material changes to our standard commercial posture (especially liquidated damages and escrowed fees).

We can do the following as short‑term proposals while we review with Legal & Finance:
1) Agree a fee‑at‑risk schedule that ties payments to verified missed SLOs (not just incident counts). We propose a three‑tiered sliding scale (minor/major/critical) with a maximum payout per month capped at 30% of committed monthly fees.
2) For t...

messages.subject: Re: Fee exposure and exit mechanics for pilot - draft asks
messages.body:
Samir —

Appreciate the transparency. A few contract‑level clarifications and Redwood counter proposals attached inline in the redline:
- Definition of Downtime/SLO breach: must be tied to our published measurement methodology (rolling 30‑day window, per‑route availability computed from server-side telemetry). Incidents that are customer‑specific misconfigurations or networking issues outside Redwood’s control should be excluded.
- Escrow: we are open to an escrow deposit that acts as a security appliance for the pilot, but propose a modest initial amount (5% of three‑month committed spend) with a step‑up to 10% only if SLOs are missed more than once in the pilot period. The escrow should...

messages.subject: Re: Fee exposure and exit mechanics for pilot - draft asks
messages.body:
Marissa / Rachel —

Thanks for the counter. A few clarifications from our side after legal review:
- We cannot accept an uncapped cure period. For critical, patient‑impacting failures we need expedited termination if remediation is not underway within 7 business days.
- The escrow approach you propose (5% step to 10%) is reasonable as a compromise, provided release criteria are clear and escrow funds are available without additional procurement steps.
- Per‑minute damage calculation is acceptable in principle, but ClearHealth will not agree to a hard cap below 1x monthly committed fees for the pilot; our procurement policy requires at least parity with that number for high‑risk integratio...

messages.subject: Re: Fee exposure and exit mechanics for pilot - draft asks
messages.body:
Samir —

Thanks — we can do Wednesday 08:30–09:00 PST for the call. Quick confirmation of Redwood’s position to make the call productive:
1) We will accept an escrow starting at 5% of the three‑month committed spend, auto‑step to 10% if verified Major or Critical breaches occur more than once in the pilot. Escrow release requires an agreed postmortem and remediation plan signed by both parties.
2) For critical incidents with proven customer impact, we will agree a termination right if Redwood fails to materially remediate within 7 business days after a validated postmortem finds systemic fault — but termination will be effective only after the cure period unless there is evidence of gross...

Hi Rachel,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the latency profile on the embedding pipeline looks promising for our cohort scoring use case. We are in the final stages of procurement but have two items blocking approval from our legal and procurement teams: one commercial and one SLA-related.

1) Commercial: We need a temporary onboarding concession (one-time credit) to offset engineering ramp and data migration costs. Our procurement target is a $35k credit applied on first invoice.
2) SLA: Our security team is asking if you can accept a short-term, lowered uptime target for the first 6 months while we complete the integration (99.90% instead of the standard 99.99%) and to waive serv...

messages.subject: Re: Request: service credit waiver and pricing concession
messages.body:
Katie —

Appreciate the clear outline. We can move quickly on this. High-level response:
- Commercial credit: deal desk can provision a one-time onboarding credit. I will propose $40k to cover migration and incentivize a Q2 close.
- SLA carveout: we can support a temporary uptime tolerance for an initial 6-month pilot if it is documented as a pilot/limited-scope clause and we include an operational remediation plan (weekly runbooks + joint playbook). We cannot permanently change the MA.

Next steps: I am routing to our deal desk and need exec sign-off for the above concessions (commercial + SLA language). Can Marissa and Ben please confirm the recommended credit cap and any precedent conc...

Approve with conditions.

Conditions to approve:
1) Cap onboarding credit at $40,000 total and bill as a contra on the first invoice. No retroactive increases.
2) SLA carveout allowed for a six-month pilot only; service credits waived during pilot. Post-pilot we revert to standard SLA with a 30-day remediation notice if targets not met.
3) Legal must insert a pilot clause that explicitly states: pilot scope, metrics, rollback path, and that no MA term is permanently amended. Legal to sign off on SLA redline (cc'd).
4) Reliability/operations to confirm the joint runbook and remediation checkpoints before countersignature.
5) No other concessions (pricing or extended credits) without VP sal...

messages.body:
Amanda —

Thanks again for the time last week. As we discussed, we want to kick off a short renewal & expansion handshake so we have clear owners, an escalation path, and QBR timing locked before the end of August. Attached is a first-pass timeline I sketched out (renewal_timeline_draft.xlsx). High-level asks:

messages.subject: Re: Post-demo recap: prefix-cache assumptions + budget check
messages.body:
Thanks Jordan — useful recap and the attachments look good at a glance. A few clarifications from our side: 

- The 500k DAU is our steady-state target for FY27 (we'll likely ramp across Q4/1). For initial pilots in Q3 we expect ~120k DAU. Please show both pilot and steady-state rows in the spreadsheet.
- We can cache prompts/prefixes and intent keys; we cannot cache full completion text longer than 1 minute retention unless it is anonymized (legal constraint). So model caching primarily on prefix/pattern matches.
- Procurement (Aaron) asked for a simple three-line table showing effective $/1M at hit-rates 20%, 40%, 60% and a compute/storage split.

Can you please provide those three scen...

messages.subject: Fwd: Post-demo recap: prefix-cache assumptions + budget check — three-scenario costing (draft)
messages.body:
Marissa / Rachel — forwarding Sam/Aaron with the ask. Draft numbers below (internal assumptions noted). Please sanity-check & convert to a customer-facing table.

Quick assumptions used (internal):
- List model cost (hosted list price for candidate model): $18.00 per 1M tokens (this is the list model unit).
- Amortized cache infra & storage per 1M effective tokens (estimated): $0.80 per 1M (includes S3/redis + bandwidth + maintenance amortized over volume).
- Effective cost formula: effective = list_price * (1 - hit_rate) + cache_amortized_per_1M

Scenario calculations (example rows to send to customer):
- Conservative (hit_rate = 20%): effective = 18.00 * (1 - 0.20) + 0.80 = 18.00 * 0.80...

- Prepare the customer 3-scenario quote PDF with: list_price assumptions, the effective $/1M rows we drafted, and footnote explaining cache amortization.
- Include optional commit tiers (monthly commit bands)...

Hi Priya — thanks for the note and for sending the metrics. I reviewed the CSV and can see where the prefix cache and sequence-length variance are causing the spikes.

We’re happy to help you finish acceptance. Two practical options we can offer today:

Rachel — thanks, that sounds reasonable. Averon would prefer Option A (support-first). A couple of small asks before we sign: 

- Would Redwood consider 6 hours of CS delivered as on-site (or remote overlapping with our engineers) and 2 hours as office-hours for follow-up? Remote is fine, but overlap matters (we have east-coast engineers).
- If finance insists on capturing value, would Redwood accept a 10% goodwill credit on the next invoice instead of marking the extension $0? We’d sign faster with that on the table.

If you can confirm those two minor tweaks I’ll route the addendum and get procurement to sign today. Looking forward to the kickoff.

Best,
Priya

title: Evening streaming p99 spike — AcmeCo (SSE)
body:
Carlos Mendez <carlos@acmeco.com>: Hi Soojin,

We started seeing a sharp increase in p99 latency on the streaming chat endpoint (SSE) around 18:50 PT. For the next ~20 minutes p99 jumped from our usual ~320ms to ~1.8s and error rate climbed to ~0.6% 5xx. Throughput stayed similar but connections started getting dropped and some users experienced repeated reconnects.

I've attached a short trace and a snippet of our client-side errors (acme-sample-t...

Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>: Thanks for the report, Carlos — and sorry about the disruption. I'm escalating this immediately and copying the on-call infra team. Quick checklist from our side:

- Can you confirm the SDK version and whether you're using SSE or the WebSocket fallback?
- Any geographic concentration of your users during the spike?

We will run the streaming playbook and provide an ETA for mitigation within 20 mins. ...

normalized.subject: Evening streaming p99 spike — AcmeCo (SSE)

messages.subject: High p99 for streaming chat during evening peak
messages.body:
Hi Soojin,

We started seeing a sharp increase in p99 latency on the streaming chat endpoint (SSE) around 18:50 PT. For the next ~20 minutes p99 jumped from our usual ~320ms to ~1.8s and error rate climbed to ~0.6% 5xx. Throughput stayed similar but connections started getting dropped and some users experienced repeated reconnects.

I've attached a short trace and a snippet of our client-side errors (acme-sample-trace.txt). This is impacting our premium chat users — can you confirm if anything changed on your side?

Carlos
Senior Eng, AcmeCo

cc: support@acmeco.com, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: High p99 for streaming chat during evening peak
messages.body:
Thanks for the report, Carlos — and sorry about the disruption. I'm escalating this immediately and copying the on-call infra team. Quick checklist from our side:

- Can you confirm the SDK version and whether you're using SSE or the WebSocket fallback?
- Any geographic concentration of your users during the spike?

We will run the streaming playbook and provide an ETA for mitigation within 20 mins. I'll post updates here.

— Soojin
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

to: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: High p99 for streaming chat during evening peak
messages.body:
Quick triage notes (preliminary):

- Observed an abrupt connection-concurrency bump for ACME account prefix between 18:48–19:05 PT. Connection rate spiked ~3.1x above baseline (client IPs concentrated in us-west).
- SSE broker queue depth increased; downstream activators hit a temporary backpressure threshold which amplified tail latency (p99) and caused a small number of 503s when the accept queue overflowed.
- No config deploys or kernel changes in the window. No obvious regression in runtime E2E tests.

Immediate mitigations applied:
- Enabled connection-shaping for the account (throttle new connections per second) and bumped burst capacity for the dedicated SSE pool by 25%.
- Activate...

messages.subject: Re: High p99 for streaming chat during evening peak
messages.body:
Carlos — quick update: we applied two mitigations about five minutes ago: connection-shaping + account fallback to short-polling for new sessions. Our metrics show p99 trending back toward normal and error-rate dropping. Can you check your client now and let me know if you still see reconnects or errors?

Planned next steps:
- Keep the temporary burst increase for 1 hour and then evaluate.
- Postmortem + follow-up within 24 hours; will share draft with your team.

Thanks for flagging this quickly.

— Soojin
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

to: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: High p99 for streaming chat during evening peak — exec note
messages.body:
Looping in briefly: good work getting mitigations out. Two asks:

1) Please prepare a one-paragraph exec summary for the CSM/AE and an estimated impact (users affected, minutes of elevated p99, any SLA implications).
2) If we end up keeping the burst increase >1 hour, notify finance ops (potential capacity cost).

I'll be available if we need to brief sales leadership.

— Marissa
VP, Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference

messages.subject: Re: High p99 for streaming chat during evening peak
messages.body:
Thanks Soojin — we no longer see the repeated reconnects and error counts are back to baseline. Will keep monitoring for the next hour and report if anything else shows up. Appreciate the quick response and the playbook.

Carlos

Michael —

- Are we able to accept the EU Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) with an addendum (Acme proposes Art 46 fallback language)?

Attaching the redlines. Jordan (copied) will be the day-to-day...

messages.subject: Fwd: Acme DPA redlines (initial) — need quick privacy/legal review + sproc plan
messages.body:
Forwarding Acme's redlines — initial ask is to get privacy/legal's read and confirm what we can accept without escalation. Rachel FYI for timeline (customer hopes to close by 7/10).

Quick checklist request:
- Monica: can you confirm whether 30-day subprocessor notice is acceptable or whether we should push back to 60/automatic notice + 30-day objection? Also flag any other untenable redlines.
- Naomi: can you pull the latest SOC 2 exec summary + evidence link and advise whether it's safe to share with Acme legal now, or whether we should provide a sanitized summary?

Attachments: Acme_DPA_Redlines_v1.docx

Thanks —
Mike
VP, Sales — Enterprise
Redwood Inference

messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Acme DPA redlines (initial) — privacy review (round 1)
messages.body:
Mike / Rachel —

I reviewed Acme's redlines and summarized below. See attached RI_privacy_comments_round1.pdf for line-by-line notes. High-level positions:

1) Subprocessors / Notice Window
- Acme asks for 30 days. Our standard is 30 days for notice and 15 days for objection; for sensitive subprocessors (security monitoring, hosting) we typically provide 30 days and accommodate reasonable objections. Recommend accepting 30 days notice but keep objection process as-written.

2) EU->US Transfers / SCCs
- We can accept SCCs as the legal basis for transfers. Acme's proposed Art 46 fallback language is broader than necessary; recommend we propose the standard SCCs + a supplemental transfer add...

messages.subject: Re: Evidence for Acme (SOC 2 + docs)
messages.body:
Mike / Monica —

SOC 2 Type II executive summary uploaded to secure drive. Link below is time-limited (7 days) and contains: executive summary, scope, last audit date (2026-04), and the auditor's letter. Sensitive artifacts (detailed control mappings) are omitted — we can provide them under a CDA if requested.

Drive link: https://drive.redwood.com/drive/folders/privacy-evidence-ACME-2026

I'll also provide a short note you can share with Acme legal: "Redwood is SOC 2 Type II audited across our inference and control plane; selective evidence available via secure share on request. We recommend SCCs as the transfer mechanism; Redwood operational controls and contractual terms align with SCC...

to: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@acmesystems.com>, Acme Systems Legal Team <legal@acmesystems.com>
cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: RE: Acme DPA redlines (initial) — proposed compromise + eSign link
messages.body:
Jordan —

Thanks for the redlines and for pulling your counsel in. We reviewed and propose the following compromise to unblock procurement quickly:

- Subprocessors: Redwood will provide 30-days' notice for new subprocessors. Acme will have a 15-day objection window; if Acme raises a reasonable objection, Redwood will engage to either offer a mitigation or postpone onboarding.
- Transfers: Redwood accepts SCCs + a short transfer addendum limited to the term; we will not agree to open-ended Art 46 fallback language.
- Liability carve-out: Redwood will accept a narrowly tailored carve-out tied to gross negligence / willful misconduct, subject to a reciprocal cap (proposed text attached).

W...

cc: Acme Systems Legal Team <legal@acmesystems.com>, A. Patel <apatel@smithlawllp.com>
messages.subject: Re: Acme DPA redlines (initial) — proposed compromise + eSign link
messages.body:
Mike —

Thanks, this looks good and aligns with what our legal team is willing to accept. I forwarded the DocuSign link to our counsel; they signed at 11:02am PT and the envelope shows completed on our side. If Redwood countersigns today we will be good to close for procurement.

Jordan
Director, Procurement
Acme Systems

Attachment: Signed_DPA_Acme_2026.pdf (pdf, 29 KB)

Hi Samir,

Thanks again for interviewing with us for the Backend Engineer role. As discussed, attached is the take-home assignment and a small starter repo to speed you up. A few quick notes:

- Goal: Implement the pagination and filtering endpoints for the sample service, include a short README describing tradeoffs and any optimizations.
- Deadline: Monday, June 15 at 11:59 PM PT (we intentionally keep it short — 72 hours).
- Deliverable: A GitHub repo or zip with instructions to run the service, plus a short write-up (max 1 page).
- Allowed: any JVM or Node runtime (we typically run reviewers' environment on Linux; Docker is acceptable).

Attachments: redwood_backend_assignment_v2.pdf (...

messages.body:
Hi Ava — thanks, I got the files and the instructions. The deadline of June 15 11:59 PM PT works for me.

Quick question: are we allowed to use a lightweight database (SQLite) for the submission or do you prefer an in-memory/mock implementation? I want to ensure reviewers can run the tests locally.

Thanks,
Samir

On Fri, Jun 12, 2026 at 9:14 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Samir,
> 
> [original assignment and attachments omitted]
>

messages.subject: Re: Backend Engineer assignment: prompt, deadline, and reviewer schedule
messages.body:
Hi Samir — great question. SQLite is fine (or an in-memory DB) as long as you include clear steps to run locally and any seed data. If you choose Docker, please include a Dockerfile and docker-compose.yaml so reviewers don't need to install dependencies.

Best of luck — ping me if anything else comes up.

Ava

> Hi Ava — thanks, I got the files and the instructions. The deadline of June 15 11:59 PM PT works for me.
>

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Internal: incoming take-home for Samir Patel — review slots + debrief
messages.body:
Team — Samir (external candidate) accepted the take-home and will submit by Mon 6/15 11:59 PM PT. Please see the prompt I sent (attached to candidate).

Can Marcus and Vivek review the submission? Ideally one focuses on correctness/perf, the other on system design and maintainability. Rachel — would you like to join the scoring call or just the debrief?

Proposed plan:
- Tue 6/16 9:00–10:00 AM PT: review meeting to walk through code + give independent scores (30m), then 30m panel debrief.
- If that slot doesn't work, Marcus/Vivek propose alternatives.

Let me know and I'll send calendar invites and a short rubric.

Thanks,
Ava

Attachments: redwood_backend_assignment_v2.pdf

to: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Internal: incoming take-home for Samir Patel — review slots + debrief
messages.body:
Thanks for the ping. I can do Tue 6/16 9:00–10:00 AM PT. I'll focus on correctness, test coverage, and performance tradeoffs. Can you add a short rubric with the repo link once Samir submits?

Also putting a placeholder in my calendar. Vivek — do you want us to split scoring (backend infra + architecture) or both score independently and average? I prefer independent scoring then short alignment.

Marcus
Senior Backend Engineer
marcus.lin@redwood.com

On Fri, Jun 12, 2026 at 10:12 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — Samir (external candidate) accepted the take-home and will submit by Mon 6/15 11:59 PM PT.
>

messages.subject: Submission: Backend Engineer assignment
messages.body:
Hi Ava and Rachel —

Thanks for the prompt. I completed the assignment and pushed a branch to the repo: https://github.com/redwood-samples/backend-takehome/tree/samir-submission-20260616

Notes:
- README includes steps to run with Docker and a short explanation of approach and tradeoffs.
- I used SQLite for local persistence and included seed data.
- Tests: run `npm test` (or `./gradlew test` if using JVM).

Please confirm you received the submission. Happy to join the review meeting this morning if helpful.

Best,
Samir

>

messages.subject: Re: Submission: Backend Engineer assignment
messages.body:
Samir — received, thanks for the quick turnaround and the clear README. Marcus and Vivek — I've shared the repo and added a short rubric to the calendar invite (Link: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=redwood-review-06016).

Plan for the review meeting (Tue 9:00–10:00 AM PT):
- 9:00–9:30: independent walkthrough and scoring (panel reviews code briefly)
- 9:30–10:00: alignment and hiring decision recommendation to Rachel

Samir — you do not need to attend the internal scoring call. If we have follow-up clarifying questions we'll email or schedule a quick slot.

Thanks everyone — see you on the call.

Ava

--
Ava Chen | Recruiter, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com

- AWS Private Offer: Yes — we can work with the AWS Marketplace team to publish a Private Offer tied to your account. Billing typically flows via consolidated ...

Hi Alyssa — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth for your buying path: 

1) Who's on the committee (roles you mentioned):
 - Economic buyer: Rahul Desai (CPO)
 - Engineering lead: Dr. Mina Shah (not on call today)
 - Security/Privacy: Alyssa Turner (you) + security ops reviewer
 - Procurement: procurement@medtechco.com (TBD contact)

2) Contract sequence we recommend (to minimize latency):
 - NDA (one-way) -> Pilot SOW + DPA (parallel) -> MSA redline -> PO / purchase order
 - Legal owner for NDA: your security team; Redwood legal will be Ben for redlines and Rachel for execution cadence

3) Turn-time targets (our r...

messages.subject: Re: Committee roster and contract clearance play
messages.body:
Thanks, Rachel — this is helpful and aligns with our internal thinking. A few updates: 

- Procurement contact: Chelsea Park (chelsea.park@medtechco.com) — copied. Chelsea owns POs and supplier onboarding.
- Legal contact: Eduardo Ramos (legal@medtechco.com) will be the primary redliner for the MSA. He generally asks for 7–10 business days depending on complexity.
- We prefer DocuSign for signature capture, but legal sometimes asks for a stamped PDF for procurement. We can accept either as long as the flow is consistent.

One question: for the pilot SOW — can Redwood provide a short (1 page) scope + acceptance criteria we can attach to the PO? That will speed procurement reviews.

Chelsea...

messages.subject: Re: Committee roster and contract clearance play
messages.body:
Appreciate the intro, Alyssa — looping in a couple of quick notes from legal so expectations are clear on our side: 

- NDA: our standard one-way NDA should be fine. I’ve attached v1 with two optional protective clauses (data handling + subcontractor notice). If Eduardo wants to redline, we can return within 2 business days for NDA-specific items.
- MSA redline cadence: agree 7–10 business days is typical. If MedTech needs faster turn times we can prioritize during the pilot but will need a signatures agreement and a short expedited redline SLA.
- DPA: we can provide our standard DPA with modular choices for data residency and subprocessors. If you have any preferred clauses, mark them in...

cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Committee roster and contract clearance play
messages.body:
Hi all — thanks for the quick responses. Two follow-ups from our side to keep momentum: 

- Rachel: I’ll draft the 1-page pilot SOW with acceptance criteria and send by EOD today (will add expected pilot timelines and one-week checkpoints).
- Ben: can you flag any non-starter MSA clauses up front so Eduardo has a sense of the big items (indemnity caps, data residency, audit rights)?
- Chelsea: if procurement can send vendor template + preferred PO language, we’ll prepare the vendor intake packet (W-9, vendor application, insurance cert).

I’ve also attached a short clearance play we normally use — it sequences NDA -> SOW + DPA -> MSA and includes the owner and expected turn times per step...

messages.subject: Re: Vendor setup: initial info needed for First Mile Logistics
messages.body:
Hi Alyssa — great, thanks for the quick turnaround and for the W-9. A few clarifications on our PO and payment practice to set expectations:

1) PO requirement: For standard services under $5k we can process invoices without a prior PO; however for recurring shipments and any professional services we typically require a PO before payment. If you flag invoices as urgent, Finance can triage them, but having a PO speeds automated matching.

2) Invoice fields we require for automated processing:
- Redwood PO number (if available)
- Invoice date
- Invoice number (unique)
- Line-item description and unit price
- Remit-to address (must match vendor record)

3) ACH vs wire: ACH is preferred for U...

messages.subject: Re: Vendor setup: initial info needed for First Mile Logistics
messages.body:

- ACH template filled and uploaded to the DocuSign folder (redwood_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf + ach_authorization_template.pdf)
- Bank letter (original) scanned and uploaded as well
- AP contact confirmed: invoices@firstmilelogistics.com (Samir Khan). Phone: +1-619-555-0147
- We prefer ACH for US payments. If Redwood requires PO for the first shipment, please let us know and we will prioritize issuing one in our system.

Also copied our CFO on internal routing — happy to jump on a 20-min call if you want to walk through the PO matching rules to avoid rejections.

Thanks,
Samir Khan
Director, Accounts Payable
Fi...

messages.subject: Fwd: Finexa redlines (need legal review)
messages.body:
Rachel / Marissa —

Forwarding Finexa’s preliminary redlines (attached). High-level notes from the account: Finexa wants to keep April open as their target go-live but counsel flagged data residency and indemnity caps. Can you: 

- Quick read and call out any must-not-accept items?
- Suggest negotiable counters (short bullet list so I can present to Elena)
- Tell me whether we should propose a counsel-to-counsel call or handle via email?

If you both think a short 30-min call between Finexa counsel and our contracts lead will speed this up, I’ll propose times. Appreciate a priority call — the customer has budget windows tied to an April rollout.

Thanks —
Jonas

Thanks for the intro last week. Brightline is finalizing our vendor shortlist and we need a clearer picture of Redwood's premium support packaging before we move to procurement. High-level asks below — can you confirm by EOD Tuesday?

1) Pricing model: retainer vs consumption. For a 12–24 month commitment, what does a hybrid structure look like (base retainer + consumption credits)?
2) Uptime and respon...

Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>: Sam —

Appreciate the RFP excerpt. I sketched a concise commercial approach that balances predictability and flexibility; attaching a one-sheet and an XLS with the structures (RI_Support_Structures.xlsx). Key points up front:

- Hybrid model recommended: a modest base retainer to guarantee a named SLA and escalation roster + per-incident/consumption credits for burst usage. This preserves lo...

normalized.subject: Commercial proposal and support pricing for Brightline

messages.subject: Commercial inquiry: premium support for Brightline
messages.body:
Marissa —

Thanks for the intro last week. Brightline is finalizing our vendor shortlist and we need a clearer picture of Redwood's premium support packaging before we move to procurement. High-level asks below — can you confirm by EOD Tuesday?

1) Pricing model: retainer vs consumption. For a 12–24 month commitment, what does a hybrid structure look like (base retainer + consumption credits)?
2) Uptime and response commitments: sample SLAs for 99.9 / 99.95 / 99.99 and how credits are calculated (minutes vs percentage).
3) Escalation coverage: on-call windows, escalation path to engineering, and typical MTTR bands by tier.
4) Discounting: what concessions are available for a $1.2M ARR com...

messages.subject: Re: Commercial inquiry: premium support for Brightline
messages.body:
Sam —

Appreciate the RFP excerpt. I sketched a concise commercial approach that balances predictability and flexibility; attaching a one-sheet and an XLS with the structures (RI_Support_Structures.xlsx). Key points up front:

- Hybrid model recommended: a modest base retainer to guarantee a named SLA and escalation roster + per-incident/consumption credits for burst usage. This preserves low latency for your burst windows while keeping unit economics aligned to usage.

- Suggested tiers (high level):
 * Foundation (base): 99.9% / Standard 8x5 support, 24-hour acknowledged escalation, 48-72h engineering engagement; best for non-critical workloads.
 * Business: 99.95% / 24x7 monitoring, 4-...

messages.subject: Re: Commercial inquiry: premium support for Brightline
messages.body:
Marissa / Karthik —

Reviewing the sketch: for a $1.2M ARR anchor, finance can offer the following concession framework assuming a 12-month commitment with an option to extend to 24 months:

- Base retainer credit: 8% commercial credit applied to retainer line if Brightline prepays 6 months up front.
- Consumption discount ladder: 3% at $300k, 6% at $600k, 10% at $1.0M (applies to consumption tokens).
- Early-payment incentive: additional 2% off total invoice if full year prepaid via ACH within 15 days of DocuSign.
- One-time onboarding PS credit: waive first 40 hours of SRE onboarding for Enterprise / Mission tiers.

Constraints / notes: discounts are contingent on 60-day cancellation pr...

messages.subject: Re: Commercial proposal and support pricing for Brightline
messages.body:
Marissa —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and to Rachel for the pricing scaffolding. A few clarifying items before we lock in a call:

On the retainer vs consumption split — would Redwood model the base retainer to cover an agreed nominal monthly burst band (e.g., first N token-hours or baseline throughput)? If so, how do you reconcile overage during seasonal peaks?

On credits — Brightline prefers a minutes-based credit model that can be applied either as a service credit or as a reduction of future consumption fees. You mentioned invoice-credit by default; we're fine with that but need the formula in writing (sample calc for 15 minutes of degraded service at 99.95).

We'd like the draf...

Quick notes: Matt's org uses an Exchange tenant that sometimes blocks external ICS auto-accepts; if that's a problem we can add a direct Zoom link in the email as well.

Hi Sean,

Thanks for the quick reply on the RFP — Matt and I can do a 30-minute demo on Thu 21 Jan at 10:00am PT. I attached the calendar invite (enterprise-demo-20260121-original.ics) and included Matt in case he wants to accept.

Quick notes: Matt's org uses an Exchange tenant that sometimes blocks external ICS auto-accepts; if that's a problem we can add a direct Zoom link in the email as well.

Proposed agenda:
- 10:00–10:05 brief intros
- 10:05–10:20 product walkthrough (latency/cost controls)
- 10:20–10:30 Q&A + next steps

Attaching: enterprise-demo-20260121-original.ics (text/calendar)

Cheers,
Sarah
Sarah Jenkins
Director, Platform Partnerships
FinTech Labs

messages.subject: Re: Redwood demo — propose 21 Jan @ 10am PT (ICS attached)
messages.body:
Thanks Sarah — confirmed, 21 Jan @ 10am PT works for us.

Quick heads-up: I've had a few customers where Exchange tenants show the ICS but hide the meeting description (where we normally include the Zoom join). Can you confirm you see the Zoom link in the invite? If not, I'll send a separate email with the Zoom join + passcode.

Also looping in Marissa (AM) and Rachel (Ops) so they can help with an alternate invite if needed — Marissa, Rachel, can you please prepare a fallback (Zoom link + new ICS from our Zoom account) in case Matt's calendar strips the description?

Thanks,
Sean
Sean Gallagher
Account Exec, Redwood Inference

messages.subject: Re: Redwood demo — propose 21 Jan @ 10am PT (ICS attached)

> 
> Quick heads-up: I've had a few customers where Exchange tenants show the ICS but hide the meeting description (where we normally include the Zoom join). Can you confirm you see the Zoom link in the invite? If not, I'll send a separate email with the Zoom join + passcode.

I see the invite on my side and the Zoom link is visible in the description, but Matt forwarded me a screenshot: his Outlook calendar shows the event and the start/end time but the description comes up blank (no join URL). He suspects the tenant policy strips HTML fr...

messages.subject: Internal: fallback ICS + plain-text join link (for Enterprise Corp)
messages.body:
Internal only — do not CC external until ready.

Sean — quick plan:
1) Create a Zoom-hosted meeting from Redwood’s Zoom account with a short passcode.
2) Export a simple ICS that puts the join URL in the event location field and first line of description as plain text (some tenants honor location & plain-text better than HTML body).
3) Also paste the join URL & passcode in the top of the resend email body so recipients without description will still see it.

I created the fallback ICS and saved it as enterprise-demo-20260121-zoom.ics. I also tested by importing into Outlook — the location field shows the join URL, and Outlook desktop shows the passcode in the first line. That should work ...

messages.subject: Resent: Redwood demo — join link + passcode (ICS updated)
messages.body:
Hi Sarah, Matt —

Re-sending the invite with a Zoom-hosted ICS attached and the join link + passcode in the top of the email to avoid tenant stripping.

Join the demo (Thu 21 Jan @ 10:00am PT):
https://zoom.us/j/987654321?pwd=demo-passcode
Passcode: 3271

I've attached enterprise-demo-20260121-zoom.ics (text/calendar). If anyone still can't see the join details in their calendar client, please join via the URL above or reply and Marissa will send an Outlook-friendly meeting file.

Attachments: enterprise-demo-20260121-zoom.ics (text/calendar)

See you Thursday —
Sean Gallagher
Account Exec, Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0192

Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement wants to move Redwood onto our vendor registry but we need to confirm a few AP-side requirements before we can issue POs and enable EFT. High level items below — attaching our vendor packet for reference.

Key asks from Acme AP:
- Full vendor enrollment in our portal (fields include legal entity, remit preferences, bank ACH file format)
- PO acceptance:...

Appreciate the clear list. Looping in Rachel (Finance ops) and Marissa (Sales Ops) to prepare the packet and confirm the split remit/escrow detail. Quick answers to a few items:

- Split remit: we can accept split remits but need the reseller partner legal entity and tax info to appear on remittance statements; Redwood can provide an escrow routing ID if required by...

messages.subject: Supplier registration + PO acceptance requirements for AcmeHealth (self-bill & split remit)
messages.body:
Hi Lauren —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement wants to move Redwood onto our vendor registry but we need to confirm a few AP-side requirements before we can issue POs and enable EFT. High level items below — attaching our vendor packet for reference.

Key asks from Acme AP:
- Full vendor enrollment in our portal (fields include legal entity, remit preferences, bank ACH file format)
- PO acceptance: vendor must accept PO via portal hyperlink or via PO-ack email. We can enable automatic PO-ack at the portal level or accept a signed PO acceptance PDF.
- VAT / tax: provide W-9 (for US entity) and VAT parity information if any EU entities are involved.
- Split remit / escrow fl...

messages.subject: Re: Supplier registration + PO acceptance requirements for AcmeHealth (self-bill & split remit)
messages.body:
Samira —

Appreciate the clear list. Looping in Rachel (Finance ops) and Marissa (Sales Ops) to prepare the packet and confirm the split remit/escrow detail. Quick answers to a few items:

- Split remit: we can accept split remits but need the reseller partner legal entity and tax info to appear on remittance statements; Redwood can provide an escrow routing ID if required by Acme.
- PO acceptance: we prefer portal PO-ack (automated) but can also upload a signed PO-accept PDF.
- Self-bill: this is possible but requires a short SOW-level agreement and sign-off from Legal; would like to understand which invoices Acme expects to self-bill (all invoices or specific SKUs).

Rachel — can you as...

messages.subject: Re: Supplier registration + PO acceptance requirements for AcmeHealth (self-bill & split remit)
messages.body:
Hi all —

Attaching the vendor packet exports and a sample invoice CSV that maps to Acme's PO fields. Summary of what’s attached and clarifications below:

Attachments included:
- W9_Redwood_Bishop_signed.pdf — current US tax form (EIN on first page).
- ACME_vendor_packet_response.pdf — completed portal field mapping (legal name, remit address, ACH details).
- sample_invoice_acme.csv — sample line-level invoice with PO reference, SKU, qty, unit price, tax/VAT column.

Notes / clarifications:
- Escrow & split remit: finance confirms we can accept the split (we will receive a single payment from Acme and programmatically forward the 20% to the reseller using our escrow routing. We'll provid...

messages.subject: Re: Supplier registration + PO acceptance requirements for AcmeHealth (self-bill & split remit)
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel — good work compiling. Approve the following so we can move quickly:

1) Offer 2% early-pay discount for invoices Net45 if Acme pays within 15 days. Finance to document the terms in the AP packet.
2) Confirm Redwood will accept split remit via escrow; finance to supply escrow routing ID and the disbursement SLA.
3) Legal to draft a 1-page self-bill appendix for Acme to sign (Rachel / Lauren please loop in Legal).

Lauren — please confirm we’re good to push the completed packet back to Samira and ask for portal invite. Once portal access is granted, Rachel can do the PO-ack configuration and test with a sample PO.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Director, Sales Operations — Redwood Inf...

messages.subject: Re: Supplier registration + PO acceptance requirements for AcmeHealth (self-bill & split remit)
messages.body:
Lauren / Rachel —

This is great, thanks for the fast turnaround and the attachments. A few operational items before we provision:
- Please upload ACME_vendor_packet_response.pdf to our portal (we will review field mapping).
- Please send the escrow routing ID and signed W-9 to ap-team@acmehealth.com (separate secure email preferred).
- For self-bill: Acme will self-bill only reseller pass-through line items (we will share SKU list in a follow-up doc). Legal at Acme will review your 1-page appendix.

We’ll provision your vendor account once we receive the upload + secure tax docs. I’ve scheduled a 30-minute portal kickoff on Friday 2026-05-01 at 10:00 AM ET to walk through PO-ack behavior...

messages.subject: Service commitments: proposed exception matrix, credit ladder & patch windows
messages.body:
Hi Alex / Sarah —

Thanks again for walking through the Order Form yesterday. As promised I'm attaching a consolidated draft of the service commitments section (Redwood_Support_Commitment_Draft_v3.docx) and a simple CSV that enumerates our proposed planned patch windows by region (Patch_Window_Table.csv).

High-level asks from DeltaCore we captured during the call:
- Clarify uptime measurement interval and blackout carveouts for scheduled patches
- Define the credit ladder for tiered availability shortfalls (P1/P2 buckets)
- Lock in response-time targets for incident severities and on-call handoffs
- Agree process for emergency maintenance and cross-region failover

What we'd like from yo...

messages.subject: Re: Service commitments: proposed exception matrix, credit ladder & patch windows
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel — appreciate the consolidated draft. Quick read-through notes from our side:

1) Credit model: DeltaCore prefers credits tied to total monthly bill (standard) but we want to avoid open-ended liability for minor blips. Can you provide an example calculation for a 99.5% vs 99.9% outcome under your ladder? The v3 doc references a piecewise approach but we need a worked example.
2) Patch windows: your CSV shows weekly 2-hour windows during local 02:00–04:00. That generally works but can you confirm how you define the start/stop for multi-region rollouts where replicas failover?
3) Response SLAs: we need a clearer policy for initial triage vs full remediation. For P1 incidents we...

to: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Fwd: Service commitments: proposed exception matrix, credit ladder & patch windows
messages.body:
Folks — forwarding Alex's early feedback from DeltaCore. Short notes / proposed owner map: 

- Ben: please confirm multi-region patch behavior wording and add a short paragraph on \"failover window\" semantics for replicas (DeltaCore wants clarity on start/stop of the patch event across regions). See Patch_Window_Table.csv for the planned windows.
- Aditya: can you produce the example calculations for the credit ladder (99.9% vs 99.5% vs 98.0%) based on a sample monthly bill ($50k) and include a capped maximum credit? DeltaCore explicitly called out avoiding open-ended liability.
- Lauren: please prioritize a legal pass on the Availability definition and the emergency maintenance carveout...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Service commitments: proposed exception matrix, credit ladder & patch windows
messages.body:
Rachel / team — attaching a short legal markup (Legal_Markup_Service_Commitments.pdf) with three principal suggestions:

1) Definitions: tighten \"Available\" to mean \"service endpoints successfully responding to authenticated requests\" and add a performance clause that excludes transient latency spikes < 2 minutes per incident from availability calculations. This prevents microsecond blips from triggering credits.
2) Emergency maintenance carveout: allow for limited immediate maintenance when there is an active security exploit, with a 24-hour post-event notice to the customer and a documented remediation timeline.
3) Credit cap: accept a step-function credit ladder but cap monthly cre...

cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, procurement@deltacore.com
messages.subject: Re: Service commitments: proposed exception matrix, credit ladder & patch windows
messages.body:
Alex / Sarah —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. We've incorporated the items below into a consolidated v4 draft (attached as a link in HubSpot and in the Drive folder). Summary of agreed positions from Redwood:

- Availability definition: we will adopt the tightened wording Lauren proposed and exclude transient latency spikes < 2 minutes from availability calculations.
- Patch windows / multi-region: Ben confirmed an \"authoritative region\" approach in the CSV and added a short paragraph describing overlap behavior for replicas. See Patch_Window_Table.csv.
- Credit ladder & cap: Aditya's worked example is included: for a $50k MRC month, 99.9% => $250 credit, 99.5% => $1500 credit, 98.0%...

title: Reference request — timing constraints & alternate options
body:
Marissa Cole <marissa.cope@redwood.com>: Hi Olivia —

Hope youre doing well. Were updating our customer stories and I wanted to ask whether someone from BrightLayer would be open to a short 20-minute reference call next week about your experience with Redwood Inference (latency improvements and dedicated capacity use). Wed keep the call focused on three quick areas: your evaluation criteria, the impact you measured, and any advice for teams just getting ...

Olivia Chen <olivia@brightlayer.co>: Hi Marissa —

Thanks for the note and for including the template. I wish we could help but were currently experiencing reference fatigue internally — our product and comms teams are heads-down for a major launch in late May and were trying to avoid external-facing interviews for the next 8–10 weeks.

Options I can offer now:
- Fill out a short written Q&A (no live names or internal screenshots)...

messages.subject: Quick ask: 20-minute reference call for Redwood Inference
messages.body:
Hi Olivia —

Hope youre doing well. Were updating our customer stories and I wanted to ask whether someone from BrightLayer would be open to a short 20-minute reference call next week about your experience with Redwood Inference (latency improvements and dedicated capacity use). Wed keep the call focused on three quick areas: your evaluation criteria, the impact you measured, and any advice for teams just getting started.

If a live call is difficult, we can send a short written Q&A you can fill out on your schedule. Im attaching a 1-page Q&A template to make it easy.

Totally understand busy seasons — if nows a bad time, would you prefer we follow up in 6–8 weeks instead?

Thanks so much...

messages.subject: Re: Quick ask: 20-minute reference call for Redwood Inference
messages.body:
Hi Marissa —

Thanks for the note and for including the template. I wish we could help but were currently experiencing reference fatigue internally — our product and comms teams are heads-down for a major launch in late May and were trying to avoid external-facing interviews for the next 8–10 weeks.

Options I can offer now:
- Fill out a short written Q&A (no live names or internal screenshots) and you can use a single-sentence quote attributed to BrightLayer.
- Approve an anonymized case study (wed review before publishing).
- Share a short approved quote from our Head of ML instead of a live call, but that will need Legal review.

If none of those work, please follow up with us after th...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Fwd: Quick ask: 20-minute reference call — BrightLayer declining; alternatives?
messages.body:
Forwarding Olivia's reply — BrightLayer is asking to avoid interviews for the next 8–10 weeks due to a product launch. They did offer three fallback options: fill out the attached Q&A and provide a one-line quote, approve an anonymized case study, or have Head of ML sign off on a short quote (requires Legal).

Options Im proposing:
- Take the written Q&A and request a one-line quote we can use in marketing. Ill send a tracked copy and keep it minimal (≤30 words).
- Offer a one-month account credit or dedicated office hours as a thank-you (weve used credits before). Rafael, ok to propose a $2k credit or equivalent?
- Identify alternative references for the same vertical (fintech/analytics)...

to: Marissa Cole <marissa.cope@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Quick ask: 20-minute reference call — BrightLayer declining; alternatives?
messages.body:
Thanks for the context. A few quick thoughts from GTM: 

1) Reference rotation policy idea: add a 90-day cool-off after any reference interaction, and maintain a small "on-call" reference pool (customers who opt in for ad-hoc calls). That reduces fatigue and gives predictable availability. We should add this as a column in our reference tracker.

2) Substitutes: prioritize customers with recent wins and who are in the same buyer persona. Connor, please pull anyone who did a reference in the last 120 days and mark whether theyd accept another call.

3) Incentives: credits or product support sessions are fine — keep it standardized (e.g., $1k credit or two 60-min office-hours sessions). Raf...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.com>, Olivia Chen <olivia@brightlayer.co>
messages.subject: Re: Quick ask: 20-minute reference call for Redwood Inference
messages.body:
Thanks everyone — plan below and next steps: 

- Marissa: Send BrightLayer the 30-word quote suggestion + the short Q&A (attach template); offer option for anonymized case study and note that Legal review is fine.
- Rafael: Confirm whether marketing credit ($1k standard or $2k for enterprise) is okay and route through finance for DL-4829.
- Connor: Pull alternative references from the pool (priority: fintech/analytics, recent wins) and share availability by EOD Wednesday.
- Rachel: Draft the 90-day reference rotation column and Legal-friendly email template — share by EOD Tuesday.

Olivia — we appreciate the quick reply and your flexibility. Ill keep this light: sending a suggested 30-wor...

title: MSA / Order Form - Exhibit B clarifications and initial redline request

Hi all — I reviewed the attached Exhibit B and the SOW template. High-level notes and proposed actions below; I'm attaching a short annotation doc with inline flags.

Key legal points we will address in the consolidated redline:
- Residency language: we can offer region-scoped at-rest residency by Account (EU-only or EU+UK). We will propose explicit language that pins the customer’s account to EU regions; if Talon wants UK included please confirm now.
- Liability / caps: Exhibit B currently references a vendor-wide SLA that may be broader than our standard MSA; proposing our standard liability cap language (6 months of fees) in the MSA with an exceptions grid in the Order Form if Talon ne...

We look forward to your redline packet. If you can flag any items that would require escalation (e.g., material liability increases or residency that cannot be scoped), please call that out in the memo.

Dana

title: Private offer: geo-tiered commit ladder and reseller fee smoothing
body:
Claire Barnes <procurement@finedge.io>: Hi Rachel / Ben,

We're putting together an internal request for a private offer through AWS Marketplace for our EU operations. A few points we need aligned before procurement can move to PO: 

- Commit ladder: can Redwood provide a stepped commit structure (quarterly tiers) that allows us to escalate node-hour commitments over 12 months? We want a clear ladder for volume discounts if we cross thresholds mid-year....

Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>: Hi Claire — thanks for the clear outline. We've sketched a pragmatic approach and attached two stubs: commit-ladder-draft.xlsx (quarterly tiers + sample pricing) and reseller-fee-smoothing-proposal.pdf (ops playbook). High-level notes below.

1) Commit ladder (attached)
- 12-month horizon split into four quarterly thresholds (Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4). Each quarter has a base commit and a mid-quarter uplift ...

messages.subject: Private offer question — commit ladder across EU regions and reseller fee smoothing
messages.body:
Hi Rachel / Ben,

We're putting together an internal request for a private offer through AWS Marketplace for our EU operations. A few points we need aligned before procurement can move to PO: 

- Commit ladder: can Redwood provide a stepped commit structure (quarterly tiers) that allows us to escalate node-hour commitments over 12 months? We want a clear ladder for volume discounts if we cross thresholds mid-year.
- Geo-tiering: we operate in DE/FR/IE — does the private offer allow region-specific list price floors or do prices need to be harmonized across EU regions?
- Reseller fee smoothing: our reseller (CloudBridge) prefers a smoothing mechanism so they don't get variable payouts each...

messages.subject: Re: Private offer question — commit ladder across EU regions and reseller fee smoothing
messages.body:
Hi Claire — thanks for the clear outline. We've sketched a pragmatic approach and attached two stubs: commit-ladder-draft.xlsx (quarterly tiers + sample pricing) and reseller-fee-smoothing-proposal.pdf (ops playbook). High-level notes below.

1) Commit ladder (attached)
- 12-month horizon split into four quarterly thresholds (Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4). Each quarter has a base commit and a mid-quarter uplift band that triggers a higher discount tier for remainder of quarter.
- Mid-quarter escalations are applied pro rata for the remainder of the quarter, with full-tier pricing from the next quarter onward.

2) Geo-tiering
- We can support region-specific list prices for DE/FR/IE in the private offer me...

messages.subject: Re: Private offer question — commit ladder across EU regions and reseller fee smoothing
messages.body:
Rachel / Jordan / Claire — quick note from my side. For reseller smoothing I'd recommend aiming for a 3%–4% holdback with quarterly reconciliation. Operationally we do quarterly reconciliations (credit memos) to the reseller to avoid monthly variance.

For pricing anchoring, call out whether FinEdge wants asymmetric ramp (low early quarter commits then step-up) or even-step ladder — that will materially change early ARR recognition for you. If they want protection for CloudBridge, the holdback + quarterly true-up is the cleanest path.

Happy to discuss on the Thu call. I can bring our finance ops lead if needed.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Director, Sales — EMEA, Redwood Inference

to: Claire Barnes <procurement@finedge.io>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Fwd: Private offer question — commit ladder across EU regions and reseller fee smoothing
messages.body:
Claire / team — forwarding the attachments and the short plan. Irene (legal) is looped in to confirm there are no procurement redlines on the holdback mechanics; Irene, quick ask: can legal sign off on a quarterly true-up clause that limits Redwood liability and allows a reseller credit memo flow? Also, confirm that region-specific list prices are permitted in the private offer (we believe so, but want legal confirmation for tax/invoice language).

Proposed timeline: if legal signs off by Fri 26 Jun, we can finalize the private offer draft for FinEdge and aim for PO week of 6 Jul. Claire: does that timeline work for you?

Jordan Blake
Deal Desk, Redwood Inference

---

The panel will focus on productionizing LLMs for regulated healthcare workflows and we think LumaCare’s deployment story would be a great fit.

High-level ask: one speaker (product or engineering leader), 10–12...

2) Can you share a speaker agreement and a quick run-down of what marketing assets you’ll request (quotes, headshot, logo usage)?

Hi Sara —

Quick intro: I’m Marissa on the Redwood partnerships team. We’d love to invite a LumaCare leader to join a 30-minute panel at the Industry AI Forum on Dec 10 (virtual + one in-person NYC stream). The panel will focus on productionizing LLMs for regulated healthcare workflows and we think LumaCare’s deployment story would be a great fit.

High-level ask: one speaker (product or engineering leader), 10–12 minute case presentation + 10 minutes panel Q&A. We’ll handle slides, promo copy, and logistics. We can also include an optional short customer spotlight blog and a testimonial quote for the Redwood site.

Logistics we can accommodate:
- Honorarium or marketing spend to cover ti...

messages.body:
Hi Marissa —

Thanks for thinking of us. Tom and I chatted and we’re interested, but we need to confirm a couple of things before we commit:
1) Any PHI or customer data handled for demo/replay? We prefer anonymized examples only.
2) Can you share a speaker agreement and a quick run-down of what marketing assets you’ll request (quotes, headshot, logo usage)?

We’re tentatively available for a 30-min sync next Tue (11/18) at 10am PT. If that works, send a calendar invite. I’ll loop in our legal/compliance contact if there are data or recording concerns after the sync.

Best,
Sara Ng
Director of Product, LumaCare
sara@lumacare.com
