messages.subject: Parallel requests & rate cap — follow-up and data request
messages.body:
Hi Alice / Bob,

Thanks again for the time this morning — good discussion on the streaming use case and how you expect traffic to grow once the feature is in beta. Quick recap and a couple of asks so our SRE/SE teams can size a safe starting quota for the trial: 

- SLO targets you called out: p50 <= 120ms, p95 <= 450ms for synchronous responses; streaming sessions target end-to-end latency under 500ms for chunk delivery.
- Target launch concurrency: ~250 parallel requests across the fleet (worst-case spikes to ~1,000 for 15–30s).
- Typical request: ~120 tokens in + 250 tokens out (streamed).
- Adapters: clients use HTTP/1.1 keep-alive today; some teams can switch to HTTP/2 if needed.

At...

messages.subject: Re: Parallel requests & rate cap — follow-up and data request
messages.body:
Rachel — thanks for the clear recap. Per your ask, attached is a small sample of our expected traffic for week 1 of beta: acme-requested-qps.csv. Key notes:

- Sustained baseline: 180 qps (US-East), with geo failover to US-West.
- Expected daily spikes: up to ~900 qps (20–60s) during big user events.
- Streaming sessions: we expect ~40 concurrent long-poll streaming sessions during peak (each session sends keepalive heartbeats every 8–10s).
- Many clients are behind NATs and use connection pooling via our gateway, which typically maintains ~8 persistent connections per service instance.

Question for Redwood: do you enforce both token-based and request-based rate limits simultaneously? Ou...

messages.subject: Re: Parallel requests & rate cap — follow-up and data request
messages.body:
Hi Alice — thanks for the CSV, that helps. A few technical clarifications and an initial recommendation based on the numbers you shared: 

1) Limits model: Redwood enforces both request-rate (requests/sec) and token-rate ceilings. For streaming, we also track concurrent streaming sessions as a separate soft quota. 429s can occur if any of those caps are hit.

2) Mapping your numbers: sustained 180 qps maps to ~ (assume 370 tokens/sec aggregate) under your token sizes. Spikes to 900 qps for 20–60s are significant; we recommend a burst policy rather than a hard quota at that level.

3) Client-side recommendations:
 - Use HTTP/2 (multiplexing) if you plan >8 logical streams per TCP connectio...

messages.subject: Re: Parallel requests & rate cap — follow-up and data request
messages.body:
Alice — appreciate the quick turnaround and great detail. 

Proposed next steps from Redwood side: 
- Rachel and I will take Karthik's numbers and draft a trial quota package (baseline + burst window + monitoring thresholds).
- Legal/Finance: we’ll route a one-page trial addendum for sign-off (if needed) — I’ll include estimated monthly commit if you decide to move to Dedicated.
- Schedule: can we target the elevated trial window for the week of Mar 15? If that works, please pick a 30-minute slot here: https://cal.com/rachel-kim/30min and Rachel will confirm.

I’ll loop in Vivek for manager approval on the trial burst. Anything else you need from our end to unblock?

Thanks — looking forw...

messages.body:
Maya — thanks for the productive call earlier. As discussed, I attached a redline of the Order Form + SLA that reflects the negotiated uptime target (99.95% monthly) and our initial service-credit table. A couple of items I want us to confirm before legal does formal signoff: 

- Planned maintenance windows: we have proposed two 4-hour weekly windows (US PST 0300–0700) that are excluded from uptime calculations; customer requested smaller windows — can you confirm the operational constraints on your side?
- Service credit scaling: we proposed a capped monthly credit equal to 30% of monthly recurring charges for the affected environment; you asked if a tiered cap would be acceptable.
- Pri...

messages.subject: Re: Order Form — planned operational windows & credit alignment
messages.body:
Rachel — thanks for the redline. Quick answers from our side: 

- Maintenance windows: we prefer a 2-hour weekly window if possible (we have batch jobs that run early morning; minimizing the window helps downstream systems). If 2 hours is a hard blocker for your ops team, can we instead limit the weekly window to a 4-hour slot but not in the 03:00–07:00 window (our data backups run 02:00–04:00 PST)?
- Service credit cap: procurement prefers a tiered cap tied to account size. Example request: 15% cap for month-to-month usage, 25% for 12-month committed accounts, 35% for >24-month reserved capacity.
- Response times: P1 1 hour OK; please confirm that P1s include data-loss incidents and secu...

messages.subject: Re: Order Form — planned operational windows & credit alignment
messages.body:
Maya — appreciate the detail. Looping in Lauren (legal ops) and Aditya (product/ops) to propose a pragmatic compromise. High level proposal from our side: 

- Maintenance windows: shift to a single 3-hour weekly window (02:30–05:30 PST) to try and avoid your 02:00–04:00 backup overlap. We’ll commit to hold non-critical changes out of the 02:30–03:30 sub-window. If that still conflicts, we can implement a maintenance blackout for your critical backup hour (02:00–03:00) and schedule the remaining 3-hour window outside that hour.
- Service credit cap: open to a tiered cap. Proposed company-side compromise: 20% cap for month-to-month, 30% for 12-month, 40% for 24+ month reserved. This keeps h...

messages.subject: Re: Order Form — planned operational windows & credit alignment
messages.body:
Hi Lauren/Rachel — legal reviewed the proposed compromise and provided two small edits to ensure enforceability and auditability: 

1) Maintenance exclusion must include a requirement for advance notice (72 hours) and reasonable effort to avoid peak usage windows for the customer.
2) The credit calculation needs a clear formula: (Monthly MRC * % credit tier) prorated to the number of minutes of unavailability beyond the uptime threshold. Please include a worked example.

We are comfortable with the tiered cap you proposed (20/30/40) provided those two edits are included. We also asked procurement to confirm which commitment tier they intend to sign up for — they will circle back.

I attac...

to: Sarah Lim <sarah.lim@aureliahealth.com>, Maya Patel <maya.patel@aureliahealth.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Order Form — planned operational windows & credit alignment
messages.body:
Thanks Sarah — we made the two edits and included a sample calculation and worked example in the attached clean Order Form (clean_order_form_20260517.docx). Highlights of language we added: 

- Maintenance exclusion: "Planned Maintenance" requires 72 hours notice and Redwood will use commercially reasonable efforts to avoid scheduled work during Customer-designated blackout hours; if conflict arises, Redwood will notify Customer and propose minimized-impact alternatives.
- Credit calculation: we added formula and example: Credit = (Monthly MRC) * (Credit % for tier) * (minutes of unavailability beyond threshold / total minutes in calendar month). Example included showing a 90-minute outag...

messages.subject: Re: Redwood trial — request for extension + credits
messages.body:
Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — team was impressed with the latency numbers. We're ready to move forward with a pilot but procurement needs more runway to complete their security review. Two asks: would Redwood consider (1) extending our 30-day trial by an extra 30 days and (2) providing a $10k promotional credit to cover higher-than-expected initial usage during onboarding?

If we can get those in writing (SOW or contract amendment), procurement will fast-track signatures. Happy to jump on a short call if helpful.

Thanks,
Elena
Elena Vargas
Director of Data & Integrations
Nimbus Health

messages.subject: Re: Redwood trial — request for extension + credits
messages.body:
Hi Elena — appreciate the transparency and glad the demo landed well. I can start internal approvals for an extension + credit. Two quick clarifications so I scope the ask correctly:

- Is the $10k credit one-time promotional credit applied to your hosting/usage invoice?
- Do you need the extra 30 days to be attached to the existing trial SOW or as an addendum to the pilot agreement?

I'll loop in my manager and Rev Ops for approvals; should be straightforward but want to set proper constraints up front.

Would you be available for a 20-minute call Wednesday at 11am PT if we need to finalize details?

— Logan
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

messages.subject: Fwd: Request: Extended trial + promotional credit for Nimbus Health — need exec ok
messages.body:
Team,

Forwarding request from Nimbus. Quick summary: Director-level ask for +30 day trial extension and $10k promotional credit to smooth onboarding; they're asking for the concession to be in writing to speed procurement. Deal stage: pilot -> legal/PO pending. ACV of pilot expected ~$50k, with reasonable expansion potential.

Risk/constraints to flag: credits reduce short-term ARR recognition and we should ensure we can recover through committed paid usage post-pilot. This is a Strategic Healthcare account; security review timeline is the gating item.

Can we get signoff for: 30-day extension + $10k credit applied to first invoice, conditioned on execution of SOW addendum and one commit...

messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Extended trial + promotional credit for Nimbus Health — need exec ok
messages.body:
Logan —

I'm OK with the $10k promotional credit and 30-day extension with the following controls:

- Credit applied only after SOW addendum is signed and countersigned.
- Credit consumption capped to usage during the pilot; any overage billed at agreed pilot rates.
- Require a success check (30-day health check call) with AE + CS to confirm lead toward production.

Operational impact: small; revenue recognition below threshold for exec signoff, but please confirm region/finance org (US). Rachel, Priya — any legal/contract objections?

Marcus
Head of RevOps

to: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Extended trial + promotional credit for Nimbus Health — need exec ok
messages.body:
All —

Legal is fine with the structure provided we add two clauses to the addendum: (1) promotional credit is non-transferable and expires 30 days after trial extension end date, (2) usage logs must be retained for 90 days for audit. I'll draft a short SOW addendum and attach. No need for exec-level legal review.

I'll prepare the addendum and send to Logan for signature routing once Marcus confirms financial OK.

— Priya

to: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Extended trial + promotional credit for Nimbus Health — final approval
messages.body:
Approving the exception. Terms: 30-day trial extension + $10,000 promotional credit applied to first invoice. Conditions recorded: SOW addendum executed; credit capped to pilot usage; 30-day post-pilot success review scheduled. Rachel — please get the addendum signed and attach SOW. Logan — you can confirm with Elena and Procurement and proceed to route DocuSign (link below).

Marcus
Head of RevOps

-- DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/secure/12345-approve
-- Attachment(s): redwood-pricing-breakdown.xlsx

messages.subject: Unexpected duplicate charges + token count spike on 2026-03-18
messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

We noticed two unexpected line items on invoice ACME-0318 dated 2026-03-18 that look like duplicate charges for the same batch of API usage. Our finance team also reports a ~2x token count for a number of calls between 03:10–03:40 PDT. Can you confirm whether this came from our account or if it was a platform incident? We need an initial statement for our internal auditor and an ETA for a correction.

Attached is a CSV export from our side showing the invoice lines and timestamps. Happy to jump on a call if helpful.

Thanks,
Sam Torres
Head of Finance — Acme Medical Systems
sam.torres@acme-corp.com
+1 (415) 555-0124

---

messages.subject: Re: Unexpected duplicate charges + token count spike on 2026-03-18
messages.body:
Hi Sam — thanks for flagging this and for the CSV export. I’m sorry for the disruption. I’ve looped in Marissa from Customer Success and Neha from Billing so we can triage quickly.

Immediate next steps:
- Marissa will confirm whether there was an upstream platform incident impacting token counting and whether any double-billing occurred.
- Neha will pull a copy of the raw billing ledger for ACME-0318 and prepare a provisional credit if duplicate line items are confirmed.
- I’ll follow up with an incident summary and proposed remediation by EOD today (PST).

Can you confirm whether you prefer a credit or an adjusted invoice? We can do either; the credit will appear on your next invoice an...

to: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Sam Torres <sam.torres@redwood-corp.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Root cause & remediation for ACME token count / duplicate charges (postmortem)
messages.body:
Hi Sam / team — quick summary from our ops investigation:

What happened:
- At 03:08 PDT on 2026-03-18 we deployed a routing change intended to improve regional failover. An unexpected interaction with our batching layer duplicated a small portion of request envelopes for ~32 minutes (03:10–03:42). This caused token accounting to double-count tokens for affected requests and generated duplicate invoice lines for some batched billing records.

Scope:
- Affected account subset: customers routed through the new failover path; Acme was in the cohort.
- Volume impact for ACME: ~2,400 requests affected, ~1.9M tokens double-counted on the 03:10–03:42 window.

What we’ve done:
- Reverted the rout...

to: Sam Torres <sam.torres@acme-corp.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Invoice adjustment issued — ACME-0318
messages.body:
Hi Sam,

Thank you for your patience while we completed the audit. We’ve issued an adjusted invoice (adjustment-invoice-ACME-0318.pdf) that removes the duplicated token charges for the 03:10–03:42 PDT window. The net impact is a credit of $3,842.60 applied to your account and an updated invoice PDF is attached. The credit will also appear as a memo on your account and on next month’s statement.

If you’d prefer instead to receive the credit as an immediate refund via the original payment method, we can start that process — please let us know and I’ll request a finance refund (typically completed in 5–7 business days).

For your auditor, the postmortem and the CSV audit are available via t...

to: Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
messages.subject: Re: Invoice adjustment issued — ACME-0318
messages.body:
Hi Neha — thanks for the quick turnaround and for the detailed postmortem. We’ll accept the credit to the account rather than a refund. Can you also send the signed letter of correction for our auditor? Otherwise everything looks good from our side.

Appreciate the transparency and the change plan to prevent this class of bug going forward.

Best,
Sam
Sam Torres
Head of Finance — Acme Medical Systems

1) For modern slavery evidence — do you need a third-party audit or is our signed Supplier Code of Conduct sufficient for initial onboarding?

messages.subject: Re: Supplier onboarding: ethics intake + PO activation for BlueRidge Logistics
messages.body:
Thanks, Sanjay — good questions. Short answers below and next steps.

- Modern slavery / Code of Conduct: For initial onboarding a signed Supplier Code of Conduct and a self-attestation in your sustainability workbook is acceptable. If we see red flags during the compliance review, legal may request third-party evidence, but that’s not the default.
- Tax docs: For Singapore HQ, please provide your Singapore tax residency certificate (or equivalent) plus your local GST registration. Our AP team will follow up for any withholding considerations.

Next steps I will trigger from our side:
- I’ve uploaded your XLS to the intake queue and flagged it for a standard sustainability review.
- Pleas...

messages.subject: Re: Supplier onboarding: ethics intake + PO activation for BlueRidge Logistics
messages.body:
Rachel — a couple notes for the compliance queue before the call: 
- BlueRidge's Singapore domicile means we need to collect either the Certificate of Residence or a Form IRAS equivalent; AP will mark them as \"foreign vendor\" for tax handling.
- Please ask for a COI with Redwood listed as certificate holder (standard limits).
- If they cannot produce a third-party audit for sustainability, the self-attestation + signed Code of Conduct is fine; flag it for quarterly follow-up.

I will join the Thu call and can run through the COI/tax checklist.

Thanks — Monica
Monica Patel
Senior Procurement Operations | Redwood Inference

We'll join the Thu 10:00am PT call — sending calendar accept now. If anything else is required ahead of the call, let us know and we'll prioritize.

Thanks,
Sanjay

Monica Patel
People Ops Business Partner
Redwood Inference

On Mon, 9 M...

messages.subject: Re: Immediate offboarding coordination for karthik_iyer
messages.body:
See attached: severance_agreement_karthik_v2.pdf (redline).

Quick legal notes:
- Recommend 4 weeks base as a starting point given tenure and to reduce litigation risk.
- Include a standard release of claims and non-disparagement clause.
- Avoid language that could be construed as admission of wrongdoing.
- If there are any IP or code custody concerns, we should explicitly require return/confirmation of access and keys.

Outside counsel (Thompson) can finalize the release language; I've copied them for expedited review.

Rachel Kim
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachment: severance_agreement_karthik_v2.pdf

messages.subject: Re: Offboarding coordination — IT/security actions
messages.body:
Ticket created: IT-3321 (access revoke + key rotation). Summary: disable AD account, revoke SSO tokens, remove VPN entitlement, deprovision GCP service account keys linked to Karthik's identity, rotate any shared secrets he had access to.

Estimated SLA: complete within 2 business hours of receipt of "execute" instruction from HR. Please confirm exact notification time so we can schedule the revoke to avoid active builds being disrupted.

I will also snapshot his laptop via EDR and scrub company data per policy. Attaching the ticket log: it_access-revoke-ticket-#3321.txt

—Vivek
Head of Security Engineering
Redwood Inference

—Karthik

Redwood Inference

messages.subject: Acme_OrderForm_v3 - SLA redlines + evidence ask
messages.body:
Hi Hannah,

Thanks for sending the updated order form. Attached are our redlines to the MSA + Order Form focusing on SLA terms — uptime target, service credits, response times, and maintenance window restrictions. High level asks from Acme:

- Baseline uptime at 99.95% (we ran numbers vs availability impact on customer SLAs)
- Service credit formula that scales to 10% of monthly fees for sustained incidents (we need clearer stacking rules)
- P1 response time of 30 minutes and P2 acknowledged within 2 hours
- Maintenance windows limited to 4 hours/month and no maintenance allowed in our peak window (Mon-Fri 13:00-18:00 ET)

Also, our legal team is asking for SOC2 Type II evidence and a run...

to: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Fw: Acme_OrderForm_v3 - SLA redlines + evidence ask
messages.body:
Team — forwarding Acme's redlines. Quick internal sync points I want before we draft a reply:

1) Uptime: Acme asks for 99.95%. Our standard hosted SLA is 99.9%. For Dedicated we can offer 99.95% at +20% commit. Propose we push for 99.9 baseline and offer a paid upgrade path to 99.95 (order form option).

2) Credits: Acme wants up to 10% monthly fees stacked. Ops/legal prefers a capped model to avoid runaway liability. Proposed compromise: tiered credit matrix up to 8% for multi-day outages, with a combined monthly cap of 12% for repeated failures (see SLA_Comparison_Table.xlsx attached).

3) Response times: P1 30m is aggressive for global infra. We can commit to 1 hour for P1 response (a...

messages.subject: Re: Acme_OrderForm_v3 - SLA redlines + evidence ask
messages.body:
Hi Sarah — thanks for the clear redlines and the timeline. We reviewed this with Ops and Legal and have a proposed compromise below (high level) — attaching a counter-redline PDF (Redwood_SLA_counter_redlines.pdf).

Proposed compromise (summary):
- Uptime: Baseline hosted SLA at 99.9%. Offer a contract option to upgrade to 99.95% (applies to dedicated pools or higher commit) for an additional fee; we will call this the "Premium Availability Addendum."
- Credits: Tiered credit matrix (month-over-month): 0.5% for 99.7–99.9, 2% for 99.0–99.7, 8% for <99.0. Combined monthly cap at 12% of monthly recurring fees; single incident credit cannot exceed 8%. Stacking rules and remediation timelines ...

messages.subject: Re: Acme_OrderForm_v3 - SLA redlines + evidence ask
messages.body:
Hannah — thanks for the quick turnaround and the thoughtful compromise.

Acme legal will review the counter-redline tomorrow morning. We appreciate the Premium Availability Addendum option; procurement will model the costs. Can Ops please send the SOC2 and runbook excerpt to procurements@acmeanalytics.com?

We're available for a 30-min call Thu 10:00–10:30 PT or Fri 11:00–11:30 PT — Thu works best. I'll invite our counsel and the platform engineering lead.

Also attaching our procurement timeline for visibility. Goal is to sign the order form by next week pending legal review.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sarah Jones
Senior Procurement Manager, Acme Analytics
---
Attachment: acme_procurement_timeline.x...

title: Urgent: panel coverage needed for Backend IC interview (Samir Patel)
body:
Elaine Torres <elaine@talentworks.io>: Hi Rachel —

Samir Patel (applied via TalentWorks) is available next week for the final on-site (virtual) loop. He can do windows:
- Tue 2026-05-25: 10:00–13:00 PT
- Wed 2026-05-26: 14:00–17:00 PT

We need a backend system design interviewer (senior IC), a coding interviewer, and a cross-functional product/PM panelist for a 90–120min loop. He prefers mornings but can be flexible within the windows above.

Can you ...

rachel_kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>: Team —

Short notice: Samir is ready for a loop next Tue/Wed. We need to staff the 11:00–12:30 PT block on Tue (preferred) or the 14:00–16:00 PT on Wed as a backup. Ask: who can take the backend system design interviewer role? The ask list:

We'll start by checking our serving logs and infra metrics for the 18:00–18:30 PST window and report back within 4 hours. If this looks like an internal issue we'll treat it as a Sev 2.

Best,
Rachel Kim
Senior Account Lead, Redwood Inference

messages.subject: Re: Intermittent timeouts hitting /v1/generate — triage & closure
messages.body:
Hi Alex — quick update and closure.

Thanks for the quick reply and the additional gateway configs you sent overnight. Per Marissa's triage below and the logs we've shared (attached), we did not find any server-side errors or capacity limits in our us-west-2 fleet that would produce 504s during the 18:15 PST window on 2026-02-16. Our replayed requests completed normally.

What we did find that may explain the symptoms:
- Your gateway has an idle-connection timeout of 28s which can surface as 504s on the client when paired with transient packet loss.
- A small subset of client IPs showed connection resets during the window (see attached filtered logs).

Action taken by Redwood:
- We review...

title: Intermittent SNAT imbalance + MTU stalls during Fairview private cutover
body:
Adam Turner <adam.turner@fairview-health.com>: Hi Rachel,

We cut over the private link for the Fairview VPC last night and are seeing intermittent TCP stalls / retransmits affecting our API traffic to your private frontdoor. Symptoms: 5-10% of connections see long stalls (~6-12s) on first few hundred bytes, then recover. We see packet reordering and occasional SYN retries.

Quick notes:
- Traffic is egressing through the secondary NIC on the appliance (eth1)....

Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>: Thanks Adam — sorry you're seeing this. I'm pulling Marissa and Karthik in for network-level triage.

Please do the following while we look:
- Run an MTR/TRACE from your edge to the private frontdoor IP and paste results.
- Confirm exact ephemeral port range on your firewall (are you NAT-ing to a small pool?).
- If you can, run iperf3 for short bursts to try and reproduce microbursts.

Symptoms: 5-10% of connections see long stalls (~6-12s) on first few hundred bytes, then recover. We see packet reordering and occasional SYN retries.

Quick notes:
- Traffic is egressing through the secondary NIC on the appliance (eth1).
- MTU counters on our edge show fragmentation events but no ICMP blackhole logs.
- We captured a short tcpdump during a hit (attached).

Can you triage? This is impacting production traffic and we'd like a hotfix path.

Attachments: fairview_cutover_capture_0514.pcap (pcap, ~2.3...

Thanks Adam — sorry you're seeing this. I'm pulling Marissa and Karthik in for network-level triage.

Please do the following while we look:

- Confirm exact ephemeral port range on your firewall (are you NAT-ing to a small pool?).
- If you can, run iperf3 for short bursts to try and reproduce microbursts.

Marissa / Karthik — attaching the pcap from Fairview. Initial hypothesis: SNAT imbalance across our frontdoor cluster + MTU-related fragmentation/clamping on the secondary NIC could cause reordering and TCP stalls. Also want to validate SG/stateful timeouts on the Private connector.

Attachments: quick-tria...

messages.subject: Re: Intermittent timeouts after private cutover
messages.body:
Hi all — dug into the capture and a few frontdoor nodes. Summary and suggested mitigations below.

What I see:
- Multiple flows from Fairview are being SNATed by different frontdoor hosts with uneven ephemeral port distribution. Some nodes are at >85% of ephemeral port allocation under short bursts. That can cause port reuse and spurious RSTs from intermediate NATs.
- The capture shows out-of-order sequence numbers and duplicated ACKs consistent with asymmetric path / reordering.
- Edge counters show small fragments and IP reassembly attempts; MTU looks like 1500 on the wire but we see a middlebox clamping MSS down to 1400 intermittently (likely a load-balancer or firewall rule).
- Securi...

messages.subject: Re: Intermittent timeouts after private cutover
messages.body:
Marissa / Rachel — thanks for the quick turnaround.

We validated this morning after your SNAT pool expansion + MSS clamp. Production error rate on the API endpoints dropped from ~5% to ~0.7% over the morning window. We still see a small number of retries during very short bursts (~0.5% of connections) but nothing close to the impact we saw overnight.

Next asks:
- Can you provide the permanent rollout plan for increasing SNAT capacity and ensuring autoscale pre-warms during cutovers?
- We'll need a follow-up joint validation run before the final cutover (preferably Wed 05/19 02:00-05:00 UTC).
- Please open a Jira with the remediation steps we discussed and include runbooks so our NOC can...

Hi Elena — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and concrete asks so we can lock economics and quota behavior: 

Call summary (from our side)
- Finlytics baseline: ~30M tokens / month (user-driven search + summarization).
- Expected peaks: product releases can push spikes up to ~10–12M tokens/day for 2–3 days, otherwise typical daily peak ~1.5M.
- Key asks: predictable unit economics ($/1M tokens), how we handle surge/spike traffic (rate limits, burst headroom), and overage terms + SLA on quota increases.

Open items for you to confirm
1) Baseline monthly tokens (approx): confirm 30M or update.
2) Target unit economics: you mentioned a target in the ~$700–900 / 1M tokens range —...

messages.subject: Re: Post-call recap: cost / quota qualification + surge handling
messages.body:
Hi Rachel — thanks for the clear notes. A few clarifications from our side: 

1) Baseline: yes, 30M tokens / month is our current forecast for the next 6 months. Could grow if adoption goes well.
2) Target $/1M: we were hoping to get to ~$750 / 1M tokens as a target CEP (cost-expectation point). If you can be at or below that with a modest commit, that will make the finance team happier.
3) Spike profile: when we do releases the large spikes usually run 8–12 hours (overnight launch, heavy indexing and user activity). During those windows concurrency rises ~6–8x vs normal QPS.

Questions: 
- The 20% surge allowance seems small relative to our multi-hour release spikes. Is there a mechanism...

messages.subject: Re: Post-call recap: cost / quota qualification + surge handling
messages.body:
Elena — great questions, thanks. Below I outline two practical patterns customers use, with concrete numbers so you can model cost: 

Option A — On-demand overage (simple)
- Baseline price: $800 / 1M tokens (as noted).
- Surge allowance: 20% no-charge short windows.
- Overage: 1.5x baseline ($1,200 / 1M tokens).
- Pros: simplest, no upfront commit. Cons: expensive at scale for predictable one-off releases.

Option B — Paid short-window headroom (recommended for release events)
- Mechanic: buy temporary headroom by reserving a time-limited quota uplift for a predefined window (6, 12, 24 hours).
- Pricing example: 6-hour uplift of 4x baseline rate priced at a premium of 0.6x baseline for th...

messages.subject: Re: Post-call recap: cost / quota qualification + surge handling
messages.body:
Quick internal — ok to approve a $15k onboarding credit and a one-time 30% overage credit on the first invoice to help close. Also fine to offer the 6-hour uplift path at the 0.6x premium pricing in your note. Please record the promo in HubSpot and mark deal DL-2026-0187 as \"promo-approved\".

—Marcus
Head of Sales | Redwood Inference

High-level proposal (summary):
- Term: 12 months, option to extend to 24 months with step-down pricing in year 2
- Commitment: $600k annual commit (billed upfront) — covers estimated steady-state usage of ~1.2B tokens/year
- Discounting: 18% off list for annual upfront versus current monthly list; additional 3% loyalty credit if you extend to 24 mo...

messages.subject: Re: Proposal: Annual upfront incentives & ramp schedule for Streamline AI
messages.body:
Thanks Rachel — appreciate the clear outline. A couple of follow-ups from our side:

- The $600k number looks in the ballpark but our CFO is hesitant on full upfront at that level. Would Redwood be open to a 3-way split (40/40/20 over first 3 months) if we commit to 24 months?
- On the ramp: those quarter %s look reasonable but we need a safety net if usage lags (e.g., credits we can use the following year).
- Legal asked us to confirm the termination refund calculation — can you share the exact formula?

Also looping procurement to verify what PO timing looks like. We need to close the commercial terms by end of month to hit our Q2 roadmap.

—Chris
Chris Nolan
Head of Product Ops | Strea...

cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
messages.subject: Re: Proposal: Annual upfront incentives & ramp schedule for Streamline AI
messages.body:
Chris — helpful questions, thanks. Quick answers below and I attached a one-page term sheet (rachel_term_sheet_2026-03-19.pdf) with the exact refund / termination language for legal to review. Key clarifications:

1) Payment flexibility: we can do a 3-way split (40% at signing, 40% at month 3, 20% at month 6) conditional on a 24-month commitment. For that we propose a 20% discount (instead of 18%) in year 1 and the additional 3% loyalty credit applied in year 2. So effectively year-1 net discount is 23% if you sign for 24 months and take the staged payment.

2) Usage lag protection: we offer a one-time carry-forward credit equal to unused committed tokens up to 20% of annual commit to be ...

messages.subject: Re: Proposal: Annual upfront incentives & ramp schedule for Streamline AI
messages.body:
Quick add from growth side — if Streamline signs 24 months and takes the staged payment, we should offer:
- $50k co-marketing credit (applied over first 12 months)
- 1 dedicated onboarding week with our solutions engineer (no extra charge)
- A 10% invoice credit if we miss SLA in any billing quarter (documented in SOW)

These concessions are relatively low-cost for us and accelerate adoption. I recommend we include them in the DocuSign envelope so procurement sees the full commercial picture. HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/4821 (updated with the staged payment option).

—Ben

title: Order Form — Output & Model Use (training/feedback)
body:
Samir Patel <legal@lumenhealth.com>: Vanessa —

Thanks for sending the Order Form. Our legal team reviewed the current language and we have three non-negotiable items related to IP and model use:

1) Output ownership: Lumen requires an explicit clause confirming Customer owns the output (generated text/data) and Redwood only has a limited service right to display/produce those outputs for the Customer.
2) Training rights: We cannot grant rights for R...

vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai: Samir / Marta —

Thanks for the redline and the memo — appreciate the clarity. We want to get this over the finish line quickly. High-level responses:

- Output ownership: We are comfortable acknowledging Customer ownership of outputs for their own use. We typically carve out a very narrow "service rights" grant so Redwood can reproduce responses in logs and debug issues; we propose the language below.
- Trai...

normalized.subject: Order Form — Output & Model Use (training/feedback)

messages.subject: Re: Order Form draft — output & model use
messages.body:
Vanessa —

Thanks for sending the Order Form. Our legal team reviewed the current language and we have three non-negotiable items related to IP and model use:

1) Output ownership: Lumen requires an explicit clause confirming Customer owns the output (generated text/data) and Redwood only has a limited service right to display/produce those outputs for the Customer.
2) Training rights: We cannot grant rights for Redwood to use Customer inputs or outputs to train, fine-tune, or improve models unless we provide prior written consent.
3) Feedback: Any feedback Redwood asks for must not be used to create productized models for other customers; at most it can be used for debugging and aggregat...

messages.subject: Re: Order Form draft — output & model use
messages.body:
Samir / Marta —

Thanks for the redline and the memo — appreciate the clarity. We want to get this over the finish line quickly. High-level responses:

- Output ownership: We are comfortable acknowledging Customer ownership of outputs for their own use. We typically carve out a very narrow "service rights" grant so Redwood can reproduce responses in logs and debug issues; we propose the language below.
- Training rights: We cannot accept an absolute prohibition across all telemetry because we rely on aggregated, anonymized telemetry to maintain service quality and safety. However, we can remove any right to train our base models on Customer-provided data or outputs without express written...

messages.subject: Fwd: Order Form draft — output & model use (need legal redline)
messages.body:
Priya / Markus —

Looping you in with Lumen's redline (see Samir) and the note I sent earlier. Quick asks from Sales:

1) Can Legal produce an editable redline of the MSA + Order Form that adopts the proposed compromise language (Customer owns outputs; no training without consent; limited aggregated telemetry allowed; feedback license limited to service improvement only)?
2) We need suggested clause text for Section 23 (Model Use & Data) and a short justification we can send back to the customer (they raised regulatory/PHI concerns).
3) Timeline: can we get initial legal redline by end of day today? Lumen's procurement is asking for sign-off this week and we have pricing and implementatio...

messages.subject: RE: Fwd: Order Form draft — output & model use (need legal redline)
messages.body:
Vanessa / Markus —

Thanks for the context and the proposed language. Draft guidance and our recommended redline approach below — short version first: we can accept Customer ownership of Outputs and a strict prohibition on using Customer Inputs/Outputs to train base models, while permitting the Provider to use limited, aggregated, non-identifiable telemetry for safety/ops. We will also add a mechanism to request narrow, explicit consent for model training in writing (per-deal).

Suggested clause (short form for Order Form):
"Customer Data and Outputs. Customer retains all right, title and interest in and to the Outputs. Provider may retain copies of Outputs solely for the purpose of provi...

title: Direct loss boundaries: MSA + Order Form redlines
body:
Jordan Price <jordan@acme.com>: Hi Vivek / Marissa,

Attached is Acme's consolidated redline to your MSA (see Redwood_MSA_acme_redline_20260228.docx). Two items Legal is focused on and would like clarification / counter-language for before we proceed: 1) the interplay between the Limit of Liability cap in the Order Form and damages exclusions (particularly lost profits and loss of goodwill), and 2) whether certain data-loss remediation costs can...

Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>: Team — forwarding Acme's ask. Quick context: this is their standard procurement push for a higher cap and explicit carve-outs for data remediation. Sales prefers a relatively narrow set of carve-outs so we don't blow the cap.

Request for Legal: 1) propose 2-3 alternative redline options (seller-favorable, middle ground, customer-favorable) that we can give Acme this week; 2) include short examples ...

To keep timelines tight: can you confirm Redwood's stance and provide sample clause language we can review? We need to present this to procurement/legal on Wed.

High-level Acme position: consequential losses (lost profits, lo...

Team — forwarding Acme's ask. Quick context: this is their standard procurement push for a higher cap and explicit carve-outs for data remediation. Sales prefers a relatively narrow set of carve-outs so we don't blow the cap.

Request for Legal: 1) propose 2-3 alternative redline options (seller-favorable, middle ground, customer-favorable) that we can give Acme this week; 2) include short examples that illustrate what counts as \"direct\" vs \"indirect\" (lost profits, data restoration, loss of goodwill).

Marissa
Account Executive — Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

messages.subject: RE: Redwood MSA redlines — proposed options and drafting examples
messages.body:
Team — below are three concrete drafting options and short, realistic examples to send to Acme. I attached a compiled doc with the redlines (draft_clauses_redwood_v1.docx) and highlighted the positions we feel comfortable taking.

Summary guidance (short):
- Keep consequential exclusions broad (lost profits, business interruption, loss of goodwill, reputational harm).
- Preserve a carve-out for (i) gross negligence/willful misconduct and (ii) breach of confidentiality or IP infringement.
- For data loss, split recovery: direct remediation costs (data restoration, forensics, reasonable engineering time, express customer notification costs) are permitted as \"direct\" damages up to a correl...

messages.subject: RE: Redwood MSA redlines — direct vs consequential damages
messages.body:
Vivek / team — thanks for the clear options and the worked examples. Acme prefers Option B but would like explicit language that data restoration costs are recoverable even if the root cause is a non-willful bug (so long as the bug is caused by Redwood's product or change). We can accept the cap at $5M if we get the broader data remediation carve-out.

Two quick asks before we sign: (1) add 30-day mutual notice + cure for any alleged breach that purports to give rise to damages (gives both sides time to remediate), and (2) include a one-time order form amendment that documents whether the cap uses the 12-month fees metric or the fixed $5M number.

If you send an updated redline incorporat...

body:
Amit Singh <amit@greenshore.ai>: Hi Dev,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team is seeing promising signal on entity extraction and the reduced token costs from your Optimize recommendations. We’re approaching the end of our 30-day pilot and wanted to discuss two possible paths forward while procurement finishes the PO paperwork:

1) Short top-up: purchase an extra 150k tokens (estimate attached usage) as a one-off voucher so we can comp...

dev_patel@redwood.com: Amit —

Appreciate the clear options. Quick response from our side: both are feasible but have different operational and commercial implications. A quick summary to help you and procurement decide: 

- Voucher/top-up (150k tokens): fast to issue, shows as a one-time credit on your account, no contract amendment required. Tokens expire 90 days after issuance. Cannot be reclassified as committed spend retroactively,.....

messages.subject: Pilot extension request — short top-up or 3-week add?
messages.body:
Hi Dev,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team is seeing promising signal on entity extraction and the reduced token costs from your Optimize recommendations. We’re approaching the end of our 30-day pilot and wanted to discuss two possible paths forward while procurement finishes the PO paperwork:

1) Short top-up: purchase an extra 150k tokens (estimate attached usage) as a one-off voucher so we can complete the final integration tests (2–3 days).
2) Time extension: grant a 3-week timebox extension to the existing pilot (no immediate invoice), so our product team can validate throughput under heavier traffic.

High-level constraints: we need a path that doesn’t trigger a formal ...

messages.subject: Re: Pilot extension request — short top-up or 3-week add?
messages.body:
Amit —

Appreciate the clear options. Quick response from our side: both are feasible but have different operational and commercial implications. A quick summary to help you and procurement decide: 

- Voucher/top-up (150k tokens): fast to issue, shows as a one-time credit on your account, no contract amendment required. Tokens expire 90 days after issuance. Cannot be reclassified as committed spend retroactively, but we can apply good-faith credit toward first invoice if you convert to a paid plan within 30 days (we’ll document that in a short acknowledgment email).

- Timebox extension (3 weeks): we can extend the pilot window without changing the SOW, but our operational rule is that w...

messages.subject: RE: Pilot extension request — short top-up or 3-week add?
messages.body:
Amit / team —

Quick commercial note from our side: we’re comfortable issuing the 150k-token voucher today with these stipulations:
- Voucher valid for 90 days from issuance
- If Greenshore signs a paid contract within 30 days of voucher issuance, we will apply 50% of the voucher value as a first-invoice credit (non-refundable).
- Voucher cannot be split across accounts or transferred.

Alternatively, if you prefer a 3-week extension:
- We’ll provide the extension and enable burst capacity up to 2x for 72-hour windows during that period (subject to our fair-use policy).
- We’ll document the extension via a 1-paragraph Annex emailed by an authorized Greenshore procurement contact; no forma...

messages.subject: Re: Pilot extension request — short top-up or 3-week add?
messages.body:
Thanks all — after a quick sync with procurement, we want to take the voucher route to avoid delaying internal approvals. A few clarifying points so procurement can accept the email-based terms: 

- Please send the voucher and the short acknowledgment email that states the voucher is redeemable and that 50% may be credited to first invoice if we sign within 30 days.
- Can you confirm the token-to-dollar mapping you’ll use for the voucher? Procurement needs that for the PO.
- We also accept a follow-up Annex if we later need to document extension language (just in case).

If you send the voucher and the acknowledgment now, procurement will issue a small PO to cover the nominal processing f...

Hi Rachel,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. We're starting Redwood's vendor onboarding in our procurement system and I wanted to capture a few mandatory fields so our AP team can accept POs and process invoices without delays. Can you confirm the following for the Redwood legal entity that will issue invoices to KiteWorks (EU customers):

- Legal name to appear on invoices (exactly as in vendor portal)
- Registered VAT number (for reverse-charge vs VAT charged scenarios)
- Preferred remit-to bank details for SEPA / IBAN + BIC (file a stub is fine)
- Billing contact and email (for invoice queries and remittance advices)

Also, on our side we require either:
- a VAT registration numbe...

Hi Rachel / Marissa,

Thanks for the details — that helps. A couple of follow-ups from our AP team:

1) GL code mapping: our PO lines must include a 6-digit ledger code (GL) that we use for cost center allocation. Do you have a default GL mapping we should use on the PO or will you accept a blank GL and send an expense allocation with each invoice?

2) PO comments: we require the supplier legal name to match exactly and that the invoice \"Invoice To\" section contains \"KiteWorks Inc. - EU Operations\" so our matching rules trigger.

3) Self-billing: KiteWorks can operate a self-billing workflow but we need the signed mandate and confirmation of the entity that invoices (your Ireland enti...

messages.subject: Fwd: Onboarding: PO / Invoice requirements & EU VAT agent flow
messages.body:
Forwarding to our vendor enablement and ledger team.

Procurements / AP team at KiteWorks — looping in Jordan for the GL mapping. A few internal notes to speed this:

- Legal name confirmation: Redwood Inference Europe Ltd. (use exactly as provided)
- VAT: IE1234567V (we will attach the VAT certificate in portal upload)
- Invoice origin: Redwood Inference Europe Ltd. will issue.
- Self-billing: We prefer standard supplier invoices (no self-billing) except where the customer requires it. If KiteWorks will force self-billing, we can sign the mandate but will need it routed to legal for review (turnaround 24–48 hrs).

Jordan — please confirm the default GL mapping we can provide to KiteWorks...

2) Invoice attributes: Redwood will include these fields on the PDF and in the invoice metadata when we submit to your AP portal: PO number, Redwood vendor ID, Redwood legal name (exact match), VAT number, invoice-level...

We loved the outline you shared for the hands-on workshop next month. Two quick asks from our side as we finalize attendees:

title: MSA redline: IP indemnity / liability-cap carve-out (Acme Dynamics)
body:
Laura Chen <laura.chen@acmedynamics.com>: Hi Rachel,

Attached is our redline of the MSA for the pilot. Our counsel is insisting that any indemnity for third-party IP infringement not be constrained by the general liability cap. Specifically we propose language that (i) excludes IP infringement indemnity from the liability cap and (ii) preserves recovery for reasonable defense costs and any equitable relief (injunctive).

We understand Redwood's position ...

Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>: Laura — thanks for the redline. I received the doc and looped in Sales leadership and Legal (cc'd).

High-level: Sales wants to keep deal momentum; Legal wants a narrow, defined carve-out rather than an uncapped, open-ended exception. Can Marissa/Neha review the attached and propose compromise language that addresses Acme's regulator concerns while keeping Reedwood's commercial risk bounded.

...

normalized.subject: MSA redline: IP indemnity / liability-cap carve-out (Acme Dynamics)

messages.subject: Acme - MSA redline (IP indemnity carve-out)
messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

Attached is our redline of the MSA for the pilot. Our counsel is insisting that any indemnity for third-party IP infringement not be constrained by the general liability cap. Specifically we propose language that (i) excludes IP infringement indemnity from the liability cap and (ii) preserves recovery for reasonable defense costs and any equitable relief (injunctive).

We understand Redwood's position on limiting exposure, but given the nature of the workloads Acme will run on your platform and our regulator requirements, we need clear carve-outs. Please confirm you received the redline and let us know whether Redwood can accept the carve-out in principle or if you need altern...

messages.subject: Re: Acme - MSA redline (IP indemnity carve-out)
messages.body:
Laura — thanks for the redline. I received the doc and looped in Sales leadership and Legal (cc'd).

High-level: Sales wants to keep deal momentum; Legal wants a narrow, defined carve-out rather than an uncapped, open-ended exception. Can Marissa/Neha review the attached and propose compromise language that addresses Acme's regulator concerns while keeping Reedwood's commercial risk bounded.

Proposed next steps: 1) Marissa to send suggested language, 2) Karthik to confirm economic fallback (e.g., holdback/escrow or higher cap for IP only), 3) Rachel to coordinate response and timing for an updated redline back to Acme.

I've attached Redwood_response_redline.docx with a first-pass placeh...

messages.subject: RE: Acme - MSA redline (IP indemnity carve-out)
messages.body:
Rachel / Karthik — see my take below and suggested redline (attached). Key points:

- We should not accept an uncapped indemnity. Instead propose: IP infringement indemnity remains excepted from the standard liability cap only to the extent of direct damages and reasonable defense costs caused by a third-party claim, and limited to a fixed sub-cap of $3,000,000.
- Preserve our right to cure: allow Redwood to obtain a license, modify the service, or otherwise remedy the alleged infringement before damages are assessed.
- Control of defense: require prompt notice and sole control of defense, but Acme may participate at its own counsel expense. We should require cooperation and access to evi...

messages.subject: RE: Acme - MSA redline (IP indemnity carve-out)
messages.body:
Team — quick note from Sales perspective. We need to keep the commercial ask simple for Acme: they want operational certainty. If Marissa's $3M sub-cap + defense costs gets us across the line, I recommend we propose a limited economic make-whole (e.g., 12 months of fees in escrow or a $50k holdback) and a defined SLA credit that applies only if an IP claim triggers service disruption.

Please also confirm: does the proposed carve-out cover claims against models/customers running customer-trained models on Redwood infra? We should explicitly exclude customer-provided content from Redwood liability and require Acme to warrant they have rights to content they load into the service.

If Legal...

messages.subject: Re: Acme - MSA redline (IP indemnity carve-out)
messages.body:
Rachel / team — thanks for the quick turnaround and the proposed compromise. Acme's counsel reviewed Marissa's suggested language. We can broadly accept a limited, quantified sub-cap for third-party IP claims if the language: (i) explicitly excludes IP indemnity from the general liability cap in Section X, (ii) covers reasonable defense costs, and (iii) does not require Acme to waive injunctive relief.

Two items for final redline: 
1) Carve-out scope: the indemnity must apply to claims based on Redwood's service or Redwood-supplied models, and expressly exclude claims arising solely from Customer Content or Customer modifications.
2) Cap amount: Acme will accept the $3,000,000 per-claim ...

- Option A (preferred if execs can still make it): keep the room reserved for 6/15; we’ll hold the agenda and have Marcus ready to present. If anyone confirms they can’t travel, we’ll add a remote dial-in for them.
- Option B (safe fallback): flip the 6/15 session to remote-only and run the same agenda over video (we have a concise exec demo + Q&A that fits 60–75 minutes).

We can cover any unavoidable hotel cancellation fees on our side if that helps procurement justify the change. Please confirm: do you want us to push the calendar invite as remote now, or shoul...

- If we go remote, I recommend we keep the original time so external vendors and FinWind’s exec calendar still lines up.
- If we need to reimburse hotel cancellation fees, send receipts to finance@redwood.ai and tag with DEAL-8931. I’ll coordinate with Marissa on the CS follow-up.

Got it, thanks for the materials. I'll own the coordination and route to Legal + Billing.

Quick notes / plan:

- Kimberly: please scan for any IP/data residency/indemnity items that need escalation.

We aim to return a short annotated list (no more than 8 high-priority items) by Tue 7/6 EOD. If we identify any material non-start...

messages.subject: RE: Uxify MSA + order form (first pass) — Legal quick-scan
messages.body:
Team —

Completed a 30-minute scan of Uxify_MSA_v1_redline.docx. I captured the top items that will drive negotiation time:

1) Data residency / Exhibit A: Uxify's Exhibit A is more prescriptive than our typical template. Legal needs to confirm whether Uxify's residency commitments require infrastructure-side language (we may need to add routing/region specifics).

2) IP assignment & license scope: Uxify has a broader background IP carve-in clause. Recommend we propose a narrow license back to the customer for generated outputs rather than full assignment.

3) Indemnity and liability cap: Their draft leaves our liability cap unaddressed for third-party data claims. Suggest explicit cap an...

messages.subject: Re: Uxify MSA + order form (first pass) — Signer + billing check
messages.body:
Emma/Rachel —

Quick confirmation from Commercial Ops:
- We can accept DocuSign for the initial order form and MSA unless Uxify requires notarized signatures. Please confirm.
- Billing contact: please confirm the AP contact and PO numbering expectations. If Uxify uses Net30 with purchase orders, we need to add invoice-to details to Exhibit A.

Emma: can you provide the name/email for the finance approver and whether they require the invoice to include a PO number? If yes, we'll map the PO field in the order form.

I'll hold off on embedding any redline language until Legal finalizes the 1-pager.

Thanks,
Jordan Blake
Commercial Operations — Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com

messages.subject: Quantaflow vendor registration & PO/payment authorization
messages.body:
Hi Laura —

Thanks again for the warm intro earlier. Per our conversation, attaching our AP onboarding packet and a current W-9 so your team can create a vendor record for "Redwood Inference" in Quantaflow's procurement system. A couple of items we need confirmation on from your side: 

- Preferred PO number format (we've seen both free-text and strict PO-line numbers). Do you require a line-item PO mapping for every invoice?
- Do you support split invoices for multi-entity billing? We sometimes need to split an invoice between US (Redwood Inc.) and EU entity for tax purposes.
- Banking: do you need an ACH mandate form on file or is bank account + remittance details sufficient?

Attachmen...

messages.subject: Re: Quantaflow vendor registration & PO/payment authorization
messages.body:
Hi Rachel —

Thanks for sending the packet. We can initiate vendor onboarding once we have:

1) Completed vendor registration in our portal (link below).
2) Tax documentation: W-9 for US entities; if you have an EU VAT certificate for your EU entity, please include it.
3) Bank remit + ACH mandate (we prefer the mandate for faster setup).

Our PO format: QF-YYYY-XXXXX (PO number on the invoice header and on every line item). We do allow split invoices but we need the invoice to indicate which line items map to which legal entity and a separate subtotal for each entity for tax.

I've attached a sample PO (QF_PO_Sample.pdf) and our vendor portal registration link: https://vendor.quantaflow.c...

messages.subject: Re: Quantaflow vendor registration & PO/payment authorization
messages.body:
Hi Laura —

Thanks, this is helpful. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Redwood will bill Quantaflow under one supplier record, but the invoice will include a tax-split section when required. For the EU split we would include the EU entity VAT ID on the line items that should be routed there — is that acceptable for your AP system?
- We'll complete the vendor portal registration today.
- I'll get the ACH mandate filled and back to you — is the DocuSign link the best way? (you can also accept a signed PDF via email if that's preferred).

Operational ask: Can you confirm whether Quantaflow requires PO acknowledgment or a three-way match (PO/Invoice/Receipt)? Some of our product-led bil...

messages.subject: Re: Quantaflow vendor registration & PO/payment authorization
messages.body:
Rachel / Marissa —

Quick note from finance: if they require the ACH mandate we should plan to return it signed by Redwood Inc. (US bank). For EU split we will provide a separate invoice from Redwood EU Ops (we have the VAT cert on file). I'll prep a one-page runbook for the AP team describing how our invoices indicate PO mapping and entity split.

I can take the lead on the runbook and the ACH/mandate follow-up today.

-Jordan
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

messages.subject: Re: Quantaflow vendor registration & PO/payment authorization
messages.body:
Hi Rachel / Jordan —

That sounds fine. A few specifics to make vendor activation smoother: 

- Please register at https://vendor.quantaflow.com/register/abc123 and include Redwood's legal name exactly as it appears on the W-9.
- Upload the ACH mandate (Quantaflow_ACH_Mandate.pdf) signed by an authorized signer. We accept DocuSign or a scanned signed PDF.
- For split invoices: include two subtotals and a short note in the invoice body identifying which line items are for Quantaflow EU vs Quantaflow US. Our AP system will create separate GL postings based on that mapping.
- PO acknowledgement: Not mandatory, but we do a PO+invoice match by default. Three-way match required only for hardwar...

messages.subject: Re: Quantaflow vendor registration & PO/payment authorization
messages.body:
Great — thanks, Laura. We'll: 

1) Complete the Quantaflow portal registration today (Rachel)
2) Return the signed ACH mandate via DocuSign (Jordan will sign)
3) Upload W-9 + Redwood EU VAT cert (Marissa will attach the VAT cert)
4) Jordan to send the one-page billing runbook to procurements@quantaflow.com by EOD

Proposed calendar slot if you want to review the onboarding packet together: Thursday 18 Mar 2026, 11:00am PT — https://cal.redwood.com/rachel/30min (or tell me a time that works).

Thanks again — looking forward to getting this live.

Rachel Kim
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

(Attachments to follow: Redwood_W9.pdf, RI_AP_Onboarding_Packet.pdf)
